description:
Papyros is an alternative for Scrabble. Consonants in different combination are on the board.  Every player has to combine them with vowels. Furthermore there are 3 types of cards which allow the players to change consonants, build alliances with other players or to flip the consonant-markers on the other side.
You get Egyptian coins for every letter of the word you complete. The player who gets 49 Egyptian coins first wins the game.

Realm of Wizards is a game for 2-4 players. Each wizard is assigned an element, earth (green), air (yellow), fire (red) and water (blue). The wizards summon creatures to fight to the death. The winner is the last player left in the game.

A board section is supplied for each wizard that is partaking in the game and places it at the start of the game.

Each turn the wizards collect energy (magic crystals) from their current space, if it happens to be a space matching their own element type they will gain more crystals, next the wizards can move and select up to two creatures they may have summoned to move, landing on a strategic area of the board can make a big difference between success and failure. Once movement has been performed it’s time for any creatures of opposing wizards sharing a space to battle to the death, during the battle phase a creature can battle a wizard and if they win that wizard is eliminated and their part in the game is over. Once all the battles have been fought the wizards can summon a new creature to aid them in the conquest, if they happen to share a space matching their own element type they can summon two. Lastly new spells (creature counters) are collected and replenished, some are expired and returned to the counter pool ready for the next turn when each wizard left in the game must gather their thoughts and put their minds to their next strategy and prepare for the actions from the remaining wizards left in the game.

Each turn is broken down into steps which each wizard will perform before the next step begins. By placing their wizard and creatures in strategic positions the players can gain an important edge over their opponent that could be the difference between success and failure. A wizard in control of the elements will ultimately bring about the destruction of his enemy and rejoice in victory.

Die Jahreszeiten (The Seasons) is a small expansion for Theomachie. This expansion was available only for supporters of the game on German crowdfunding platform Spieleschmiede.

With this expansion the seasons come into play. Each season is associated with an elemental sphere.

There are two ways of using this expansion:
Variant 1: Each season increases the power of the cards from the corresponding sphere and gives access to an additional gift.
Variant 2: During the prayer phase, cards of the corresponding sphere can be taken for free from the display.

Hephaistos is a small expansion for Theomachie. The expansion was available only for supporters of the game on German crowdfunding platform Spieleschmiede.

Hephaistos is an additional card from the Greek Mythology and has his own abilities. These abilities are unique, as well as the abilities of the other Gods. So a player controlling the Greek Mythology may choose one out of 4 Gods, instead of 1 out of 3.

The Spieleschmiede Erweiterung is a small expansion for Theomachie. This expansion was available only for supporters of the game on German crowdfunding platform Spieleschmiede.

The expansion contains 8 additional prayer cards (4 different cards). These prayer cards have symbols of two different spheres, instead of one. During gameplay these cards are handled as if they originated from both spheres.

War Fighters is a unique, 2 player (3 player if you want to), traditional card game that incorporates aspects from 4 different card games, in a lightning-fast paced game. Players have 5 different game play styles to choose from and can play with different amounts of cards, in a half deck, full deck, or custom deck format. This is an “E-Rated” card game, sure to get your family involved, and easy enough for anyone to enjoy.

There are 10 decks to choose from: Ninja, Pirates, Aliens, Robots, Monsters, Undead, Ghosts, Vampires, Werewolves, and Zombies.

The game is designed to bridge the gap between games for really young children such as: "Uno," "Crazy 8's," and "Old Maid,"  and games for older children such as:  "Magic the Gathering," "Pokemon," etc.  It is designed to be an easy to pick up and play card game for children ages 6+, families, and casual players.  There are multiple ways to play the game, multiple decks to build, and players can even make up rules once they get comfortable with the gameplay. The trading card and custom deck options are just that...options, and are not required to play the game, unless you want to, and that is always welcomed!

War Fighters can be played by anyone, of any age, at any time, in multiple ways, without having to spend hours on rules getting confusing.

Basic Game Play (#1):
-Divide one deck in half evenly and give each half to a player.
-Each player will receive:  5 square fighters (Paper), 5 circle fighters (Rock), 5 ex fighters (Scissors), 4 attacks, 2 blocks, 2 truces, and 2 star leader fighter cards for a total of 25 cards in the basic version of the game.
-Each player shuffles their half decks
-Each player puts their decks to the left as their "draw pile."
-Each player draws 5 cards.
-At the same time, each player plays a fighter card in the play area between them.  A fighter card will have a symbol of a circle, square, ex, or star on it.
-To determine who wins, Square beats Circle, Circle beats Ex, Ex beats Square, Star beats all.
-If both players play the same cards (ex:  circle vs. circle) then "war" is initiated and each player draws 3 cards from the draw pile and places it face down into the play area, and then plays another fighter card for the win. War continues until someone wins.  Each time the same cards are played, war is initiated again.
-The winner of each round gets all the cards in the play area and puts them into their "discard pile" to their right.
-When the cards in the play area are the same, players must draw the 3 cards and lay them face down followed by another fighter card.  However, when the cards are different (ex:  circle vs. square) a player can then decide if they want to use an "attack" card, "block" card, or a "truce" card.
-An "attack" card is basically a "+1" or "one up" to the other player for the win.
-A "truce" card means you do not want to fight anymore and want your card back, so both players get their cards back.
-A player can choose to "block" an "attack" card as a defense to try and win the round.  A "truce" card cannot be blocked in the basic version of the game.
-After each round, each player draws back up to 5 cards for the next round.
-Each player can use their full hand in each round as needed, but can only use one fighter card during a round, unless  "war" has been initiated and the three face down cards need to be drawn.
-It takes one attack and one circle to beat a square, one attack and one square to beat an ex, and one attack and an ex to beat a  circle card.
-If a star card is thrown, it takes two unblocked attacks plus any fighter card (square, circle, ex) to beat the star card
-The objective of the game, or how to win, is to collect all the leader cards from the other player.
-On a 5 card hand, if a player does not have any fighter cards, they must show the other player their hand to prove they are not cheating, hand over one of their cards, shuffle all their cards, and draw a new 5 card hand.
-During war, if a player does not have a fighter card to finish the round, that player must count the number of cards in their hand, shuffle all their cards, and draw another hand with that number of cards.

Game Play #2 - (War Strategies):
-During this game style, players can choose to use attack, defense, and truce cards in order raise the stakes before going to war and/or to cancel out the war.  So, when players use the same cards (Ex:  circle vs. circle), the attack, block, and truce cards can now be played.

Game Play #3 - (Aggressive Vs. Defensive):
-In this style of play, one person gets all the attack cards to start with, while the other gets all the blocks and truce cards.
-As an additional rule, one of the players can get the "power" card as well.
-The "power" card acts as 2 attack cards.  When used as an attack card, if a player wins the round, the other player's fighter card is taken out of the game until it is over, making the game go even faster.  Star Leader cards cannot be taken out of the game since they are needed in order to win the game.

Game Play #4 - (Power Cards):
-In this mode, the loser of the previous round gets to use the power card to help "even the odds."
-One power card comes in each box.

Game Play #5 - (Custom Deck Options):
-In this option, players can play half decks, full decks, or customs decks, and build their own decks to battle with.
-Players can choose if the cards are deck specific as well.  For example, if one person uses ninjas and the other uses pirates, the you can assume that pirates do not know how to throw ninja attack darts or stricken, and therefore the cards are no good to pirates and only the ninja cards the player acquires can use those attacks.  This option is a bit advanced so most players who play this tend to put the other players fighter cards to the side and use the attacks, blocks, truce, and power cards in a non-deck specific mode.

Extra Game Information:
-This game is very open so players who are comfortable with the rules tend to master it and then make up their own rules for added fun.
-All decks play the same, but have different artwork to cater to everyones passions.
-This game can also be used to play "matching games," where you lay cards face down and try to pair them up.

This expansion module includes the American plastic military pieces not included in the base game. These expansions add different components that do not come in the base game. The stats/rules of these components are different than the ones that come in the base game. The rules/stats for the expansion components come in the base game.

This expansion module includes the Japanese plastic military pieces not included in the base game. These expansions add different components that do not come in the base game. The stats/rules of these components are different than the ones that come in the base game. The rules/stats for the expansion components come in the base game.

description:
Leave no Man Behind!

Earth is facing its most desperate hour. The Union Armed Forces, well trained and equipped with the best weapons technology can offer, have mobilized to defend their world from the sinister Neo-Soviet empire and all Maelstrom-borne threats. Orbital strikes, high-powered laser cannons and specialized missiles allow the Union to dominate a battle from a distance as their special forces squads, drones, and power-armored troops close with the enemy to deliver the decisive blow!

The Union Forcebook describes the fierce and determined Union Armed Forces, providing background on the formation of the Union and describing its unparalleled military organization and weaponry. The Union Force List offers new units, drones and the Ares assault suits. This book also contains a North America Battle Terrain Table, rules for orbital strikes and deployment, and new campaign rules, including force traits.

Eat! Breed! Kill!

Like a primal force of nature, the savage alien race known as the Growlers smashes their way across the Maelstrom, voraciously eating all opposition. Practically unstoppable, the unique packmind telepathy and hyper-adaptive physiology of the Growlers allow them to conquer even the most extreme environments. There's only one defense against a Growler's ferocious, unpredictable attack-another Growler!

The Growler Forcebook is the ultimate source of information on this mighty race, including details on the Growler life cycle, culture, breeds, colonies and their homeworld of Primordia. A complete Growler force list covers the four main breeds (Razorbacks, Bluefangs, Whitetusks, and Fireguts) and new abilities. This book includes a Primordia Battle Terrain Table, rules for mixed-breed and custom-breed forces, and Growler-specific campaign rules that show how to grow a Growler pup into a terrifying chieftain.

For Mother Russsia!

A dark and twisted phoenix has risen in the East, staking claim to the Earth and beyond. Surging forth from their irradiated homeland, the nightmarish mutant hordes of the Neo-Soviet Empire methodically sweep across the globe, satisfied by nothing less than world domination. Their mastery of the dark arts of genetic manipulation and bio-hazardous weaponry may be the edge they need to succeed in their megalomaniacal quest!

The Neo-Soviet Forcebook describes the deadly and diabolical forces of the Neo-Soviet Empire, providing background on this massive military and their ethically questionable arsenal. The Neo-Soviet Force List offers new units, mutants and the Ursa heavy assault suits. This book also contains an Asia Battle Terrain Table, rules for creating your own mutants and new campaign rules, including force traits.

The Flame Burns Eternal.

Masters of melee and mystical combat, the zykhee flow like water across the battlefield, slicing through and slashing down all who threaten to extinguish the sacred flame of the Bael Fire. Thousands of years of tradition have sculpted the current crop of zykhee warriors into the deadliest practitioners of hand-to-hand combat the Maelstrom has ever known.

The Zykhee Forcebook describes the disciplined and wickedly agile warriors of the zykhee, providing detailed background on this alien race. The Zykhee Forcelist offers new units including wardyns, Death Dancers, Soulless, and Stalkers. This book also contains a Vykas-Denox Battle Terrain Table, new campaign rules, new force traits, and five unique personalities to aid they zykhee in their quest to protect the sacred flame.

All Carbons Must Die!

Eternally vengeful, the Shard scour the Maelstrom with only one objective-the utter destruction of all carbon based life forms. Pure hatred fuels their relentless war and their jagged crystalline husks are the only weaponry they need to wreak their havoc. By channeling their cores of pure energy, the Shard unleash piercing lasers, disruptive force fields and epic blasts from the dreaded matrix cannon to lay waste to any and all who are unfortunate enough to be caught in their whirlwind of rage.

The Shard Forcebook describes the hostile and hellbent forces of the Shard, providing background on the tragic history of this advanced race and how imprisonment within the Maelstrom has forced them to "devolve." The Shard forcelist offers new units such as concussors, ambients, primes, the god-like axions, and the devastating eradicator war machine. This book also contains a Shard Battle Terrain Table, new campaign rules, force traits and five new Shard personalities who are especially efficient at reducing carbons to dust.

Praise the Vorack!

The ancient, undead pharon race dominates the Maelstrom from its homeworld deep in the Inner Ring. Armed with the untold secrets of the Maelstrom, ultra-advanced weaponry and dark magic, the pharon legions wage war on the living and feed on their fallen enemies to sustain their long-dead bodies.

The Pharon Forcebook provides background on the mysterious origins of the cursed and demented pharon race and presents new pharon units such as slaves, Fists of Anubis, slayers, nobles, harvesters, and soul reavers. This book also contains a Pharon Battle Terrain table, new campaign rules and force traits, and five new pharon personalities that will terrorize your foes!

It's Time to Fight!

Earth is playing host to a legendary showdown. The Zykhee Wardyn Myntokk has caught up with his sworn enemy, the notorious Growler Alpha Chieftain Razorfang. With the safety of Earth at stake, the Union and Neo-Soviet forces are on the hunt as well. Will Trowlers continue to flourish on Earth? Will Myntokk succeed in slaying Razorfang? These questions can only be answered by you! Now it's in your hands...

Razorfang's Exodus is a series of special scenarios chronicling the Growler presence on Earth. When you return the enclosed VOR Battle Card, the result of your campaign will directly affect VOR's future storyline. The book offers new rules and characters for the Growlers, Union, Neo-Soviet and Zykhee, and provides race-specific campaign record sheets.

A casual to intermediate resource-management (and engine-building) game.

Players farm hemp and grain, while taking actions to construct buildings and exchange goods for advancement and to prepare for the end game. As players construct buildings, new buildings become available from a semi-random deck. Act 1 buildings cost wood and energy, but provide resources including stone and wood. Act 2 buildings cost Stone and energy, and provide better resources like brick and bread. Act 3 buildings are costly, but provide victory points. The final round is triggered when there are only three remaining buildings that can be constructed. Buildings, stone and brick are worth victory points, the player with the most points wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Polder is a game inspired by this same Dutch word, which describes the terrain gained from the sea since XII century. To do so, they pumped the water out with windmills.

The goal of each player is to be the first one to form a continuous canal that connects the windmill with the polder or center of the board.

Pieces shall be grouped by size out of the board. Next, each player chooses a windmill from the board and takes either one of these actions: place a piece on its ring, turn around a whole ring -just a quarter-, change a piece of the board for a new one.

In case of a tie, the player with more small windmills in the canal to the center wins.

New York didn't collapse. Destroyed by atomic bombs, transformed into a fortress among the ruins, it still fights in the defense of the ideals of Free America. So far it has become a capital of the small state of the same name and has start a crusade to restore the human world. At the same time it has usurped the role of guiding the nation and removed its opponents wherever it can. Again and again, the new troops set off on expeditions to the wilderness in search of new resources and prewar technologies, recruiting useful people along the way. The city itself has become a fortified police state, one of the few safe places in the ruined world, where you pay for peace with your obedience.

Neuroshima Hex! New York is a single army expansion for Neuroshima Hex! The advantage of this army is the diversity of its units, including a lot of shooting units. A great strength is the Headquarters, which gives additional endurance points, and the presence of spies, which can use enemy modules.

The army's disadvantage is a smaller possibility of creating strong offensive coalitions, and the fact that the HQ's special ability is rather defensive.

Neuroshima Hex! New York was originally released as part of the two-army expansion Neuroshima Hex! Babel13.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
All Hands! ... Nim variant for 2-4 players played with 16 pawns, game board and 'lifeboat'.

Ducosim, the Dutch game association, publishes the game magazine Spel! with 'Spel! enkaarten' (game cards) i.e. games which fit on A5 cards.

All Hands! is Card #0011 and was included with Spel! on 16th March 2013.

The object is, not to be the last tar left on deck as, you will go down with the ship!

Sixteen salty sailors (pawns) are lined up on deck (on the dots on the board) and, in turn, players remove adjacent pawns and place them safe at sea in the lifeboat.

The player to remove the last pawn loses.

The Dutch game association Ducosim publishes the game magazine Spel!. With this magazine they will publish several 'Spel!enkaarten' (gamecards). These are games which fit on a A5 card. #0009 is BattleStack and was published for the SpellenSpektakel convention.

The game is a diceless variant of Katoiz. Just as in that game, the board consists of a 6x6 board build up with tiles. These tiles have 3 characteristics: form (square, triangle or circle), color (blue, red, green, yellow or white) and size (big or small). Next to that, each player puts a home tile next to one of the corners in his own color. To move the tiles around you can move a tile horizontally of vertically if 1 characteristic with the adjacent tile corresponds. To move diagonally, 2 characteristics have to correspond. Different to Katoiz, instead of rolling dice to determine which tile you can move on your turn, here you move tiles in a fixed order by color.

The aim of the game is to collect a stack of four tiles on your home tile.

"The Bitcoin Harvest" is a paper-and-pencil game for 2 or more players that was published on issue 16 of Bitcoin Magazine. It is a derivative of Hippos & Crocodiles by the same designer.

It uses a type of puzzle piece that has been around for more than a century: the pentomino, and also the concept of harvesting (picking items already placed on the board).

RULES

Start by drawing a rectangular grid of any size. 10x10 is recommended for beginners, but for more than 2 players you might need a bigger grid.

Secondly, and by turn, each player chooses a different pentomino shape. This pentomino will be his ‘harvester’, and the only piece he will use during the game.

The player that chose a shape in first place draws two coins in any empty cells of the grid.

Then, starting with the second player, players take turns doing the following:

1. Draw your harvester on the board, so it doesn’t overlap other previously drawn pentominos and no parts of it land outside the board. You can (and should) draw it so it overlaps some coins. Notice that you can draw it rotated or symmetrized, as long as it fits with the grid.
2. Score as many coins as the pentomino has covered
3. Draw another 2 coins in any two empty spaces (not occupied by coins or pentominoes).
4. Pass your turn to the next player.

If you’re not capable of legally drawing a pentomino (no room for it) you must pass your turn. If all players pass in succesion the game ends. The player with the highest score wins.

As a variant, before the game starts players can agree on the number of coins drawn each turn.

Doomsday Machine is an automated, deadly trap - an intelligent security system protecting a secret base hidden in the radioactive wastelands. Its algorithm commands it to destroy any intruder, whether it is a man, mutant or a Moloch robot. What could be hidden behind such a powerful defense system?

Doomsday Machine 3.0 is a reimplementation of an extra army, which was given away as a freebie at Spiel 2007. Essential skill of that army (which contains only shooting units) is the ability to redirect shots and rotating units (once per turn).

It was included as the fifth army in the English Z-Man edition of Neuroshima Hex! 3.0.

Doomsday Machine is available for purchase directly from Portal Games.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Somewhere in Essentia, a singular region called Alerya is located. Aside from possessing four more terrains different from the already known (lake, tundra, jungle and desert), it has a peculiarity: the essentia magic has gotten weaker there, so the supplied powers are limited. However, it is the place chosen by the two Orders to keep safe their ancient relics.

Dawn and Twilight are up to another battle. A brave wizard and a ruthless sorcerer will try to guide wisely their golems to take the relics from their enemy.

Alerya is an Essentia derivative by the same designer, that uses a different board, new terrain tiles and magic relics.

A player wins when she manages to steal the two relics of her enemy, or possess more enemy relics than the other player when all the golems of an Order (hers or her opponent’s) have been eliminated. If there is a draw, the player with remaining golems on the board wins the game.

Take to the Skies!

Fly a patrol over the trenches and tangle with enemy planes in this set collection card game!

Climb and dive as you jockey for position, attempting to line up the perfect shot. Choose from over 20 different historic aircraft, each with different performance attributes which suffer as the plane takes damage. You may wind up an ace, or go down in flames!


            
        
    


A World War I aerial combat set collection card game. In Dogfight players strive to collect then play sets of cards in order to shoot down their opponents. The game is over when only one side remains, or when the victory conditions of the scenario being played are met.

The game features set collection, card drafting, and variable player powers.

Dogfight contains 54 cards used to form the draw deck during the game. Each card is from one of six Attack Sets, as noted by the title across the top of the card: Crossing Shot, Diving Shot, Head On Shot, Strafing Run, Trailing Shot, and Turning Shot.

Attack Sets may have special conditions governing when the set can be played. Before trying to damage an enemy plane, you must collect a number of cards from the same Attack Set equal to the card’s Set Size. These cards are then played together as a Bid for Damage. If the attack is successful, then the target plane suffers damage equal to the Damage Value listed on the cards.

Some cards in the deck have a Defensive Value and can be used to avoid an enemy attack.

The draw deck is also used for randomly assigning specific types of damage to target planes. For each point of damage inflicted, one card is flipped over from the draw deck and the listed damage is applied. The information along the bottom of the card is used solely for this purpose, and is ignored during the set collection portions of the game.

An educational mathematics card game from Finland. Each player is dealt seven cards face-down from a deck of 50 (numbered 1-15, and three jokers). One card is dealt on the table face-up and the dealer calls "MIX". Players now simultaneously pick up their cards and try to create an equation using exactly three of them with addition, subtraction, or multiplication that will result in the number on the face-up card. For example, if the face-up card shows 11, then (2 x 3) + 5 would do. Fastest player to create a valid equation calls "FIX" and wins a point; play until 21 points to find out who's the winner of the whole game.

Box back claims three play variants, but the rules actually have four: Solo game (practising math), Team game (team gets a point if any team member is the fastest), Joker game (jokers are included in the deck, can be any number) or Memory game (cards are simply used to play the traditional flip-a-card-at-a-time memory game).

boardgame: Cat Herders: The Cat Herding Game of Herding Cats
description:
Nine cats have escaped into the park. They’re grumpy and would prefer you leave them alone. Nonetheless, you employ a team of animal control agents and your job is to round up the cats. You win by nabbing five of them.

Cat Herders is a luckless, 2-player combinatorial game, designed to be an elegant simulation (an evocative simulation using a minimum of rules).

The game is played on a hex grid. The central dilemma is that, while your goal is to capture cats by moving your herders to next to them, when you move a herder next to a cat, the cat runs away. The only way to capture a cat is approach it at just the right angle and surround it, before your opponent does. It's tricky.

A good analogy for the feel of play: it's a bit like a multi-player version of chat noir.

Rules, design background, and an example game can be found here.

Trivia game about sports, athletics and Olympics.

Roll the custom color dice and choose one of the two color deck to draw a card. Some decks need knowledge, some need co-operation with other players, some need creativity.
Ansqer the question or solve the puzzle and advance with your game-piece.
The player who finishes first is the winner.

"Saint-Côme-du-Mont is a collection of X-Terrain suitable to add a small stone church to your games. The church steeple is an excellent place for look outs and snipers. And the stone construction of both the church and parsonage will make it necessary to go in and clean the enemy out."

This expansion expands play with three typical country buildings - a small stone church, a single story parsonage, and a open crypt with shed. Each building has special sighting rules and provide excellent cover. Additional scenarios are included.

CONTENTS:

X3A Saint-Côme-du-Mont - a stone church with steeple 
X3B St. Comé le Presbytrère - a one story stone parsonage 
X3C La Crypte de St. Comé - a church graveyard and crypt 
10 Scenarios 
3 sets of 3 Story cards 
7 Order cards

boardgame: Sergeants Miniatures Game: Operation Tonga Expansion
description:
Requires Red Devils Starter Set.

"Sergeants Miniatures Game: Operation Tonga™" expands the map and scenarios for SMG: Red Devils™ allowing up to 4 squads to engage in a single battle.

The 18 Scenarios in Operation Tonga covers the night of June 6, 1944 when the British 6th Airborne Division parachuted into Normandy between the Orne estuary and the Dives River and marshes. The region was defended by elements of the 711th and 716th Infantry Divisions and the 125th Panzergrenadier Regiment of the 21th Panzer Division. The overall objective of 6th Airborne was to control or destroy lines of communications and to secure the eastern flank of the invasion. They were there to keep German artillery away from the supply lines coming across Sword Beachhead.

Compatible with both Red Devils and Pegasus Bridge.

Modules require ownership of base games, Day of Days for German and American modules, or Red Devils for German and British modules.

The Tactics Modules are a new addition to the SMG line. These modules includes Team Panels in which you match up qualifying soldiers to make cohesive Teams that get added abilities and equipment.

Included are 16 Team cards that may include Command, Teamwork, React and all new Equipment cards.

Current Modules exist for:
- German Light Infantry 
- US Paratrooper 
- British Airborne

Contents:
4 to 5  - Team Panels 
7  - Smoke and Fire Markers 
16 - Battle Patrol Cards 
1  - Rules for Equipment and Tactics

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – The Champion's Purse
description:
"Life is not a song, sweetling. Someday you may learn that, to your sorrow." –George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

The Champion's Purse, the second chapter pack of the "Conquest and Defiance" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game, offers players new themes and strategies to explore. As the Great Houses of Westeros clash over the Iron Throne, players must continually evaluate whether the reward of bringing powerful new cards into play is worth the risk of losing those cards to the enemy. Whichever House you lead, you'll find iconic characters, like Viserys Targaryen and Wex Pyke, leading you closer to The Champion's Purse.

The Champion's Purse includes three copies each of twenty new cards and introduces the "prized" keyword, which balances tremendous risks and rewards in deck-building and play.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
One of the wargames made by Tsukuda Hobby.

The game covers the hypothetical situations in 1990 that was near future in those days.
The sea lane deffence was loudly discussed in those days in Japan.  Prime minister Nakasone made an announcement of unsinkable carrier for Japanese islands.
The hypothetical enemy is Soviet union which was broken into pieces soon after the Nakasone's announcement.

The map covers Sakhalin to Taiwang.  But the combatsystem is quite tactical.  Unfortunately there are only 3 scenarios.  And 2 of them are just drills between US force and Japanese self-defence force.

Very rare game even in Japan.

Gaffes & Promises is a card drafting game featuring events from the 2013 Australian Federal Election. Players represent political parties trying to garner votes using promises and subjecting their opponents to gaffes.

Over three rounds, players draft cards with varying support for four demographics: Families, Students, Pensioners and Big Business. Three demographic cards are in play per round, and the player with the most support in each at the end of a round wins that card. The player with the most demographic cards at the end of the game is the winner.

In this co-op children's game players must find the missing grandmother whose birthday party it is. They also have to find necessary party supplies before time runs out.

The game is fairly standard roll-and-move, with advanced rules that introduce variable player powers.

description:
Steam: Map Expansion #4 brings Steam players to two new parts of the world. In South Africa, 3-6 players will need to focus on mining as much as they do markets. The Poland-based expansion promises "Broad to Standard Gage Transitions" and includes two sets of rules: one for 3-4 players and another for 2 players.

Steam: Map Expansion #4 also includes a new tile sheet for use with these game boards as well as additional tiles for Steam: Map Expansion #3.

GAME SYSTEM

    
    
         This entry is to allow for discussion/rating of the game system as a whole. It is not for a specific product or release. Versions will appear on the individual item pages.

    


Future Card Buddyfight is a Japanese collectible card game from the same creators as CardFight!! Vanguard and Weiß Schwarz. It was released in January 2014 alongside an anime series by the same name. A manga adaptation began circulation in November 2013.

In it, players take turns playing monsters and other cards in an attempt to reduce the opponents life points to 0. Monsters are played in one of three zones—center, right, left. If the center space is empty, that player is vulnerable to attack.

There are five card types: Flags, Items, Monsters, Impacts, and Spells. A player’s deck consists of 52 cards: 1 Buddy (a monster card), 1 Flag—both of which start the game in play—and a main deck of 50 cards which cannot contain more than four copies of any given card.

1 booster box contains 30 booster packs. Each pack contains 5 random cards. The rarities of cards are categorized into Common (C), Uncommon (UC), Rare (R), Double Rare (RR), Triple Rare (RRR), Buddy Rare (BR) and Special Parallel (SP).

Options was a game that 3M apparently planned to publish in their bookcase game series, but it never appeared. At least one prototype exists.

The game is played on a 12x14 grid. Each player has an identical set of number tiles (two each of 0-9) plus two "proxy" tiles and two blocking tiles. The board is seeded with the proxy and blocking tiles and then players take turns playing a tile or replacing a proxy with a matching regular tile and placing the proxy somewhere else.

When a tile is played it must be next to or one space away from an "object" tile, and must either be one lower or higher than that tile, or equal to it if the play is on one of the starred spaces in the center.

Points are scored for getting scoring tiles (those numbered 1, 3, or 7) in scoring rows; a scoring row is an orthogonal row of three or four of a player's tiles, but if there are three they must be in sequence (with wraparounds allowed). Points are also scored for creating situations in which the opposing player cannot play.

Operational level wargame for battle of Gettysburg in American Civil War. This game published first in external supplement for Command Magazine Japan edition #113, and later updated and web-published.

The players control either Union or Confederation.
In each turns, players repeat command allocation, initiative check and movement until time advance occurs in the initiative check. 
The battle is assigned in the movement, but all battles resolved in the end of the turn.

The winner determined by the victory points based on occupation of the cities by Confederation.

English and Korean rules available.

description:
Rocky Crown is a boardgame for 2-4 players in which dwarven clans build and fight over a newly established dwarven city. They must also explore the tunnels below the grand halls, hoping to find riches and treasures that will place them on the throne.

The game takes up to eleven turns or until the dwellers of the underground overrun the city. The winner, should the dwarves survive, is the clan holding the most prestige points.

Every turn players must bid to take up different positions which are entrusted with the diverse aspects of the daily life of the city. The clans must lead expeditions into the dark, assume responsability over the great works taking place and supervise the defence of the city both above and below ground. In doing so, they'll gain prestige that'll take them closer to their goal of becoming king of the new city

The Fast and Fun Game of Phrases, Sayings and More! Every round is a verbal free-for-all in this extremely fast-paced family game. Your goal? Find the missing word in a common phrase, saying, or expression before anyone else! You’re given the first letter of the missing word to help. So if the Presenter says “(B) Up The Wrong Tree”, shout BARKING. If they say “Timing Is (E)”, yell EVERYTHING. If you hear “When Pigs (F)”, holler FLY. It’s that easy.

The Presenter starts things off, then it’s up to how quickly you react. It isn't good enough to be fast: you have to be the fastest! So whatever happens, DON'T SHOOT THE MESSENGER...

CAN'T FOOL ME! you say? We'll see... If you like to think and have fun at the same time, this is for you. It’s fast, challenging and tricky, so prepare to pit your wits against others in this highly entertaining brain-teasing game. You're given a Keyword & Clue and have to find the Answer.  All set? If the Keyword is "Sun", and your Clue says "Reach for a spoon, not your sunglasses", be the quickest to shout out SUNDAE. If the Keyword is "Dragon", and the Clue states "No knight ever tried to slay one", yell out DRAGONFLY. Or try this: Your Keyword is "Comfort" and the Clue is "Money can't buy you love, but it can buy you this". Did you guess the Answer: COMFORTER? So fooled or not, get ready to scratch your head, shout and laugh a lot!

Includes: 320 Question Rounds on 160 Dual-Sided Game Cards, Gameboard, Game Pieces, Instructions

Line Up The Gemstones To Win! Loose Ends is an entertaining game of constant surprises, where good old-fashioned fun meets strategy and luck. Just roll the Pyramid Die and start taking the Gemstones out of the bag. But where should you place them on the Grid? Line them up in color patterns to form Scoring Combinations and win points. But fall short and your opponents can take advantage. So think carefully before you act: everyone’s fate is intertwined. It’s easy to learn and always intriguing, with enough twists and turns to keep you on your toes!

Includes: Large Gameboard, Glass Stones, Gemstone Bag, Pyramid Die, Scoring Chips, Instructions

Will you guess the answers in time, or is that just WISHFUL THINKING™? Let's see how quickly you make the connections in this fun and challenging word guessing game... You have 60 seconds to figure out the three Mystery Words on the card, which all begin with the same letter. Ready?

The Presenter says "Think of words that start with J" and then gives the first Hint: "Voyage". The answer? JOURNEY. If the next Hint is "Shock", say "JOLT". If the final Hint is "Extra-large", say "JUMBO". The more you solve, the more spaces you move on the board. There’s a twist: Opponents bet which Mystery Word you’re least likely to guess - so make them regret it! This quick-thinking and fast-paced game is great fun for everybody.

The fun of playing cards with all the excitement of a board game! Up 4 Grabs has  plenty of twists and turns with every flip of a card. Collect the "aces" and "faces" (face cards) by landing on squares around the board, challenging an opponent to a "Duel", and many other ways. It’s fast-paced and constantly changing: you’ll find yourself rapidly gaining the cards you need, and then losing them just as quickly. Plan your strategy and decide what combination to go after. But keep track of what your opponents are doing and thwart them when you can. Collect the four cards you need and win!

Argent: Mancers of the University is an expansion to Argent: The Consortium featuring a new candidate, new rooms, and a bunch of new spells, treasures, apprentices, and supporters.

Technomancy is the school of magic that deals with observable, repeatable phenomenon. Technomancers themselves don't use magic, but they build devices that contain, process, and manipulate the natural magical energy of the world. The Department of Technomancy encompasses three schools:

Alchemy - Mystical biology and medicine, the creation of homunculi, and the transmutation of base elements.
Mechanics - The creation of fantastic machines that defy the laws of physics by magical means.
Artifice - The binding of enchantments to magical gear, creating items that have powerful mystical properties.

description:
Ultimate Werewolf is an interactive game of deduction for two teams: Villagers and Werewolves. The Villagers don't know who the Werewolves are, and the Werewolves are trying to remain undiscovered while they slowly eliminate the Villagers one at a time. A Moderator (who isn't on a team) runs the game.

Ultimate Werewolf takes place over a series of game days and nights. Each day, the players discuss who among them is a Werewolf and vote out a player. Each night, the Werewolves choose a player to eliminate, while the Seer learns whether one player is a Werewolf or not. The game is over when either all the Villagers or all the Werewolves are eliminated.

Ultimate Werewolf: Deluxe Edition features all new artwork, a great new design, totally rewritten and more comprehensive rules, and an even better moderator scorepad. What's more, it supports more players than ever: 75 of your closest friends can converge on one or more villages using the components in this box.

description:
Who are the most famous werewolves in the world? Better yet, how about the most famous *wolves* in the world? Ultimate Werewolf: Wolfpack has them all and in ways you'd never expect.

This expansion for Ultimate Werewolf has six new roles for your werewolfing fun. Think werewolves are too meek? That's because your villages have never had The Big Bad Wolf, who leads the werewolves to eliminate villagers twice as fast while he's in the game. On the opposite end of the spectrum is the terrifyingly-confused Fruit Brute, who is, of all things, a vegetarian. Elizabeth Taylor was pretty scary as Martha the crazy socialite, but every game you'll want to know who in your group is actually afraid of Virginia Woolf. And there's three more famous wolves, including one that totally coincidentally shares a name with one of the X-Men.

Ultimate Werewolf: Wolfpack is compatible with Ultimate Werewolf and Ultimate Werewolf Deluxe Edition

Disintegrate your friends!

Ray Gun Roulette is a high tech, high stakes game of bluffing for 2 to 10 players. Players are participating in this deadly game to acquire as many crystals as they can. Choose from an assortment of highly illegal firearms and blast away until you're either the last player alive or you possess all the crystals!

Each player starts with two primary shields (blue gems) and three crystals (the currency of the game). If a player runs out of crystals they have lost the game. A primary shield can absorb one blast from one of these lethal firearms, if a player gets hit with just one of these weapons unshielded, they will get disintegrated, and lose the game. The weapon cards contain all the information needed to use them.

Each weapon, in addition to the illustration and weapon name, have a number of features,
-Kill-o-watt rating: the power rating of the weapon, if both players are targeting each other, the higher kill-o-watt weapon wins and damages their target.
-Deflector shield rating: some weapons have a built-in deflector shield. It can deflect a shot coming from a player that you are not targeting this round. If the kill-o-watt rating is less than or equal to the deflector shield rating (remember to multiply the deflector rating by 1,000 before comparing) the shot has no effect. Otherwise the shot goes through and can be absorbed by a primary shield or disintegrate the player.
-Special ability: each weapon has a unique special ability. Some of these can be particularly game-changing and devastating. Any player may negate the special ability of a weapon at the cost of one crystal (thrown into the pot for the current round), once per round, immediately after the effect would have taken place.

A round of Ray Gun Roulette has a number of phases,
-Choose weapons, dealer deals each player 3 cards, they choose one and discard the rest.
-Ante up!, each player places a crystal in the pot.
-Aim, players pick a target, making a "gun" hand pointing at them. This stage is when the bluffing really takes place, feel free to switch targets, intimidate, trash talk, and fold at any time.
-Last chance, Dealer declares "Last chance to fold, targets locked!". From this point on players cannot switch targets. Players may not fold once this phase has ended.
Fire!, all together players call out "3, 2, 1, FIRE!" and flip over their cards, revealing what weapons they were using.
-Determine winners, Determining the winner is simple; if you lost a primary shield this round or were disintegrated, you lost. Otherwise the player has won and will split the pot with the other winners. crystals that cannot be split evenly between the winners remain in the pot for the next round.
-Play continues until either one player has all the crystals or is the only one alive.

The chef that makes the most dishes wins!

Tabula Pasta is a game where the players are all chefs working at a small Italian restaurant. They are trying to out do each other by getting the most satisfied customers. Each customer's order is determined by rolling three special dice (meat, pasta, and sauce). The chefs must race against each other to fulfil the orders using the cards in their hand, the deck, and the pantry. The chef with the most satisfied customers by the end of the game is the winner!

The board game is a table, set for four persons. To begin the game you place a meeple (patron) in each chair. Determine each patron's order by rolling their own set of special dice, one each of a sauce, meat, and pasta die. The players (chefs) then have to match the patron's order by drawing cards from the grocery delivery (deck) or the pantry (three piles of discarded cards, shared by everyone, that haven't been used to fill an order). Once an order has been filled the player shows everyone their match, moves the patron next to them to keep score, places the used cards in the bus bin (discard pile, you can never dig from the trash to complete an order, that's gross!), and finally seats a new patron (if any are left) and rolls a new order for them. Play continues until everyone has been served.

In addition to the six types of pasta, meat, and sauce cards there are also wild cards and secret ingredient cards (a pasta, meat, or sauce that counts as any other type, but you can only use one to fill an order). There are variations for faster games: using less patrons and/or removing the meat cards and dice (light lunch).

description:
Japanese analog game developer Group SNE makes their popular horror themed tabletop RPG "Ghost Hunter" into a fine co-op tile laying board game.

In this newly introduced rendition of the game, as a team, players explore a mysterious mansion to complete several missions ( long forgotten artifacts, monsters hard to beat, mysteries about the mansion, etc etc... ) which bundled scenarios task them. Players can move their chosen heroes ( each one gets uniquely powered and equipped ) up to two tiles. If there's no tile to move in, you may draw　as many tiles as you need from a stock for placement.

Tile by tile, the mansion would gradually reveal itself to players. On their explorations, your heroes sustain mental/physical damages ( your  characters might go mad or die if it's too much to bear ), fight off some deadly monsters, and discover powerful artifacts to help your team keep on.

description:
It is the year 2056, not much is left of mankind. A nuclear war destroyed all civilisations on earth. The survivors are in desperate need of food. For food, even radiated, means at least short-term survival. Can your group of survivors outlive the other groups in the struggle for food and survival?

Starvation is a quick boardgame that can be played with 2 to 5 players. Although the rules are simple the tactics are not.

The game starts with a board with scattered food tokens. At the start all players take turn placing their survivor pieces on the board. After this the game starts.
Each player first has to choose use up food to let his pieces survive or to give up his pieces. After that he is allowed to let his pieces move, taking with him a limited amount of food. The player with the last survivor on the board wins.

A division to army level game having a map from Pskov up to Leningrad (but not Finland or Finns) and east over to strategic Tichvin.  20mis/32kms per hex, 11 10-day turns.  Terrain is simplified:  it was all bad up there, off the roads.  :)

Game system uses Disruption and Entrenchable (flip-side) infantry/rifle units, to simulate momentum of advance and/or positional inertia.

Includes special rules for the Red Banner Baltic Fleet and Marines, railway guns, Red Air Force 7th Fighter (interceptor) Aviation Korps, optional reinforcements (historically used), etc.

Free, to print off, assemble, and play.

There was an old Lady who swallowed a fly...  This trick-taking game is themed on the popular children’s song. The object is to take tricks by playing the highest-ranked animal card. Problem is, when an animal card is played, its rank changes and usually for the worse.

Before each round, ranking cards are placed on the table in a row in order of rank:  fly, spider, bird, cat, dog, goat, cow, horse. The fly is the lowest ranked at the start, and the horse is the highest. The animal cards are shuffled and dealt. The start player plays a card, and the corresponding card in the rank row is shifted down one space. If an animal is already the lowest ranked animal, it is moved to the furthest space on the right and becomes the highest ranked animal.

Play proceeds clockwise. In turn, each player plays a card. A player must follow suit if possible, otherwise he may play any card.  Each played card causes the corresponding card in the rank row to shift down as described above.

After everyone has played, the player whose card matches the currently highest ranked animal wins the trick (regardless of suit). If two or more players have played the same ‘winning’ card, the player who played first wins the trick.

This is a 3-4 player game. Partnership rules are included as well.

In this two-player card game, one person takes on the role of the Hero while the other takes on the role of the Witch. The Hero and the Witch must memorize the layout of the "Cursed Wood" (a 4x4 grid of cards) and then flip over the cards so no one knows what they are. From there, the Hero (using only their memory), must traverse the "Cursed Wood" by flipping over cards one at a time. If the Hero finds 5 Blood Roses, they succeed in their quest! If they find 5 Frights, the Hero fails!

Magic and mayhem add complication to the Hero's quest! The Witch can shift the cards and change the layout of the "Cursed Wood" each time the Hero finds a Fright!

MicroSport Soccer is a fast-playing finger-flicking action game played with a portable board and small coin.

The basic game revolves around players taking turns flicking the coin in an attempt to land it on the goal. Individual player zones govern ball possession, while additional rules cover passing, turnovers, throw-ins, and goal kicks. The adjustable size of each team's player zones and goals allows amateurs to compete with experts.

A previously unpublished expansion for the ASL Starter Kits, utilizing boards and countermixes for ASLSK 2 and 3. Included as an insert to Tactical Wargamer's Journal, Issue 3.

Sweeping the Doorstep is a two-player scenario depicting the fighting at Carpiquet in July 1944. The scenario was designed as part of the Battles of Carpiquet (q.v.) contest but was not released with those scenarios as it was the only ASLSK scenario in the contest. In addition to the scenario, playtest notes and historical articles are included.

Tactical Wargamer's Journal (ISSN 1918-9729), published on a non-regular schedule, covers topics of interest on all aspects of the world of commercial tactical wargaming, with its focus set on warfare at the man-to-man, squad and platoon level in the 20th Century and contemporary era. Tactical Wargamer's Journal (TWJ) places emphasis on both military history and the history of tactical level gaming itself.

Issue 3 focuses on the history of the 21st Army Group in Northwest Europe and depictions of the Commonwealth Armies in that theatre in tactical-level wargames.

description:
Rock city is a dice placement game in which players command their own band and compete to develop and present the best songs during the three festivals of this great city.

Before each of the festivals, players have 6 rounds of action. In each round, players roll 3 dices each, and during his turn he can perform one action. To perform the action, the player will use 1, 2 or 3 dices. The round goes on until no player has any action left (i.e. dices).

The actions vary between perform in underground bars, practice your musics, write new lyrics, buy materials at the music shop, publish a sample CD or just perform at the Casino to get some extra cash. Each of those spaces needs a different combination between quantity of dices and the sum of them.

Once players have written one or more lyrics, they are ready to try to get the best melody possible for that tune!

The winner of the game is the player with most points, that will be earned by playing musics during the shows. You can also earn points by adding a nice production value to your show or having some fancy different instruments on your band.

Your humble tribe has struggled generation after generation for mere subsistence. After eons of hunting and gathering, feasts and famine, war and peace, your people have finally entered an age of innovation.

With the advent of horticulture, your tribe has settled into a sedentary way of life and through this change, idle minds have begun to develop new ways of understanding and manipulating the world around them. Great advancements are being made in subsistence, government, technology, culture, commerce, diplomacy, and warfare.

This sea change has inspired a need for guidance and through careful consideration your people have elected you to lead them into the future.

Civicus is a civilization themed, tile placement and area control game in which players compete for territories, resources and exotic goods. These essential elements of the game are earned, maintained, and developed through sound logic, thoughtful tile placement, and strategic planning.

Each round the players will take turns placing new terrain tiles. Strategic tile placement is crucial for a player’s success, as this aspect of the game determines the geographical matrix for game play and dictates resource and action point generation. Resources and action points are required for nearly every aspect of the game, including settlement and temple construction, attribute development, and wealth generation.

Throughout four ages, the players will compete for territory control, by building and developing settlements. In turn, these territories will provide the players with the raw materials required for cultivating a great and powerful nation. The greatness of your nation is determined, in part, by a preset scoring system which focuses heavily on settlement construction, exotic good control, as well as, fortune and fame.

The nation with the most victory points at the end of the fourth age wins!
          [Crowds cheer… fireworks erupt… enemies conspire]

boardgame: Reise um die Erde: ein humoristisches geographisches Gesellschaftsspiel
description:
The first game published by the Otto Maier Verlag, later to be known as Ravensburger Spieleverlag.

The game was inspired by Jules Verne's "Around the World in Eighty Days". The players travel around the board rolling dice to get to the field with the number 80 accomplishing their travel around the world, using money to overcome any troubles along the way.

boardgame: Dueling Eagles: Aerial Combat over Malta
description:
Published as Spel!enkaart #0013 in Spel! 17.2 December 2013.
Postcard game.

4 turn game. The Italian player wins when succeeding in bombing his target with the BR-20M or by eliminating one the English planes without losing one of his own. The British player wins when the target is still on the map or when he succeeds in eliminating more than one Italian plane.
The game ends sooner when either British or Italian player loses al his planes before the 4th turn.

An additional standard card deck is needed to play.

In this game Snoopy has four different wishes: becoming a top pilot, top baseball player, top dog or famous writer. (Each of the cards is 4 times in the game.) All players (2-8) try to collect the five cards that are part of the same wish.

You can collect by walking around on the board or owing to a 'menu-card'. The first player who collects his 5 cards is the winner.

Orðabelgur is intended for 2-6 participants and suits people of all ages. This game encourages players to listen and think, be merry and be informed. The game offers contemplation, consideration, word-play and jest, and participants play with the Icelandic language in new ways. The vocabulary in the game covers a great number of Icelandic words, both old and new. The aim of the game is to collect five words in a sack by answering questions from five categories according to certain rules.

The questions are on cards, each indicating which category it belongs to. Two questions of different difficulty levels are on each card, making the game a good fit for a family. A word-shark directs the game, and reads our questions and possible answers and awards players with words for their word-sack when they have been earned.

Playout: The Game™ makes fitness fun for people of all ages and fitness abilities. Turn bodyweight exercise into a fun competition with over 10 unique games and infinite exercise combinations, all using a single deck of cards. Why workout when you can PLAYOUT!


     Play alone or with friends
     No additional equipment needed!
     Full body fitness in 10 minutes of fun!


Playout: The Game™ is an exercise card game that makes fitness fun through social play, competition, variety, and adaptability. With Playout, you can get fit while having fun, anywhere, anytime.

30 BODYWEIGHT EXERCISE CARDS - 10 Upper, 10 Core, 10 Lower - A comprehensive full body calisthenic routine for all fitness levels. Each card features an individual exercise and includes direction and tips on how to perform the excercise.  In addition, each card includes three rep counts to vary the difficulty as well as a point value for completing the reps for the exercise.

12 WILDCARDS - Challenges to increase the difficulty and variability of the exercises by adding reps, incorporating new motions or body positions, slowing down motions, or adding new exercises.  Depending on the game played, the Wild (i.e. Joker) cards include directions for each of the difficulty levels that can be applied to an Exercise card to make it more difficult. Additional points are awarded for completing the Joker cards.


     8 MINIGAMES - Intensely fun competitions that push you to the limit. When these cards are encountered, a mini game occurs that really ratchets up the game.  10 or 20 additional points can be earned for both participating and winning the minigame.


Stretch, Warm-Up, Cool-Down, Rule, and Information Cards

GAMES

     The Playout deck can either used as an activity to facilitate an exercise routine, or it can be used to play several games.  These games can be played as individual players or team vs. team:
         Playout Pro (High Intensity)
         The 6 Minute Playout (Moderate/High Intensity)
         Playout Rummy (Low/Moderate Intensity) - Be the first to get rid of all you cards by making sets.  From each set made, choose an exercise for yourself and opponents to perform.  Award points to those who complete the exercises.
         Playout Grand Prix 
         Playout All-Star
    


SCORING

     In general, scoring works as follows:
         Exercise Card:
             Full reps - 10 points + WildCard value
             At least half the rep = WildCard value
             Less then half the reps = no points
        
         MiniGame Card:
             Qualify = 10 points
             Winner = 20 points
        
    


The player (or team, if playing in teams) with the most points  at the end of a particular game (or set of games in the case of a multi-game competition) is the winner.

Game found in the Postman Pat Annual for 1998.

A simple game for pre-school children.

Each player has a tree with 20 apples on it. In your turn, a coin is flipped and the following happens:
Heads - Pick 1 apple (cover an apple with a small piece of card or similar)
Tails - Pick 2 apples (and cover as before).

The winner is the first player to cover every apple on their tree.

Game appeared in the Postman Pat Annual for 1995.

Children are encouraged to take a walk through Greendale village and meet some of their favourite characters.

Simple roll and move game for pre-school children. Players must obey instructions if they land on a pink space. The first to reach space Z, wins.

Game appeared in the Postman Pat Annual for 1994.

A simple roll and move game for pre-school children. The Greendale policeman is having a busy day. Each player rolls the die and moves, obeying instructions when they land on a beige space. The first to get to the end of PC Selby's day (space 49), wins.

Game featured in the Postman Pat Annual for 1994.

Simple game for pre-school children.

Each player uses one of the two pictures. The pictures are divided in to 10 oblong segments. These are numbered from one to four. In a turn, the player rolls the die and if he rolls a 1, 2, 3, or 4, he covers one segment with that number (use a piece of card or similar to mark that the segment has been rolled). If a 5 is rolled on the die, the player must uncover one of the segments. If a 6 is rolled, the player can cover two segments of his choice.

First player to cover all 10 segments, wins.

Game appeared in the Postman Pat Annual for 1994.

A simple game for pre-school children.

Players roll a die to move along the trach to the top of the hill, where they can have a picnic with Postman Pat. Should a player land on a rabbit hole, they go down to the other end of the hole. Should they land on a short-cut, they can move nearer the top of the hill. The first to get there, wins.

Game appeared in the Postman Pat Annual for 1993.

A simple roll and move game for pre-school children.

Players must get from Sam Waldron's house to Pat's house for tea. Move according to your die roll and obey any light brown spaces you land on. First to get to the end of the route, wins.

Game appeared in the Postman Pat Annual for 1993.

A simple roll and move game for pre-school children.

Players are in an air race over Greendale. Move according to your die roll. If you land on the light blue spaces, you must obey the instructions. First to space 35, wins.

Game appeared in the Postman Pat Annual for 1993.

Two players are required. Each gets a picture board featuring 6 characters from the Postman Pat stories. In your turn, roll a die. Using playing cards as 'markers', you cover the picture that matches your roll on the die. If you roll the same number twice, you miss a turn. First to cover all six characters on their picture board, wins.

12-21-12 the game where you spend the last day of the earth however you like. Be it rape, pedophilia, murder, arson, whatever you want! Afterall, there aren't any consequences during the last day!

This micro-expansion gives you the ability to kill off the other players to make sure your final day is better than theirs!

The Bible Game is a trivia game based off the Bible. There are 4000 questions and Answers with Bible references. There is also seven different categories;

1. Life of Christ.
2. New Testament.
3. Old Testament.
4. W.W.W. Who, Where, or Woman.
5. Quotes.
6. Alpha and Omega.
7. General Knowledge.

Game description from the publisher:

"Money is honest. Credits may be cold, uncaring, heartless abstractions, but they offer an unavoidable truth: The more, the better." –Iain Stirling

The network lies before you; which path will you take? Focusing primarily on the interests of Jinteki and three new Criminal runners, Honor and Profit is a deluxe expansion for Android: Netrunner - The Card Game. Its 165 new cards (three copies each of fifty-five different cards) feature a dizzying array of new tricks and traps, events to alter the tempo of the game, new psi talents, and six new identity cards that engender the exploration of all-new deck-building strategies. Additionally, fans of every faction will discover plenty to love among the expansion's neutral ambushes, ice, ops, AI, and connections.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

A new peril erupts in the realm with The Firelands expansion for Talisman (revised 4th edition). The legendary Ifrit have returned to exact fiery vengeance on the land of Talisman. The Ifrit's inferno will char the land, burning away players' resources and setting entire regions ablaze. Players must race to battle this terrible threat since only the Crown of Command can truly extinguish the Ifrit's flames. Only the greatest of heroes will rise from the flames to complete the quest across The Firelands.

Talisman: The Firelands includes four new characters: the Dervish, the Warlord, the Nomad, and the Jin Blooded. New terrain cards reshape the land in the wake of the Ifrit's destruction, and three new alternative ending cards offer players different ways to engage in the endgame.

boardgame: Talisman (Revised 4th Edition): The Nether Realm Expansion
description:
Game description by the publisher:

Journey through the realm of Talisman on your quest for the Crown of Command but beware the fiery Pyrochanter, fearsome Titan Wraith, and other Nether Deck enemies that seek to thwart your plans in The Nether Realm expansion.

The Nether Realm — designed by Jon New, the man behind the Talisman-dedicated fansite Talisman Island — offers three new alternative endings for Talisman, along with new Nether cards. Fate also plays a part in this expansion, helping against some of the larger foes found in the Nether deck, but it could also prove to be your downfall.
Mickey Mouse Flip Chip is an odd kind of connect 4 game, you use levers built into the base to launch chips up that randomly fall into the board. First player to 4 in a row wins.

Krampus is a Werewolf/Mafia re-theme for the holidays where some players are Krampusz, a race of Alpine Christmas demons who abduct naughty children and carry them off to their lair to eat.  Most of the players, though, are children who are trying to tattle to the Parent (the moderator) about the right Krampusz. There are also a variety of other Christmas roles, including Frosty, Elf on the Shelf, Jesus, and the Ghosts of Christmas Past, Present, and Future.

If the children correctly tattle on all of the Krampusz, they win. If the Krampusz outnumber the children, they win.

Collect a Corpse is a tile laying, build your own royal corpse, game for two players. There is also some dice rolling.

You start the game with either the torso of the king or the queen of Sweden. The goal is to finish your corpse as complete  as possible with your own limbs at the right places.

On your turn you draw a card that either shows a bodypart of either the king or the queen, a black dice or a white dice.
If it's a bodypart (it doesn't have to be your own) place it on your torso. You can later exchange them for parts matching your torso if you have the maximum number of that part (usually 2).
If it's a card roll a die and see the charts: The white die is usually constructive for you and the black die destructive for your opponent.

When the last card is drawn you score points. Parts matching your torso score double points if they are in the right place otherwise the score the number printed on them (inner parts give 2 the outer 3 points). You get no points for the opponents parts on your body.

Knightcap is played by two players (light and dark) on an 8x8 chess-like board with 63 horses of the same color. Both player can use every horse as long as that horse can jump to an empty field in a chess-like manner. The piece jumped over is captured.
The dark player only captures pieces on dark fields, the light player only those on light fields. Multiple captures in a single turn are possible.

A single point is scored for every horse captured over the amount of 30. First player to reach 10 points wins. Several rounds may be played to reach the goal.

Democracy was published in the year of the first democratic elections in South Africa - 1994.

In Democracy you are the leader of a political party. All players try to increase their influence with partymembers and their assets such as business, news papers, TV etc. (75 influence cards). Some of the character- and asset-cards show guaranteed votes. The assets must be assigned to a character.
Players can trade their influence cards (assets) freely.

There are 120 event cards with positive and negative effects - so there is a lot of luck. These generate income, which is needed to pay some bad events or the travel-costs from region to region by car or plane, or have direct effects to your political reputation.

The partymembers travel throughout the country canvassing in the regions. Each region has a base resistance of 7. The influence of the canvassing partymember(s) + their assets + D6 must be greater to win the region. Players mark their regions with vote counters - each region is worth 1 vote.

An election is called - 2 ways:
- Automatically after all 50 regions are won, or
- A player can call for an election if he has one of 3 election-card (and only after a minimum of rounds are over, depending on number of players).
Than the last 5 rounds are played. In this "window period" nearly everything is allowed. Players can play special influence cards such as muckrakers, callgirls, third forces or assassins to "destroy" the political reputation or even kill the party leader. Hopefully he has (and can pay) bodyguards ...
Only in this period a player can take a region from another party.

Winning of the game - 2 ways:
- When all other party leaders are killed you win
- After the election, the player with the most votes wins.

Boston, December 16, 1773. The Sons of Liberty boarded the tea ships and threw the chests of tea into Boston Harbor. This is the key event to the birth of American Revolutionary war which leads to the United States of America birth. In this game you will relive this historical adventure.

The deck is composed of 57 cards: 24 American cards, 24 Great Britain cards and 9 special cards

In each turn, you launch an attack to the next player. He/she can defend it or ally to it. To ally, he/she needs to play a card with the same numeric value, without taking into account the faction. The joint attack passes to the next player. This third player can ally or defend too. To defend, he/she needs to play cards from the opposite faction and with a higher number value than the attacking ones. After defending, each player can add more cards to the attack.  If the attack is defended properly the attacking and defending cards are discarded. If it is not, the defending player puts in his hand all attacking and defending cards. When the deck runs out, the first player who runs out of cards is the winner. Also, there are special cards which modify a little bit the game rules.

A diceless card driven Euro style board game about a Zombie Pandemic on the US Passenger Rail Network.

Description from the Kickstarter:

"In Zomtrack, the cards control the action.

You draw a hand of cards and use them to implement the strategy you have chosen. Back the survivors and spend your time destroying infestations and gaining victory points (VPs) and bonus cards. Did the horde you backed become dominant? Did the survivors’ strongholds hold out? End-of-game bonus cards score based on how well the strategy you backed ends up working."

The classic Monopoly game with a Seattle Mariners theme. Beloved Mariners Players & Coaches replace the classic Monopoly properties. Ichiro and Safeco Field replace Park Place and Boardwalk. Home and Away Jersey cards replace classic Chance & Community Chest cards.

"Save the Christmas" in English.
Roll and move on the board and who finishes first is the winner.

DDR Forest is a two-player constructible board game that combines strategy and luck. Collect three Relic Cards and land on your opponent’s Starting Square. Die rolls determine your movement. Cards are played to hinder your opponent’s progress. The board unfolds at random each turn allowing for a different map every game.

Tired of "Tic-Tac-Toe" and "Hangman"? Try Pencil Football (a.k.a. Airport Football)! It's a paper and pencil game for 1 or 2 players simulating a game of American Football. All you need to play is something to write on and something to write with. That means it's free!

The game is played in 4 quarters. Each quarter consists of a player taking a turn on offense and on defense. Offense always starts at their opponent's 35 yard line.

Offense: You have 4 downs to score. Write the appropriate letters in a 6-digit code to represent your play:
Run = three 'R's 
Pass = two 'P's
Hail-Mary = three 'P's
Field Goal = one, two, or three 'F's (depending on field position: >30, 10 - 30, <10 yard line)

Defense: Without seeing your opponent's play, choose 3 distinct numbers between 1 and 6. These are the left-to-right digits in the offense's play that you block this down.

A run is successful and moves the offense 10 yards if at least two 'R's are unblocked. 
A pass is successful and moves the offense 20 yards if both 'P's are unblocked.
A hail-mary is successful and results in an instant touchdown if all 3 'P's are unblocked.
A field goal is successful if at least 1 'F' is unblocked.

If you score a touchdown you can attempt a field goal from the 5 yard line or attempt a 2-point conversion from the 5 yard line.

description: Players roll a colored die and are allowed to place a piece of the color they rolled on a space of the same color on their "Pipifax", a jester whose clothing they need to fill with pieces.

Players place triangular tiles with colored edges, trying to form squares. However, tiles can only be placed in a way so that adjacent edges have the same color. Some triangles contain apples or pears. Forming squares where all tiles contain the same fruit yields double points.

The ThemeYou are a priest of a troubled Temple.  A Thieves’ Guild has emerged and is challenging your Temple for influence over the land.  You must decide whether to work along with your brethren in the fight against the Thieves Guild or betray your brothers and guide the Thieves’ Guild to prominence.

				
				
					GameplayIn Allegiance, players use cards to adjust the influence of the Thieves’ Guild and Temple. At the end of the game, the organization with the most influence emerges to prominence. Players’ allegiance can shift during the game as they accrue cards that align them with either the Temple or the Thieves’ Guild. All players with allegiance to the Temple win if the Temple has the most influence. One player with allegiance to the Thieves’ Guild wins if the Thieves’ Guild has the most influence.

Will the players maintain allegiance to the Temple and work cooperatively to try to achieve victory? Or will one or more of them turn rogue and align with the Thieves’ Guild in a high-risk, high-reward attempt to win it all?

Pochecko is a strategy board game that combines aspects of Poker and Chess. It is a 2-4 player game for ages 12+.

In Pochecko, the players move markers around a board in order to claim poker cards and build a poker hand off of 1 of 2 community cards.  As a counter maneuver, players may instead move one of the community blockers in order to block opponents from claiming the hand they are going for. The player with the best 5 card poker hand at the end of the game wins.

From the rules: "The object of the game is not only to afford amusement to the players but also to illustrate to them how proposed Share-the-Wealth Plans, Excessive Old Age Pension Plans, etc., will increase taxes and place a heavy burden on all citizens and, at the same time, make it possible for the shrewd manipulators to gain a dominating position in economical affairs of the country.

At the outset, let it be understood that before the Government can bring about a share-the-wealth program it must own all properties and all monies. Under a share-the-wealth scheme it is proposed that the Government shall make every man a king and give each person an equal share of the money, after which time each must take care of himself. If a man has good luck and is a shrewd manager, he may beat the game of "inflation." If he isn't, he will be no better off than theretofore and distribution of the wealth of the country will not have availed him anything".


This game is played like modern Monopoly. From Wikipedia: "Inflation board game came to the attention of Parker Brothers management in the 1930s, after they began sales of Monopoly. Copeland continued sales of the latter game after Parker Brothers attempted a patent lawsuit against him. Parker Brothers held the Magie and Darrow patents, but settled with Copeland rather than going to trial, since Copeland was prepared to have witnesses testify that they had played Monopoly before Darrow's "invention" of the game. The court settlement allowed Copeland to license Parker Brothers' patents".
