boardgame: Board with Life: The Metagame (The Microgame)
description:
Board With Life: The Metagame (The Microgame) is a 2-5 player card game representing a single game being played by the Board With Life crew. Each of the 5 main characters is represented by a suit/color, and each has an identical set of 5 action cards, with corresponding power that will help that character win the (fictional) game in question:

- Eliminate (1) - removes another card from the game
- Defend (2) - protects previously played cards
- Swap (3) - swaps cards between players
- Strategize (4) - adds to the power of any other card
- Gloat (5) - does nothing really, but has the most power

The game begins with a quick draft so each player can develop their own strategy, but each turn of the game consists of a player playing a single card. You execute its action, and the card remains in front of you as power towards winning that character. Players are vying to have the highest total power in each character, but player with the highest only scores points equal to the power his opponents control. And since there are five different characters, there are five opportunities to score, so players want to have the highest strength in each character, but only by a slight margin and in as many characters as you can for maximum points.

Distributed as a free print & play game released to backers of the web series.

A library-themed spin on the popular Cards Against Humanity.

To begin, each player is dealt 10 white cards, and one player (the judge) begins the game by flipping over the first black card.  The other players then put down one white card each that fill in the blank on the black card.  The judge then decides which submission they think is funniest. The player who submitted the winning card gets to keep the black card.

The game is free to print and share online.


Cards Against Librarianship by Emily Lloyd is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.
In this game you try to be the first player to complete your 6-piece puzzle. The die shows you which puzzle piece you can take from the table – if it fits in your puzzle, you get to keep it!

Noms is a four-player Whist-like card game with bidding, in which the bidder's partner is chosen by calling a card, and the identity of the partner remains secret until the called card is played. A standard 52 card pack is used.

Deal
Any player may deal first, and the turn to deal passes to the left after each hand. The cards are not shuffled between deals: the dealer simply gathers the cards from the previous hand, stacking the tricks together, cuts the pack and starts dealing. The deal will result in everyone having a hand of 13 cards.

Bidding
Bids are an undertaking to win at least the stated number of tricks with the help of a partner chosen by nominating a card. The bidder will choose the trump suit but the bids do not mention a suit, only the number of tricks.

Play
The highest bidder leads to the first trick, naming the trump suit and the called card. The identity of the partner is definitively revealed only when the called card is played, though it may be guessed earlier because the partner will try to play cards that will help the bidder.

Scoring
The bid succeeds if the bidder together with the holder of the nominated card win at least as many tricks as the number that was bid. In this case they each score the amount of the bid.  If the bidder and partner fail to win enough tricks between them, the other two players score the amount of the bid.

A separate cumulative score is kept for each player and the game can continue until someone reaches the agreed target score, typically 101. The player with most points then wins.

boardgame: Ravensburger Spiele Minis: Such Die Affen
description:
"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

This is a game similar to Mastermind but smaller. Each player receives a screen, 3 monkeys (red, blue, yellow) and a board with 6 spaces from 1 to 6.

Each round a player will hide his monkeys (in spaces 1 to 6) while the other tries to guess their location. For that the second player will place her monkeys where she thinks player 1 has his and reveal her board. Player 1 then has to say how many monkeys are in the right position and so on until she gets it right. Then the roles will be exchanged. The player that guesses the right combination in the fewest tries will be the winner.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

The object of the game is to win as many Word cards as possible.

Each round a player will reveal one of the cards (that are placed on the table) and spin the roulette. Then all players will try to say a word that starts with the letter indicated by the roulette and that is in the concept shown by the cards. The first player to do so will win the card.

Cards can have the following categories: profession, food, vehicle, drink, country, name, plant, star, animal.

Content: 1 letters roulette, 9 word cards

Black Stories are morbid and mysterious riddles for teenagers and adults. The players try to reconstruct the crime by asking, guessing and fiddling about.

This was published as a special collector's edition with 110 stories, containing the 50 stories from Black Stories, the 50 stories from Black Stories 2 and 10 additional fan made stories.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

Content: 8 big cards, 8 small cards, 1 pawn, 1 die.

The big cards are placed in a circle face up. Small cards are placed in the center face down. In a turn you roll the die and advance the pawn. Then you must try to reveal the small card that matches the picture in the big card where the pawn is. If you do so you can keep the small card. If the small card is already in front of another player you take it from him. If you are the one holding the small card requested, then you win!

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

12 numbered cards in 2 colors are placed face down in the table. 
On a turn you must throw 2 dice and then revealed 2 number cards. If the number and colors of the cards match the ones on the dice you receive a triumph chip. Then you place the cards face down again. If they don’t match then the other players in turn try to revealed the corresponding cards.

The game ends when a player gets 2 triumph chips.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

A player holds a cup while the others have a mouse by the tail (they are in the middle of the table touching by their noses). Then the player holding the cup rolls a die and must quickly catch the mouse of the color indicated by de die.

Only the player with the corresponding mouse can move his mouse trying to escape.

If the player holding the cup catches the mouse, they receive a victory point. If not, it is gained by the player owner of the mouse (who will have the cup now).

If another "mouse" moves its owner must move around the table in 4 feet.

The game ends when a player reaches 5 victory points.

"Spiele Minis" is a series of small surprise boxes (i.e., you don't know what you get) from Ravensburger. They are meant to be carry-on or gifts, suitable for children ("Mitbringspiel"). Some of the cubes contain actual games, others contain magic tricks, puzzles etc.

20 pieces of cloth are placed in the middle of the table. Each player has a clothes line.

On your turn you must throw a die and you get a cloth in the corresponding color, which you hang on your clothes line. If no cloths of that color are left then you receive nothing.

If you throw a sun you must take a sunbeam and place it aside (there are 6). If the sun in the die is shining you can chose any cloth from the middle, but if its unhappy you must return one of yours to the middle (or one of an opponent if you don’t have any left).

The game ends when the sixth sunbeam is taken. The player with more cloth on his clothes line wins!

An educational game for developing memory  and the powers of observation. 
In the old days, children used to be told about the spooks, which were rather sweet creatures and really liked playing hide-and-seek. It is never easy to tell where a spook is hiding for it might be under the carpet… or behind the curtain…  or somewhere else...

Objective: gain most victory points finding matching cards
 - acting by turns -
there are 64 cards with matching qualities regarding: colour (4), animal (4), mood (4); additionally on each card you see a mathematical symbols like (2 arrow; arrow 3..)
8 cards are placed in a circle, hidden. Active player turns one and follows shown symbol e.g. 3 steps to the right, turns appropiate card and follows shown symbol again. If all cards are turned or ending in a vicius cycle player may take up to 3 cards putting them on her/his hand. Matching cards (animal/mood/colour) can be formed and dropped (victory points depending on number and matching items). Handlimit: 5 cards

Apart from plenty of buildings to construct, there are also orders to process and events to take care of. Der Bauherr was conceived for two players but also works with more.

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Expansion II – Tribute of Blood
description:
Mythic Battles: Expansion 2 - Tribute of Blood includes new and powerful Mercenaries Units who may now join your army. You may now enroll Chimera, Hydra, Myrmidons, a catapult or brigands.  Players can reinforce their armies while facing off against one another in six new epic scenarios.

Mythic Battles: Expansion 2 - Tribute of Blood also includes Achilles and Atalanta, two new Heroes with otherworldly powers to add to your deck to get bonuses.

Due to confusing background advertising game is also listed as

Objective: be the first completing your player board with coloured chips & learning colours.

- Acting by turns -

Choose a playerboard and throw a coloured d6; you may take a corresponding chip and place it upon an appropriate space of your board. If the die shows 'white': chip is up to your choice, with 'black' you must return a chip of your choice.

Player with a complete player-board wins.

If I Ran the Zoo...Con: The Smofcon 3 game is a simulation of organizing a WorldCon event. Players form committees, perform actions, make decisions, and follow through on those decisions through the bidding, planning, and convention phases of the process using a choose-your-own-adventure style situation resolution book. By conserving goodwill, utilizing people, and balancing finances one committee will produce the most successful event and be crowned WonderCon, while the others will bear the label BlunderCon.

From the publisher's website

It’s every Gunship for itself in our highly-destructive Free-for-All Mode!

Form temporary truces as you must guard from attack from more than one direction.  But remember: Only ONE side will be left standing when the debris field clears!

Set includes one Green Carrier Board and one White Carrier Board.  Also includes two standard 12-sided dice.

Each player gets 4 rockets and a Sputnik. The moves are determined by a color die. The starting spaces are the colored dots on the rockets at the edge of the game board. The Sputnik is the last piece to start. It circles the Earth till the last rocket has arrived on its destination space. The first Player, who gets his rockets on the 4 destination spaces near his starting spaces and lands the Sputnik on earth in the center of the board, wins the game. A rocket, which lands on a space with another rocket on it, capture this rocket and releases it only when it reaches its destination space.

This game was published 1957 (launch of Sputnik 1) or up to a few years later.

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Z-95 Headhunter Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

In many ways, the Z-95 Headhunter was the precursor to the X-wing. Though the Z-95 was later outclassed by the X-wing in most respects, it could perform a tighter turn and was cheap, durable, and reliable enough that the Rebel Alliance continued to make use of it throughout the Galactic Civil War, most commonly in close air support roles.

The Z-95 Headhunter enters the Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game as the Rebel starfighter with the lowest squad point cost. None of the four ship cards in the Z-95 Headhunter Expansion Pack weigh in at more than nineteen squad points, and at just twelve points, the Bandit Squadron Pilot ties the Imperial's Academy Pilot as the starfighter with the game's lowest point cost value. However, the Z-95 Headhunter isn't designed to fly like a TIE fighter; it comes, instead, with the focus and target lock actions, as well as the ability to load and fire a missile. Accordingly, the Z-95 Headhunter Expansion Pack comes with two missile upgrades, the Assault Missiles and the deadly Ion Pulse Missiles.

Altogether, the Z-95 Headhunter Expansion Pack includes one Z-95 miniature, four ship cards, five upgrade cards, one maneuver dial, and all the tokens necessary to bring your Z-95 Headhunter to battle!
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – TIE Defender Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

When it was first released shortly before the Battle of Endor, the TIE defender was perhaps the most technologically advanced starfighter in the galaxy. It improved upon the design of the TIE fighter by adding shields, a hyperdrive, and more powerful weaponry.

In Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game, the TIE defender is a lethal starfighter that features three attack, three agility, three hull, and three shields. Imperial players can further load it up with both cannon and missile upgrades, and because only the Empire's most elite pilots were allowed to fly the TIE defender during the Galatic Civil War, both of the unique pilots among the expansion’s four ship cards come with the elite talent upgrade.

The TIE Defender Expansion Pack introduces one TIE defender miniature, four ship cards, one maneuver dial, all the tokens you need, and five upgrade cards, which include a pair of elite talents certain to make your elite TIE defender pilot a truly fearsome predator throughout the game's fast-paced dogfights.

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – E-Wing Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

When it was designed, the E-wing was intended to match or exceed the performance of the X-wing in every respect, and the E-Wing Expansion Pack for the Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game introduces a starfighter that combines the best of the X-wing's firepower and durability with the A-wing's speed and maneuverability.

Furthermore, the E-wing's advanced technology is reflected in the fact that it can equip both system and astromech upgrades alongside a complement of torpedoes. The partnership of these upgrades permit some interesting, new combinations, and the E-wing may open some wildly creative squad builds. For example, by partnering R7-T1 with Advanced Sensors on a ship piloted by someone like Etahn A'baht, who is capable of using "Push the Limit" (included in the A-Wing Expansion Pack and Imperial Aces), you could perform three actions before you reveal your maneuver. And if that maneuver is green, Etahn A'baht would even end his Activation phase without a stress token!

With its E-wing miniature, four ship cards, five upgrade cards, maneuver dial, and tokens, the E-Wing Expansion Pack comes with everything you need to pursue entirely new tactics in your X-Wing battles!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – TIE Phantom Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

By adding a stygium crystal cloaking device to a modified V38 assault fighter that came equipped with both deflector shields and a hyperdrive, the developers of the TIE phantom created one of the most menacing starfighters in the galaxy!

The TIE Phantom Expansion Pack brings this fearsome fighter to life in the Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game with new rules for cloaking that allow it to slip off opponents' sensors in the middle of battle. Imperial players will be able to further customize their phantoms with system and crew upgrades, so the Rebellion will never know what to expect.

The TIE Phantom Expansion Pack includes one TIE phantom miniature, four ship cards, five upgrades, one maneuver dial, reference cards for its new rules, and all the tokens you need to bring your TIE phantom to battle!
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Tombalo is a balancing game. Together, the players are building a dam from sticks and logs. Each player starts with a supply of sticks and logs.

Each turn, a player will draw a card, which shows how many sticks he may add to the dam. He then has to place these sticks on top of the existing construction (or on top of the starting pillars, if there is nothing yet). If any sticks drop in to the water, the player gets new sticks from the supply.

The first player to get rid of all his sticks wins the game.

Divinity is a cooperative turn-based card game for 2 to 4 people, in which each player assumes the role of a deity trying to forge the universe as it is eaten away by powerful disasters of mysterious origin. As players work together, the universe either succumbs to the calamities or is saved by the grace of the gods!

The goal of the players in Divinity is to accumulate points by creating various objects in their galaxies. The divine cards are used by players to achieve their goal, while calamity cards are force-played through to game, undermining the players' efforts. Depth is created through the interaction of all these cards, creating new situations every game and requiring players to consider card abilities carefully in order to master the game.

When the game ends, players add all their points together to judge the fate of all they've built. Players must play wisely, as both divine and calamity cards can cause the game to end. Control of both points and when the game ends is critical to success.

Divinity is inspired by cooperative games heavy in strategy and planning, as well as light and chaotic party games. It's made to be quick to set up, easy to learn, but full of endless wonder. Why not try being a god today?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
2-6 Players build up defenses and toss Zambies around as weapons. Turn all your enemies into Zambie Bosses and be the last Player alive to win.

A simple game, quick to learn and quick to play. The more people you can get to play the more crazy the game becomes. The game revolves around drawing cards that consist of Zambies and Defenses. You use these cards to turn the game in your favor by blocking incoming Zambies, building up your enemies Zambies pools and once maxed, turning them into Zambies Bosses.

The Game doesn't end when you lose, Oh no, there is much more. When your Zambie pool is maxed out at 15, you collect the Zambies in your pool, and now you get to throw those Zambies at other players.

Zambies is a fast paced card game and can take 5 - 30 minutes to play.

Micro Quest is an adventure micro-game consisting of only 30 high quality cards and 30 custom meeples.  Players will utilize the Market Economy and Quest Cards to gather the kings forces of highly trained Knights, Thieves, Clerics, and Mages.

Your land is under siege and the king has commanded you and your compatriots to acquire, train, and supply battle ready troops to support the war effort.

In Micro Quest, players compete for the king's favor (aka: victory points) by managing, training, and paying tribute with troops to support the king's army. 
 The game play consists of 3 phases:

1. Market Phase - players vie for control over the available units.

2. Quest Phase - players train their units by completing epic quests, activating powerful skills, and obtaining glory.

3. Tribute Phase - players compete in an all-out battle for the 'King's Favor' and must be careful managing units in order to pay proper tribute to the king.

boardgame: Question Quest: The Language Card Game
description:
From publisher:

Welcome to the world of Question Quest! It's a wondrous place where language learning happens amid adventure, excitement, and strategic gameplay.

Each turn players compete for Crystal Points by asking and answering questions in conversational English. But things get wild when Special Cards like "What's your answer?" and "Tell me more!" enter the mix! The Crystal Rush is on!

Question Quest is a card game for 2-6 players, playable in 20-40 minutes.

Players take turns asking each other questions in English. Players score points by answering questions correctly or by asking questions that their opponents answer incorrectly.

The game ends when the last card is drawn or at the end of the allotted time. The player with the most points is the winner!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
480 questions (96 cards) are used to become more familiar with the state/region/territory you are living in (Groningen). A die determines the category (5):
language & history; culture & cohabitation; economy & corporate activities; nature, territory & environment; sport & tourism. If you can give a correct answer you get the card; on the back of each card you see a sixth of a map; with a bit of luck you can find matching ones - winner is the first to gather all six parts of the map.

480 questions are used to become more familiar with the state/region/territory you are living in (Friesland). A die determines the category (5):
language & history; culture & cohabitation; economy & corporate activities; nature, territory & environment; sport & tourism. If you can give a correct answer you get the cart; on the back of each cart you see a sixth of a map; with a bit of luck you can find matching ones - winner is the first to gather all six parts of the map.

Objective: be the first to get the permanent majority of votes for the coming election of the mayor - learn more about the city of Potsdam
- moving/acting by turns.

Each player rolls the die, moves according the number, follows instructions of space landed on; buys, if possible and/or (preferable) votes.

"Chance" cards are created with circumstances out of the history of Potsdam. Correct answers give economic advantages.

Voting process about the next mayor: Each owned company corresponds to a number of votes. The results of each voting are kept and the final winner is the first player who reaches the target.

Objective: be the first to get the permanent majority of votes for the coming election of the mayor - learn more about the city of Wolfsburg
- moving/acting by turns.

Each player rolls the die, moves according the number, follows instructions of space landed on; buys, if possible and/or (preferable) votes.

"Chance" cards are created with circumstances out of the history of Wolfsburg. Correct answers give economic advantages.

Voting process about the next mayor: Each owned company corresponds to a number of votes. The results of each voting are kept and the final winner is the first player who reaches the target.

The Nebula is a cosmic cloud of dust and gas. The stars in the sector are too young to support colonizable planets, yet for brave explorers the Nebula offers powerful Discoveries and a perfect hiding place...

The expansion contains two Nebula hexes, one Ancient Monolith Discovery Tile and one Development / Rare Technology Board (on the back side of the rules sheet).

The two Nebula hexes are shuffled with the other Sector hexes before building the Middle and Outer Sector stacks. The Discovery Tile is shuffled with the other Discovery Tiles.

The Nebula is divided into three subsectors, connected by Wormholes. For Move, Explore and Influence rules purposes, each subsector functions exactly like a normal hex. When you Explore and place the Nebula, place one Discovery tile in each subsector with an Discovery symbol and one Ancient Ship in the subsector with the Ancient symbol. You cannot place an Influence Disc on the hex. The Discovery Tiles can only be claimed by having a Ship in the subsector at the end of the Combat Phase. If you place a Discovery Tile benefit in the Nebula, you must place it in the subsector where it was found.

When you collect the Ancient Monolith tile, place it in the hex where you found it. It functions just like a normal Monolith.

Objective: be the first to get the permanent majority of votes for the coming election of the mayor - learn more about the city of Lüneburg
- moving/acting by turns.

Each player rolls the die, moves according the number, follows instructions of space landed on; buys, if possible and/or (preferable) votes.

"Chance" cards are created with circumstances out of the history of Lüneburg. Correct answers give economic advantages.

Voting process about the next mayor: Each owned company corresponds to a number of votes. The results of each voting are kept and the final winner is the first player who reaches the target.

Objective: be the first to pass finish line 
learn more about flying a glider

- moving by turns/simple 'Roll and Move' game -

Meeples are moved according the die roll;
ending on a red space (20x) follow instructions..

Lepelstronten: Het Bordspel is the board game developed for the 8th anniversary of gaming club Stobbeldeen.

Using dice, players try to move their pawn to a central target as soon as possible. As soon as one of the players succeeds, the target moves, forcing the other players to change direction and try to reach the new location. During the first phase of the game, users can, instead of rolling the dice, choose to buy cards, which can be used during the 2nd phase to move faster or block other players.

The player who doesn't reach the target in time will be penalized. After a predefined number of rounds, the player who has the least penalty wins the game.

Hastings 1066AD is a wargame simulation of the battle fought in 1066AD to determine the future King of England. One player plays the Norman forces under William the Bastard, while the other plays the Saxon forces under King Harold.

Each turn simulates one hour of time, and each unit represents 300-350 infantrymen or Norman knights.  One hex is about 50 meters across.

Contents:

    One 11x17” hard-mounted game map
    One sheet of 88 5/8” game counters 
    Two six-sided die
    Rulebook
family: Series: Twenty Decisive Battles of the World (TPS)

boardgame: The First Battle of the Marne, 1914 AD
description:
The First Battle of the Marne, 1914 AD is an operational level game. 
This September battle changed the course of the war. One player (or team) controls the Allies (France and Britain), while the other controls Germany.

Contains:

    11x17" hard-mounted map
    Playing pieces
    Rulebook
    Player Aid Card

family: Series: Twenty Decisive Battles of the World (TPS)

description:
You and your fellow explorers have discovered the underground city of Agartha!...and its fabulous gems!  Unfortunately, removing the gems has disrupted the integrity of the area, and now the whole city threatens to collapse around you!  Your only hope is to work your way through the city and figure out which gems act as the keys to stop the collapse.

But be careful!  The more you move throughout the city, the faster it weakens, and the sooner it will collapse...

...and if that wasn't enough!  The gems are all cursed!  If the you're skilled enough to survive, be sure you have the least cursed gems...or else the consequences will be swift and painful!!!


Players use deductive reasoning to establish which gems were removed from play at the beginning of the game.  This is accomplished by strategically moving along a play area composed of tiles that the players may rotate, push, and move between.  Rotating and pushing tiles speeds up the end of the game, so it's best to be judicious in how often you do so.

A cooperative game, if the hidden gems are not identified by game's end, then there is no winner.  However, if the group does solve the mystery, then the player in possession of the least cursed gems is victorious!

The kids game that matches delicious foods, and well... Matching.

Kids flip over one card at a time, looking to match type of pretzel, and flavor of pretzel. However, also in the cards are bonuses, that act like a match over-rider, for that extra edge against other players.

Game play lasts around 5 minutes, and teaches children to notice minor details.

In two player, the person with the most matches wins!

The objective of this car race is to be the first player to reach the finish line crossing 100 "steps". The movement of the cars is determined by a number die. On certain spaces on the track a color die is used to decide, if the result of the number die can be used or if the player has to skip the turn.

Published in 1920's-1930's.

Papegaaien is a party game where all competitors answer questions simultaneously and get points whenever more than one player gives the same answer.

One round goes as follows:
- a player draws a card containing 7 questions on a subject. These questions usually read: "Name a x that has attribute y"
- the player reads the questions one by one, each time awaiting the other players answers.
- the other players answer the questions out loud. Each time 2 or more players give the same answer at the same moment, those players score a point. The question asking player judges the simultaneous-ness of the answers.

A session exists of 6 rounds, and in each round each player asks questions once. In the end the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: House of Cards Pack
description:
Someone in the Netflix marketing department had an epiphany: House of Cards and Cards Against Humanity both contain the word "cards." When we got a phone call from Netflix, we enthusiastically agreed that the two products indeed contain the word "cards." One month and many brainstorming sessions later, we completed the House of Cards Pack: 25 brand-new cards about power, politics, passive-aggressive handjobs, and other scenes that we can never unsee.  - Cards Against Humanity

The expansion packs include Mad Libs-style quotes from House of Cards and ribs against Netflix itself, while the cards for filling in those blanks include the type of over-the-top inappropriateness you'd expect from the game — this time with just a bit of a political twist.

Though Netflix and Cards Against Humanity were initially giving out printed sets of the expansion pack for free, they ran out of stock after just two hours. However, you can print out a PDF of all the cards for free.

Wuzzits is a super silly matching game where your goal is to find and assemble the Wuzzit monsters with the most points by drawing, discarding and trading cards with other players.

Featuring wild and wacky art that kids absolutely love. Each of the 62 cards is either a top or bottom half of one of the 30 different Wuzzits. The first player to correctly assemble 3 complete Wuzzits (top & bottom) ends the round and then players add up their points based on how many monsters they were able to complete.

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Wrath of the Cosmos
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The far-reaches of space have converged on the earth! In Sentinels of the Multiverse: Wrath of the Cosmos, heroes must join forces to defend the world from threats from beyond our galaxy. This expansion includes two cosmic heroes, four destructive villains, and two far-flung environments.

The Cosmos are leaving their mark on the Multiverse! Do you have what it takes to save the earth from the impending annihilation? Stand and fight for your planet!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The King is old.  The kingdom is divided and the great Wizard Morcai is mustering an army atop Fire Castle.  From every town in the land the call goes out for heroes to take up the quest for the Five Weapons of Power.  Ancient artifacts of immense power capable of giving their wielder the power to be...king!  You must heed this call but your quest will not be easy.  You must leave your home with only a small supply of modest provisions and brave the Overland's dangers as you seek news and information of the Five Weapons of Power.  Along the way you will brave harsh weather, get lost in unfamiliar terrain, encounter powerful and sometimes hostile strangers, enter new towns, visit great castles, make loyal friends and do deeds that will make you a legend.   And only if you survive all that will you be worthy to vie for one of the Five Weapons of Power.

Journey To The Overland is a solo tabletop role playing game where you assume the role of a lone traveler journeying throughout the Overland in search of Five Weapons of Power.  Play begins in your hometown which will be determined by the race you choose for your character – human, dwarf, elf or native Overlander.  From there you will determine your character starting characteristics, starting equipment and first daily action.  Each day you must select a daily action that will either involve traveling, resting, working, training or visiting towns and castles to obtain news and information with the ultimate goal being to track down one of the five legendary weapons with the power to make you King!

No. Players: 1
Playing Time: 1 hour per session
Complexity: Moderate
Components: miniature figure, game map, character sheet, encounter cards, event cards, weather cards and dice;  (miniature figures and dice not provided).

Forsaken Lore, an expansion for Eldritch Horror, thrusts investigators headlong into perilous and strange new encounters, while Ancient Ones defeated in the past attempt to reclaim the world. In order to stop them, investigators will need to uncover new mysteries, and dig even deeper into the lore surrounding each of the game's Ancient Ones with the help of new Research cards.

Meanwhile, Yig's cultists and Serpent People work to prepare the way for the Father of Serpents to dominate our planet. With the help of powerful new Spells and Assets, along with mighty new Artifacts, Investigators must once again race across the globe in order to prevent humanity's destruction in Forsaken Lore.

description: L'Aquila e il Leone - La Battaglia di Vicenza - 7 ottobre 1513  is a wargame for two players that aims to recreate the battle that took place in the areas close to the city of Vicenza between the armies of the League of Cambrai and the Most Serene Republic of Venice. The players pull chits indicating which section of the battle field is activated, with a mechanism somewhat reminiscent of the Series: Commands & Colors system. The units are moved on a game map divided into squares and depicting the battle field, and combat is resolved through the roll of 2 six sided dice. A secondary map provides the Venetian armies with an alternative path through the marshes, allowing them to outflank the opponents. The winner is the player that controls two of the three sections of the battle field.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: History: War of the League of Cambrai

description:
The players choose one each of the 6 pawns and start at the snail's shell in the center of the game board. The game is played with 1 color die.

A player who roll his own color, begins the game by rolling again and moving on the spiral. On the roll of any color, the pawn advances to the next space of that color. If a player rolls his own color, he moves and may then roll and move again.
When a pawn arrives on an occupied space, it throws the other pawn out, back to the snail's shell where it must start again.

From the last spaces on the spiral, a pawn advances to one of the 6 snail heads in their own color as soon as the player rolls that color. The first player to achieve this, wins the game.

A trivia game focusing on Austrian topography. Players draw cards describing towns, popular sights or landmarks, etc. and are then asked to pinpoint them on the game board which is a map of Austria. However, no place names are included on the map.

The map is divided into several zones of varying sizes. Players can try to gain more points by choosing a smaller (thus more concrete) zone, or play it safe, by only choosing a general area (larger zone) for the location in question.

Objective: score as many goals as possible within a given time or
eliminate all opponent's meeples (except goalkeeper)

- acting by turns -

Players try to score by flipping a smooth disc into opponent's goal
 - if a meeple (building a barrier) is hit remove it.

Notes:
Likely published early 1930's

boardgame: Durch Oberbayern und Tirol: Ein neues Reisespiel

Objective: Be the first moving up to field 83 (Munich/round trip)
learn more about Bavaria/Tirol
- moving by turns -
throw the die and move according rolled number; ending on any red marked space follow instructions.

remarks: game should had been published during 1938-40 (thats the only time artist became available)

succeeds Von München durch Oberbayern nach Tirol

Game description from the publisher:

After decades of exploration, disputes arise among the colonizing European nations over the discovered lands, resources and trade routes. Old-continent quarrels become new again in the colonies. At times, precarious moments of peace are maintained — until wars erupt again. Human greed is an eternal source of conflicts...

Archipelago: War & Peace introduces forty new beautifully- and individually-illustrated evolution cards: twenty character and twenty progress cards. These cards — which can be combined with the base game or used as a complete replacement — are more aggressive, interactive and (sometimes) drastic than the original ones, covering the major themes of war, peace, and trade, among others.

With Archipelago: War & Peace, you can add spice to your games and make Archipelago new again!

Contents:

     40 Cards
     1 Card Tray (fits up to 90 sleeved evolution cards)
     1 Rulebook

description: FurCon is the first official Room Party Expansion Pack! The deck features 55 New Cards, all with a furry-convention theme! With 22 new Events, 19 new People, and 14 new Items, watch the fur fly as these parties are about to get a little wild!

Objective: Be the first moving up to field 60 (start: Berlin - finish: Hamburg)

- moving by turns -

Throw the die and move according rolled number; ending on any red marked number follow instructions.

Notes: 
Game reflects Nazi ideology at that time
Likely published 1939/40

boardgame: Through the Locks to the Golden Gate
description: A spin and move game for two players representing ships journeying through the Panama Canal, from the Caribbean Sea to the Pacific Ocean (the Golden Gate being the strait, not the bridge which wasn't built until two decades later).

A game for two players, similar to Chess, but the board is bigger, and there are also a lot more pieces.

Components:
One 13 X 12 squares board(totally 156 squares)
74 pieces in two colours(37 pieces each side)

Pieces are divided in three groups:
- Infantry
- Cavalry and
- Archers

Every player has the following pieces:

- INFANTRY UNITS - 
1 Leader
9 Heavy Infantry
11 Light Infantry
   
                    - CAVALRY UNITS -
4 Heavy Cavalry
4 Light Cavalry


- ARCHERS -
4 Infantry Archers
4 Horse Archers
   
The object of game is to capture the enemy Leader. If the Leaders are the only pieces left on board it's a stalemate and the game is ended a draw. If one player cannot move any piece, or cannot move Leader to a safe square, that player loses the game.
Players play one after the other like in chess(black goes first), but they can play not just one, but also two (and in special condition three) moves per turn.

Each player on his turn can move any two pieces he has (but cannot move the same piece twice like a prolonged move). If he plays with a two units of a different type (e.g. one Infantry and one Archer), he can move even the third piece but that piece must belong to remaining unit (e.g. If a player moved one Infantry and one Cavalry, he can also move one Archer).

Player cannot move and take pieces on the same turn. When taking pieces player can do it with only one unit (and so he loses maneuver advantage of playing with more pieces). The only way to take two pieces on the same turn is that you take one piece with either Infantry or Cavalry, and if there was any piece within range of your Archers, you can take that piece as well.

Leader and Heavy Infantry are Invulnerable to Archers, but they cannot move on the square which is targeted by Archer.

The Supreme Glorious Leader needs to be pleasured and players, as managers of Leader's pleasure teams, must provide Dear Leader with the entertainment he requires.

Dear Leader is a quick tile game in which players score points by providing Dear Leader with the entertainers he desires, but if Leader is not in the mood for the entertainers you provide, there will be executions!

Players begin each turn by taking turns drafting entertainer tiles  from a central market. Using these entertainer tiles, each player creates a team of entertainers and 'sends it off' to Dear Leader. At the end of each turn, Dear Leader selects one of the 5 types of entertainers (Parades, Cinema, Dancers, Clowns, Libation). The entertainers of the type that Leader selected are scored (placed in a score pile) and the entertainers of the incorrect types are executed. Each of the 5 different entertainer types has a special ability and players can sacrifice them to affect scoring or Dear Leader's decision making.

Game ends when there are no more entertainers. Players score a point for each tile in their score pile and bonus points for having many of the same tile type in the score pile.

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes: The Card Game – Designer Edition
description:
You are the detective. Play your cards. Solve the crime.

Sherlock Holmes: The Card Game was originally published in Europe and sold out very quickly. Now, for the first time, this classic game will be printed and available in North America.

Excalibre Games is proud to release a Designer edition of this game with the highest quality components and enhanced artwork. Included are two decks of linen cards. Rules are in full 4-color and printed on thick gloss stock. The score pad is also on gloss stock. The box is specially UV coated both top and bottom.

This Designer edition contains new cards with capabilities not found in the original game, yielding very different game play from the earlier edition. In addition, there are also promotional cards which are available separately.

Overview

3-8 players play detectives trying to arrest a villain. Secretly, one of the players is the villain, and the other players must try to deduce who it is and play an Arrest card to catch them. The villain's goal is to use all of their cards, and then discard their Villain card to escape. Players earn points for either making and arrest, or escaping as the Villain.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Aye, Dark Overlord! The Red Box is a fantasy party game filled with humor. It is fast to play and easy to learn.

Each player is an evil goblin servant of the evil Dark Overlord (Rigor Mortis, the Master of all Evils), and all of them are gathered at the Dark Overlord's knees to explain why they brought that last important mission, ordered by 'His evil Excellence' himself, to failure again. Panic wraps you in its freezing grip, what can you do to save your poor skin? Could you say you are not the one to blame, but your inept companions?

Yes of course, that's the solution! There is only one little problem: they've probably got exactly the same idea... and the Dark Overlord is not known for his mercy...

This roleplaying game is all about telling lies and shifting the blame on your poor fellow ones, so the righteous Wrath of your Master can give them what they deserve!

The expansion brings you new cards with places to explore, people to meet and treasures to retrieve. And that's not all... You, the goblins, will have new actions to make even more difficult your fellow party to escape the right Wrath of the Dark Overlord.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The word game Wurf & Wort challenges you to think in syllables instead of letters.

On a turn, a category card is revealed, e.g., "on the beach" or "at the airport"; each card shows a points multiplier and a few dice icons. Someone rolls these dice, then everyone tries to be the first to come up with a word that fits the category and falls within the restriction of the dice (as not all dice will be rolled each time). The number on the syllable die indicates how many syllables the word must be; the forbidden die shows which letter cannot be used in the answer; and the two letter dice show a letter that should be used in the answer along with a point value.

Whoever comes up with a valid word first scores the sum of the letter values for each appropriate letter in her answer times the points multiplier on the card, up to a maximum of ten points. If the answerer chooses an inappropriate word — or a majority of players vote down the word for being off-category — the answerer loses three points. After all 25 category cards have been played, the player with the most points wins.

The game of smarm, swag, and popped collars.

You are a freshman in college. You want to get into the most Bro fraternity on campus, but how can you show up the other pledges?

Popped collars bro. Be the first person to sport a killer ten layered pop collars, and they'll have to let you into the fraternity over the other posers!

When you start the game draw five cards. On your turn you draw one card. You then can play one card, or attack once per turn.

You have slots for two hands, one chest, one head and your feet, and can accessorize each of those items. That's 5 slots. You can not wear more accessories then you have slots, unless a card says otherwise.

You win the game when you have 10 popped collars worn.

An LPS bonus mini-game, Shōgi is an expansion for Operation Cartwheel.

The game covers Japanese and American carrier operations in late 1943, and includes new counters, rules, displays, and a new map.

This issue of Captain's Log includes the following exciting features:

The First Round, an exciting science fiction story by Stephen Kacarides. A related scenario is included and may be played by four players.

The Life and Times of Deth O'Kay, a special group of scenarios.

Ground Combat is portrayed in a stylized set of game rules. Several scenarios are provided.

The Consolidated Addenda: A compendium of three years of rules updates, expansions, and changes.

The SFB Rules Cross-Index: Provides quick access to the rules system.

Term Papers: The latest tactics from top starship commanders.

New Scenarios

boardgame: The Victory of Arminius: Teutoburg Forest, IX AD
description:
The Victory of Arminius is a tactical game for two players that depicts one of the most famous battles of the Roman Empire, the action that stopped the empire’s expansion into Germany in A.D. 9, when three Roman Legions were destroyed in fighting with the German tribes. The players take sides, one commanding the Germans, the other the Romans.

Each hex is about fifty meters across, and game turns represent about forty-five minutes of fighting. Counters in the game represent “units,” the combat formations of both sides, and markers used to denote various game functions or conditions. Unlike many other simulations, The Victory of Arminius has no “Combat Results Table.” Rather, the battle function is adjudicated entirely with dice.

Contains:

    Mounted, full-color Board 
    Rules 
    ~140 counters
    Aid Card

boardgame: The Valmy Campaign: The Revolution Saved, 1792AD
description:
The fourteenth game in Turning Point Simulations' coverage of Mitchell and Creasy' Twenty Decisive Battles of the World.

The game covers the campaign leading up to the critical battle at Valmy, where the French won their first victory of the French Revolutionary Wars, opening the door for all that was to come.

description: Pawn Voyage is a race game where family and friends battle each other for their place in paradise.  Stranded and alone in a life raft, players must stay afloat while attempting to get all their pawns to paradise before anyone else does.  It won't be easy though, the other players will be doing all they can to Trade Places with you, Challenge you for your position or sending you flying across the board to a random location.  You will find extra help along the way with Additional Turns, Shortcuts and a Mega Roll to get you into paradise even quicker.

"3rd Millennium Chess does for chess what Christopher Columbus did for the world." - Shannon Rene Gardner, designer

3rd Millennium Chess, AKA 3MChess, is a chess variant played on a wrap-around cylindrical 8 x 14 board. There are two other differences from conventional chess:

1. Each player begins with a row of eight pawns on each side of the home rank. Pawns move away from the home rank.

2. Each player also begins with four knights. A knight can share a square with an allied knight, and a knight can carry an allied knight.

Pentominoes was described by Solomon W. Golomb in a talk to the Harvard Mathematics Club in 1953, which was later published in American Mathematical Monthly and in Scientific American.  Pentomino puzzles had been described before, but it was Golomb who coined the name and described a game for one to three players.  Others have published their own variations, but the game described by Golomb consisted of an 8 x 8 grid of squares and a set of twelve pentominoes.  Players take turns placing the pentominoes on the board, taking care not to overlap the pieces or to use a tile more than once.  The last player to place a pentomino on the board wins the game.

Note: A separate database page exists for the 10 x 10 grid version of Pentominoes, as published by Taskmaster Ltd.

boardgame: King of Tokyo: The Horde Promo Card
description:
card text:
"KEEP
At the end of your turn all monsters take 1 damage (including you), unless you pay 1 (energy)"

From Zombie 15' Kickstarter campaign

Our Fifth Sweetener - The Horde Promo Card for King of Tokyo

If you buy this card, it means that the Zombie Horde has been released on all of the giant monsters. Yes, those zombies are gnashing and gnawing at The King's hairy ankles, and scratching at Cyber Bunny's metallic legs. (Even the Kraken will be chewed on, believe it or not! He's chewy, and tastes fishy...)

Sure, the zombies are tiny compared to the giant monsters, but the zombies won't stop, and they are incredibly hungry! Someone told them how delicious Kaiju brains are, and now the zombie horde won't be satiated until they get to taste some. (That's an Alistair word, satiated. Blimey!)

Oh. Yeah. Sorry. I got carried away there.

So, you buy this card, and at the end of your turn, every turn, you either pay one energy cube, or the zombie horde bites every monster - including yours - and does one point of damage. If, however, you pay the energy cube, the zombies are mesmerized by the pretty glowing ball of energy, and forget to bite anybody - including you.

We think that this card is a lot of fun to play with, and will add a certain classiness to any King of Tokyo game. And, as we used to say back in my table top role-playing group, there is no situation so Dire! so Dangerous! so Desperate! that it can't be made just a little bit worse by the introduction of Flesh Eating Zombies!. (After a while, we shortened that sentence to FEZ!, and everybody knew what that meant!)

Part of the King of Tokyo series.

Objective: be the first to complete your card by collecting/filling in matching coloured chips (4)
 - acting by turns -
fill up the board: 4 x 6 coloured chips; put a chip on each corner
roll a 6d-coloured die and choose an appropriate starting position

Active player: rolls the die and chooses a direction; move until you reach an appropriate coloured space: take a chip (if there is any); if there is a smiling face: ask another player for a chip of his/her choice; sad face; give away a chip of your choice; joker: get a chip of your choice.

You loose if you have to take a fifth non-matching chip

boardgame: Wild West: Das neue Indianer und Cowboyspiel
description:
This game is for 2 players (indians & cowboys) or 2 teams with up to 3 players. After the die is cast, the meeple can move to the next space of the rolled Color in any direction. The meeples start in their camp (colored triangle) and try to get into the enemy camp. Whenever 2 opposing meeples meet on the same space, they have to fight. The outcome is determined by the color die (red or blue). The loser has been captured, but can later be rescued by his mates. When a Player gets all his meeples into the enemy camp, or captures all enemies, he wins the game

The game was published in the 1940s or 1950s.

Peeve Wars is a card game for 2 to 4 players and is appropriate for people of all ages.

The base deck contains 18 individual card designs mixed to make a 72-card playing deck. The goal of the game is to amass a peeve army you use to annoy your opponent(s) to death. You start with 3 cool points. Each time an opponent successfully annoys you, you lose a cool. When you completely lose your cool, you lose the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
featuring "Birth of a Feud!" A new short story about Gorns and Romulans with accompanying scenario titled.."A Mission of Vital Importance".

New Ships including,Tholian Dreadnoughts makeovers..

Database,Battle group assault the Holdfast,Dek plans of the Federation Express,Hydran vs Lyran "Brothers of the Anarchist"..

Scenario's include.,Klingon D3's find the Romlans SITTING BIRDS,Gorn and Romulan ships find ONE CORNER OF HELL,

F&E Fans will find new rules and rulings, their own Proposals Board and Why articles, the Ship Information Table for the new ships, and lots of tactics.

Damier géographique is French game from the WWI-era.

This game is a variation on the classical game of checkers, with a World War I theme.

2 teams of 2 players compete on a map of Europe that is overlaid with a checkerboard pattern. Each team has an equal number of pawns that represent armies. One team represents Germany and the Austrian Empire. The other team represents France, the UK, Russia and their allies.

The board is divided in a western and an eastern battle zone. At the beginning of the game, one player of each team plays in each zone. In the western zone, each player has 36 pieces. In the eastern zone each player has 24 pieces. The battles are played simultaneously, but separately (pawns cannot move from one battle zone to the other). When there is a victor in one zone, his remaining pieces go to his ally in the other zone.

The movement rules are those of the game of checkers. Each players moves in turn. Armies move one space diagonally and capture by jumping over an enemy pawn. Each country has one or two squares that are marked as fortresses. Armies promote to 'generals' when they can occupy an enemey fortress. A general moves as a king in checkers.

A battle in a zone is won by capturing all enemy pieces in that zone. The team that wins in both zones, or wins in one and draws in the other is the winner of the game.

boardgame: Die Siedler von Catan: Kartenspiel – Magier & Forscher
description:
This is a compilation of expansions for the Catan Card Game.

It contains:
- Handel & Wandel (Trade & Change)
- Wissenschaft & Fortschritt (Science & Progress)
- Zauberer & Drachen (Wizards & Dragons)

This game's subtitle (in German) reads "Themen-Sets für Das Kartenspiel für zwei Spieler"

boardgame: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire – Victors Game
description:
The fire has started -- You and the other rebels are here to see that it keeps burning all the way to The Capitol. Using different card combinations to score points, you must gather the most resources to best aid Katniss and the cause... But watch out! One of the other players may be a Capitol spy who will attempt to steal victory from you!

The Catching Fire Victor's Game is an easy-to-learn game of strategy and deduction as players look to maximize the cards that they play while also anticipating the strategies of the other players.

POT: The Card Game is a fast-paced, lively game full of frantic trading and bartering with other players. There are no "turns" in POT so you'll never have to wait your turn or forget who went last, etc.

During each round, trade and negotiate with other players to collect 7 grams (7 cards) of the same strain of cannabis to obtain the highest amount of points possible. Once all 7 grams are in your hand, yell “Holdin’!” For that round, you receive the number of points that strain is worth. If you are "Holdin'!" with the Piece Card, this doubles your points for that round. If you are left with the Seeds Card, this deducts 20 from your points that round.

The first player to reach 420 points total wins!

Called The Game of Moneta, or Money Makes Money. The box declares "By the author of Logomachy." The object of the game is to make the most money. Each of the 50 illustrated cards shows a coin; the denominations are: 1 cent, 2 cent, 3 cent, 5 cent, 10 cent, 20 cent, 25 cent, 50 cent, $1 (silver), $1 (gold), $2 1/2, $3, $5, and $10.

RoboDev is a card based game in which players develop a robot by releasing Features. They earn points by fulfilling Contracts, building a well rounded robot and releasing the most Features.

With so many combinations of 60 unique Features, you'll create a different robot every game. Release Features in pace with your competitors to maximize your points based on which Contracts you're aiming to fulfill.

Cops & Robbers is a game about stealing loot from the bank (Deck of cards) While you steal loot you need to get away from the cops. The more loot that you steal the more cops you have chasing you. Use dirty tricks to help you get away from the cops and use other dirty tricks to get other caught and sent to the slammer. Make the fastest car that is going to help you escape but beware your other robbers, if your car is too shiny they may want to steal it themselves.

The game is played in turns with players selecting loot and other cards to ensure they have the best chance of getting away and pulling nasty tricks on others, but when a player is trying to get away other players can affect whether the player makes a clean getaway during their turn.

There are 3 decks in the game. The Cop deck, which shows how many cops are chasing you. The Loot deck which is basically your score in the game. The Robber deck which is the main deck that helps you get away, stop others getting away and lets you pull dirty tricks on others.

Silhouette Pocket is played with a deck of cards with silhouette images.

Two cards are drawn from the deck. The first player to come up with a word that matches both cards keeps them both, and two new cards are drawn. If there are a few cards where nobody can find an acceptable combination players are allowed to build combination with cards in their own stock.

When the deck is exhausted the player with the most cards wins.

Notes:
Can be combined with other 'Silhouette' editions; 
Compact design

Successful spies rely on information from the well-informed circles of the underworld. All data can be important - if not immediately, then perhaps later. Therefore the secret agents have to connect to important informants and meet them regularly. So they need a secret weekly schedule. Which Agent shall obtain his information on Monday? Which informant is to visit on Tuesday? And on which day should the meeting with the agent codenamed PURPLE take place? The player, who can crack this puzzle first, will be the winner and has really earned to be named "Top Agent"!

Objective: Conquer castles and fortresses along the Neckar river.

- acting/moving by turns -

Players roll the dice, move their pawn and try to conquer castles by finding correct answers for the questions about Knights and castles during the Middle Age.

First player occupied all castles wins the game (player elimination). Kids may play the game in a 'Roll & Move' modus without cards.

The game board features the following casles and fortresses:
Bad Wimpfen / Pfalz Wimpfen: German Wikipedia article
Burg Guttenberg: German Wikipedia article
Heidelberger Schloss: English Wikipedia article, Wikipedia article
Hornberg: English Wikipedia article, German Wikipedia article
Schadeck / Burgruine Schwalbennest: German Wikipedia article

Each player has a set of 4 meeples that start in his or her corner of the board. The goal is to move the meeples around the board to the "home" section. Movement is controlled by a die. All players may capture each others meeples. Captured meeples are returned to their player's corner and must start their journey over. The winner is the first player to move all meeples "home". Since the board is outlayed like an "8", the middle space is the most dangerous!

This game is a variant of Pachisi and was published in the 1930s or 1940s.

Ludusfidelis is inspired by artifacts from the Museu Municipal de Penafiel.  It is a game for two played an a rectangular board with a 2 x 5 grid of squares.  One row of squares is empty, the other marked with "coins."  Players have three pieces each of their own color or shape, and take turns rolling a die to determine how to move their pieces around the board in a counter-clockwise direction.  The six-sided die has the numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 4, and 5 on it.  The squares of the board are marked with roman numerals one through ten.  The goal is for a player to land all of their pieces on the squares marked with a coin.

Salto do Cavalo is a game for two played on a 5x5 grid of 25 squares.  Each player has a one piece in a different shape or color than the other player.  There are 23 neutral pieces that are also used.  Play begins with an empty board but for each player being stationed in an opposite corner of the board.  Players then take turns moving their piece like a Knight in Chess, and placing a neutral piece to mark where the player began their move.  Players may not move onto these neutral pieces.  The last player to make a move wins the game.

Suburbia: Hala Stulecia (The Centennial Hall) is a promo added to the Polish edition of Suburbia Inc., named after The Centennial Hall UNESCO Heritage building in Wrocław (where the Polish publisher comes from).

The Centennial Hall is a B-tile, which gives its owner +1 reputation per each adjacent tile (including lakes) but lowers your income by one. There are two of these tiles available. The expansion also includes two Redevelopment Planner tiles.

Hala Stulecia is four tiles in a 'punch-out' cardboard, outside of the box.

Objective: Eliminate your opponent/
 - moving/acting by turns -

Gameboard is your battlefield. You have several different military units with different qualities (range, power,...) at your disposal. 
1. Place your 25 units into the red/blue marked areal. 
2. Advance your units
3. Attack if enemy is within allowed range & vulnerable by your unit
4. Remove defeated units

Notes:
Likely published mid 1930's;
can be played also with more payers (even numbers/units are separated)

Meta is an abstract game for two played on a 4x4 grid of 16 squares.  Each player has four pieces of a different color or shape, one each labeled with one of these letters: M, E, T, A.  Play begins with each player's pieces lined next to each other in the board's middle two rows, spelling out M-E-T-A.  Players take turns moving their pieces to one empty adjacent square, vertically, horizontally, or diagonally.  The first player to move all of his pieces to the furthest row and spell M-E-T-A wins the game.  There are variations depending on how to start the game and whether to use the letters, and in which direction the letters should face.

Penim is a variant of Nim, played with five kinds of pieces: 11 small circles, 9 short rounded rectangles, 7 small squares, 5 long rounded rectangles, and 3 large squares.  The pieces are organized into heaps and the players take turns removing the pieces until none are left.  The last player to remove a piece wins the game.  The rules provide for varying degrees of difficulty, with different combinations of the pieces being used and different rules for who can take what piece.

The players start at the train station and try to get their car to the hotel (or reverse) as soon as possible. The first to arrive at his destination, wins the game.

The moves of the cars are determined by a die. When a player rolls a 6, he has to move the turntable under the game board one notch further with his steering wheel. This changes the traffic signs and the traffic lights. If a car arrives on an occupied space, an accident occurs and the arriving car has to head for the repair shop. Each player starts with 6 gas markers. For every 1 he rolls, he has to spend 1 marker. The markers can be regained at the gas station. If all markers are spend, the player has to withdraw from the game.

boardgame: Set Europe Ablaze: The Resistance War 1939-1945
description:
There were mixed feelings among the few people in the British government or military high command who heard of Churchill establishing the SOE -- Special Operations Executive HQ. While many approved of the idea of consolidating efforts that had been scattered among at least four small, unfunded departments, not everyone agreed with the whole idea.

Churchill's directive, "Set Europe Ablaze," had grand sound to it, but many felt that the diversion of crack military personnel would be a waste -- Churchill's penchant for disastrous side-shows pre-dated Gallipoli -- while others wondered at the relative value and morality of arming civilians... something not recognized by the Geneva Convention. The fact that SOE took some of its ideas from the IRA's efforts in Ireland just emphasizes the shocking reminder that those we see as brave Resistance fighters might be viewed as "terrorists" through a different lens. Having seen how the Germans handled "francs-tireurs" in 1870 and 1914, none would suppose that these Germans would be less ruthless. But, faced with propaganda about the "Thousand Year Reich," the Allies felt compelled to create a "second front" of some sort, long before Normandy.

Set Europe Ablaze captures the spirit and complex choices involved in Occupied Western Europe. Award-winning designer John Prados, who last gave us Beyond Waterloo, again focuses on action in France, Belgium, and the Netherlands. Prados brings us a card-driven game pitting the Occupying Axis forces against the combination of Allied intelligence efforts and brave local citizens, fighting the shadow war that made "the Maquis" a name to remember proudly.

Unlike many board strategy games, the focus is not on movement and combat by military units but on political-economic strategy. The Axis player wants to extract as much raw materials and finished goods as possible, to aid the war effort, and the Allied player seeks to avert this. Allied tactics include sabotage, interdiction, and intelligence gathering.

Axis responses include partisan sweeps, counterintelligence operations, and labor pool mobilization to repair damage. Turns abstractly represent about six months each, with rounds within each turn varying according to cards played. While there is no "combat" in a literal sense, there are competitions for resolving actions that have both players rolling dice. Excitement levels will be high, as players wonder what the next card will bring.

Allies will take advantage of infiltration and special units (SAS, Jedburgh, OSS) while the Axis will use Security forces, the Abwehr, Gestapo, and even Vichy police. "Barbie" competes with "Passy" and "Giskes" versus “Jean Moulin." There is even Stalag XVII.

Set Europe Ablaze and the 2014 ATO Magazine Annual

Maps - One full color 22"x34" mapsheet
Counters - 176 colorful die-cut large 5/8" pieces
Cards - 48
Rules length - 20 pages
Charts and tables - 8 pages
Complexity - Medium
Playing time - Up to 3 to 4 hours
How challenging is it solitaire? - Poor

Design - John Prados
Development - Lembit Tohver
Graphic Design - Mark Mahaffey

(from publisher's website)


(Note: Read more about the game's development in Prados' article in ATO No. 38, Guns of the Askari.)

Apostles is a non-denominational Christian-themed card game in which players represent ministers working to edify the Christian church.

Through the management of spiritual gifts and fruit, his people become equipped for works of service, so that the body of Christ may be built up. In the game gifts, fruit, mission and people are represented by cards which, once established yield spiritual blessings (cards dealt at the beginning of each round), and/or souls reached, which accumulate as points for scoring at the end of the game.

The game consists of 135 cards, out of which a hand of 9 is dealt to each player. Players use the cards to establish gifts, fruit, mission or workers (by discarding cards from his hand),  or save for later rounds. Gifts nurture and encourage good fruit in the church, which in turn manifest themselves in the form of mission.

As events affect the development of the church, players struggle to establish the most thriving ministry.

You can follow the game design thread here: 
WIP, Apostles, Christian card game

The players move their cars according to their dice rolls on the road from the start near Vienna to Schwechat. When they arrive in a city, the players have to answer a question about this city. For the right answer they get a game coin. The Player who gets the most coins wins the game. The first player to reach Schwechat gets the 3rd place, if he hasn't enough coins to get the 1st or 2nd place.

This game was published in the 1950s or 1960s.

From the designer's website (used with permission):

Aatheuo (pronounced like "off-uh-you") is a tabletop game that was inspired from my love of Uno, Scrabble, and Rummy. 2 to 4 players take turns placing tiles from their hand onto the board, creating sequences and sets or three or more tiles. You win if you are the first player to have no tiles remaining. With more than 2 players, continue playing to determine runner-ups.

The unique beauty of Aatheuo comes from the spatial-reasoning involved in re-arranging the board in any way, as long as all of the basic properties of the sequences and sets are maintained.

Aatheuo was chosen as an Official Selection in IndieCade 2012, in addition to being featured as a night game at IndieCade. There are no concrete plans for bulk physical manufacturing of Aatheuo sets, but a digital version is in pre-production.

The players start at the airfield or the train station. Each player gets some game coins and draws 2 destination cards. These destinations have to be visited on the way to the starting spaces of the opposite players. Every time a 6 is rolled, the traffic cop in the centre of the game board is rotated to change the traffic lights. If a player disobeyes a traffic rule, he has to pay a game coin. The first player to reach the finish space wins the game. The second winner is the one with the most game coins.

This game was published in the 1950s

Each player gets a train consisting of an engine and 3 cars. The players start at the station of their color and have to visit all other stations, where they leave a car on a sidetrack. Then they have to get the engine back to their home Station to win the game. When the player rolls a 1 or 4, he may adjust one of the two railroad switches. This is important because other trains can only be overtaken on parallel tracks.

This game was published in the 1930s or 1940s.

From the game's rules:

"ADD-TOO is a game where the player places his diamond next to a diamond already on the table.

There are 45 diamonds, each of which is colored orange and blue, with a number on each color anywhere from one to ten.  Colors always must be matched, but numbers can either be matched or played to make scoring combinations.

The object is to play all diamonds from the hand as quickly as possible in either matching or scoring combinations.  Players left with diamonds suffer penalties.  Each player, therefore, tries (1) to play out all diamonds from his hand as quickly as possible and (2) to make as many scoring combinations as he can.

Hands are played until a game of 200 points, or some other predetermined count is reached."

Bumblings: the funtasy card game is an entertaining card game featuring comic fantasy creatures, funny names, great illustrations and and a lot of unexpected events during the exciting battles.

This is a 2-4 player duelling game that's played by using the player's own deck. This deck can be a Starter deck, which is ready to play with, or players can build their own deck from Bumblings cards. There are Common (C), Uncommon (UC) and Rare cards (R) by their rarity. Cards in Starter decks have a special rarity (STARTER), they cannot be found in other boosters.

Players are dealt five "harmless word" cards while one player (the teller) is chosen to read an "innocent story".  The teller will be using a book mark to let the other players know what kind of card (black noun and adjective or red verb and adverb) to play.  The players will then play one of their cards, hoping to play the ones with the highest points as the player with the lowest score wins.  By playing their cards, they assist the teller in telling an innocent story that turns dirty with the harmless word cards.

2 different games:
objective 1: gain as many cards as possible delivering correct answers (trivia)
objective 2: gain as many cards as possible; Happy Families approach
1 & 2: learn more about the local region (Forchheim - in the North of Nuremberg)
 - acting simultaneously - 
active player reads the question; first player with correct answer gets the card.

Notes:
Belongs to a series of games with a strong regional (rural) focused character (contrary to 'city games');
Usually each village of the administrative district is described.

Yūgen (yoo-gehn) is a fast, semi-casual game for 2 players, with each game about 20 to 30 minutes long. There's a bit of strategy and a bit of luck involved.

The players take turns drawing and placing tiles, triggering "pushes" and "pulls" on the old tiles, thus moving 3 "philosopher" figures along the board. The main goal is to push 2 philosophers out through your borders, but final scoring can be triggered in several ways.

boardgame: Legend: History of 1000 Miglia – 1928 Chrysler Type 72
description:
This promo adds the 1928 Chrysler Type 72 to the game Legend: History of 1000 Miglia.

Comes with the automobile data sheet, a pawn and a short history.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
InEPTT is a skirmish game that runs from 1890 thru to 2020. It pits small elite teams of "Agents" against the evils and aliens of this world. The monsters are normally referred to as creatures and the Aliens as "XRAYs". InEPTT is built with three basic Genres of play 1890 to 1930, 1930 to 1960 and 1960 to 2020.

These genres have various weapons armouries with which you can build your teams to take on the Creatures and XRAY's that you will face. The game uses the term Mission for a single scenario and Operation for a series of linked Missions. You can build your team as you go as you collect credits with successful mission completion and "kills" of Creatures and XRAYs. The game is run by a Game Master (GM) who designs and runs the missions and or operations and the scope is massive!

There are two "Missions" in the core rules but those missions lend themselves to quick development into literally hundreds of missions with minor tweaks to terrain, victory conditions and forces involved. Some of the key points for InEPTT from a mini/terrain view point is – there are no specific miniatures that you "must" buy. You can use just about anything you want really, the one stipulation being that Allies and Henchmen should be a What You See is What You Get concept, but for your Agents (term for InEPTT Soldiers) you can use any figure you like. Also the teams range from 3 to 12 so you don't need hordes of figures although your XRAYs and Creatures will often need to out number Agents by as many as 3 to 1 and for zombies 5 to 1 or more – but that is up to the GM. Also the games usually run from 30 minutes (small 4 x 4 table) to 4 hours for massive 10 x 6 table with 5 teams and over 40 bad guys, with all teams having separate Mission Orders to complicate the system even more. The game has huge amounts of growth still and there is depth in every category from which to develop more and more.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Paddington Photo is a roll and move game with a twist. The game is a small box game. It consists of a 3-piece (puzzle type) game board, 3 cardboard figures (Paddington, Mr. and Mrs. Brown) and 25 plastic chips as well as 2 colour dice. The game board has spots in red, blue yellow etc. round in a circle. In game variation one all 3 figures are placed randomly on the board. On his turn a player rolls both dice. He/she assigns each roll to a figure on the board and moves two figures according to the die roll. E.g. the dice show red and yellow: then the player must move 2 of the three figures. One on any red space, one on any yellow space. When the three figures are located next to each other a photo is shot. The player achieving the photo shot gets a chip as reward. Once a photo is completed the middle figure runs away to the opposite space of the same colour. After 20 photos are shot the game ends, player with the most chips wins. In a variation a photo is especially well taken when Paddington is in the middle. Then the player gets 2 chips for this kind of photo shot. After all 25 chips are exhausted the game ends.
