description: Yaar! Anchors up! Here’s an exciting new card game that’s simple to learn and fun to play! Gather your crew of dastardly pirates to set sail before your opponents and win the game! Along the way you can sabotage their efforts with cannon fire, a bout of scurvy or incite mutiny in their midst! Convince more pirates to join your crew with spiced rum and treasure. Or even kidnap a crew member from a rival ship! If you have an abundance of Swabbies mopping the decks but not enough Gunners to man the cannons you can trade them across to a rival crew for someone more worthy of your ship. But beware you might not always get what you bargain for. After all, pirates are a deceitful lot!

An 8 card promo expansion for BabeQuest, given out for free at Viking-Con 22 in 2003.

The expansion consist of 8 cards:
Hunting Ground: Viking-Con
Move: Smuglervodka (Smuggler Booze)
H: Arrangør (Organizor)
Babe: Elverpigen (The Elven Chick)
Move: Scoresætning (Pick-up Line)
H/U: Indre Mission (Religious Group)
Countermove: Magic-nørder (Magic Nerds)
Gear: Guldterninger (Gold Dice)

Apart from adding more variety, these cards do not change the way the game is played.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – Ancestral Home
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Advance your claim to the Iron Throne with Ancestral Home, the fourth Chapter Pack in the "Conquest and Defiance" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game!

Introducing sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards), this Chapter Pack continues to support the Prized keyword, alongside the cycle's themes of multi-House decks and Neutral Faction decks. Whether you set sail with House Greyjoy, support the Prince of Dorne, or lend your strength to another Great House, you'll find plenty to enjoy in this expansion.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Find pirate’s treasure! We have gathered in a desert island of wrecked pirate ship to find treasure. To take more gold and treasure each other in frantic, pirates still remain on the island. Who could take the treasure most avoiding pirates? Good Luck~!

[Goal]
To get the most treasure tokens.

[Game Play]
The game contains 48 Gold cards, 4 Pirate cards and 1 Treasure Box card. In your turn, draw a card from top of the deck and give it to any player(including you). Rules to give a card are as follow.
1. You can give a card up to 7 times. (In case of 6~7 players, up to 5 times.)
2. Do not look a card when draw. Choose a player and then open a card in front of him/her. You can also choose yourself and open in front of you.
3. Give cards to at least 3 people including you before your turn ends. You can give to more than 3 people, but you should be included.
If a special card(Pirate or Treasure Box) appears during you are giving cards, your turn ends immediately. Pirate takes your treasure token and Treasure Box gives you the tokens taken.

[Game Ends]
The round ends when 4 pieces apper among the 5 special cards, and the player who has the most Golds gets treasure tokens. The game ends after 3 rounds.
Compare each other's treasure tokens, and decide the winner.

The stars have not been seen in the night sky for some time. Hereupon brave explorers sought the stars disappeared. You need to compete with other explorers and seek more constellations than them. Let’s go to seek quickly~ But, be careful not to collide with other spaceships!

[Goal]
Gather as many constellation cards as you can. The player who gets highest score wins.

[Game Play]
ASRUS: Zodiac is a ‘simultaneous action’ game, so there is no order to play. In each turn, every players play a card from hands. You should play face down not to show to others. After all players have played, open the cards at the same time.
1. Make the closest distance!
Only one player can get stars(cards = points). For this, you need to approach to the destination as close as possible. Basically who makes the closest distance will get stars - ‘Distance’ means the
gap(absolute value) between the number of your card and the destination.
2. Avoid collisions!
But notice, you should avoid the collision with other spaceships! Even though the closest distance, if several players have made same distances, they all collide.

[Game Ends & Scoring]
After several turns, when you used up all cards in hands, the game ends. Scoring is as below, and the player who gets highest score wins.
1. 1 point per a constellation card you acquired.

+  1 bonus point per a set of 4 colors (red, blue, green, and brown)

2. 2 points to the player who has the most energy (if tie, 1 point to each.)
3. Bonus points to the player who has the great explorer prize
But! The player whose spaceship has no energy cannot return from the exploration. 0 points!


    This game is second edition of ASRUS, it has the same basic system with ASRUS but there are spaceships and no glass stones - a little more strategic.

Adaptation of Monopoly as players travel through Houston, TX buying, selling, and trading local landmarks such as the Galleria, Pappadeaux, and the Houston Chronicle.

Objective: To become the wealthiest resident of Houston by buying, operating, and selling local businesses. Force all other players to go out of business and you are the winner!

King's Pouch is pouch-building strategy game set in a medieval kingdom. Each round offers tough choices on how to expand your pool of citizens and buildings. Using the resources you acquire, you may raise powerful armies or secure the support of powerful characters.

Will you be a peaceful protector of the royal family? Or maybe a warlord, taking what he wants by force? Or will you make a wrong step and fail to write your name in the annals of history? Take up the King's Pouch and find out!

After global nuclear war, the earth covered with thick nuclear dust couldn't be ideal for human or any life, and the situation become worse every year in spite of many efforts to recover it by survivors. The chosen scientists group of global survivors on the earth had ambitious plan to manage a big city to float in the sky above the nuclear dusted cloud. Eventually one city was successfully floated in the sky after many trial and errors, by new technology about utilizing nuclear energy and earth's magnetic field. That city was named as 'LAPUTA', according to the name of the project...

[Object]
On the game board, LAPUTA which consists of 7 areas is floated on the sky. The Sky is devided as 3 regions to defend LAPUTA efficiently. Players have to defeat the rebel fleet and protect LAPUTA. Distinguish yourself in battle and rise to the next Admiral.

[Game Play]
The game consists of 8 rounds, and each round has 3 phases as following: 
1.Placement of commanders: Commanders are actual executers who can deliver or perform your orders. Their roles are decided according to what place they put on.
2.Performing actions: Commanders on the board perform actions according to their places and roles. There are total 7 kinds of action according to 7 different places.
3.Enemy appearance and battle: Enemy fleets are emerging on the side of board and battle starts. There are 3 regions of sky(A,B,C) and battles can be happen on any region, sometimes multiple at the same time. Score evaluations are followed after each battle.

[Scoring]
After 8th round, sum up following scores:
- Current score(generally gained from battle)
- Fleet score
- Track & Commander score
- Mission card score

This is not a cooperative game, but you often need to cooperate with others at the battles for victory.

Tree frog brothers are going home. However, they want to eat flies rather than to go home where the mother is waiting at. She will get angry and not give yummy supper if the brothers are too late. Therefore, go home before she puts away food! Of course, the frogs have to eat flies enough on the load. Eating is our instinct. ;)

[Goal]
Despite of mom's call, it's more important to eat flies than go home. Because we are tree frogs! But if you are too late, you'll get a scolding from mom. So be careful. Eat flies as many as possible and escape penalty points, and then win this game between brothers.

[Play - Brief Rules]
This is a dexterity game by flipping the paper(origami) frog.
On the game board, there are circle target and moving track. Each round the players flip own frog to the target, and move his tokens on the track according to the result of the target. Players should compete with each other to get more stone tiles which have points on the track. For this, flipping the frog to proper target area is very important. But it won’t be easy because the terrible event cards are opened frequently. Flip and go, and eat flies(points)!

[Game Ends & Scoring]
Game ends when one of the following situations is happened.
1. When any player's game token arrives at a home(Mom) space on the track. 
2. When 'Mom card' is opened from Event Card deck.
In these cases, immediately game is over without the next round.
Scores are as follow: Fly points + 1.Home bonus points - 2.Penalty points for late (Depend on the above end situations.)

boardgame: HeroClix: The Hobbit – An Unexpected Journey Epic Campaign Starter Set
description:
Explore Middle-earth and enjoy a grand adventure with Thorin Oakenshield and Company! The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey comes alive on your tabletop with this excellent line of HeroClix mini-figures based on the blockbuster film.

Special powers and Epic Abilities based off of the movie will enhance your HeroClix experience as Bilbo Baggins and Gandalf the Grey are joined by both friend and foe from the classic storyline from J.R.R. Tolkein's The Hobbit book. 
 The Hobbit An Unexpected Journey HeroClix Campaign Starter Set contains 8 figures (including Gandalf the Grey, Bilbo Baggins and Thorin Oakenshield)(7 exclusive) , 48 Horde Tokens, 6 Maps, Epic-level Campaign scenarios, a 2012 HeroClix Rule book, a Powers and Abilities Card, Dice, Object tokens, and Terrain tokens offering a sneak-peek into the majesty and mystery of Middle-earth as seen in The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey feature film.

Play a scenario across a grid-based map with figures each with unique stats and abilities and defeat your opponents.

description:
Cosmos Trivia is a trivia game with 1380 easy and difficult questions. It contains 6 categories of questions: Culture, History, Sports, Sightseeings, Geography and General questions. Questions are applied to all the world's history, geography, etc.

It includes the following question types: multiple choice, enigma, rebus, tongue twisters and visual questions.

The game play includes moving around the board using a dice after each correctly answered question. At certain regions when a player or a team answers a question correctly, he aqcuires knowledge certificate for the corresponding categoty. The first player or team that collects all knowledge certificates wins the game.

Published ca. 2002-2005.

Hellas is the most complete trivia game regarding knowledge about Greece.

It's a trivia game with a variety of easy and difficult questions.

It includes 6 categories of questions: Culture, History, Sports,  Linguistics, Geography, and General questions.

It has the following question types: multiple choice, enigma, rebus,  tongue twisters, and visual questions.

All of them are regarding Greek History, Geography, etc.

Published ca. 2002-2005.

A competitive game with 600 questions about Greece and the whole wold focusing on Science.

The game play is simple: Each player rolls the dice and according to the outcome of the dice he is being asked the corresponding question. If he answers right, he collects the card.

The player or the team with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Each player gets 3 meeples, which he has to get across the board to his finish spaces, which are also the starting spaces of another player. In his turn each player can move 1 meeple 1 space in any direction. No meeple can jump over another, so they get blocked a lot. The colored spaces on the game board may only be occupied by meeples of the same color. The first player to get all 3 meeples to the finish spaces, wins the game.

The game was likely published in the 1930s.

bit Crawl Portable is the "take with you" version of Super Turbo bit Crawl FX: Alpha Xtreme, packing the punch of the full-size game in a smaller package.

bCP is a rogue-like dungeon crawler with the feel of a classic console RPG. Players select their hero, their special ability, and a boss that they will fight at the end of the game. Then players navigate the dungeon, fighting any monsters in their way, collecting treasure, and dealing with any events that might crop up along the way until the boss fight is triggered.

bCP is a co-op game with a competitive element. Players will find it difficult to navigate the dungeon alone but will be collecting treasures of varying value along the way, the choice on whether to share these with their party or hoard them for their own benefit at the end of the game is theirs alone. The person with the highest value of treasure when the boss is defeated or the heroes die at the hands of the boss is the winner.

bCP uses custom dice to resolve combat, trigger special abilities, and seed the dungeon. The game is played out through a series of rounds in which the players each take a turn consisting of two actions then the monsters take a turn fending off the heroes advances. This continues until the boss fight is triggered by a card located in the monster deck, at which time all heroes are warped to the room that the boss appears and fight desperately to best the boss of the dungeon.

With the varying player abilities and the random dungeon seeds bCP will not play the same way twice. Unlike other dungeon crawlers, bCP will not require a DM to guide the party along the way.

boardgame: Ace of Aces: Wingleader Deluxe Boxed Set
description:
The Wingleader Deluxe Boxed Set doubled the number of aircraft in the original version of the game and added rules for a campaign game. The Ace of Aces Collector's Guide, by Joseph J. Scoleri III, has this to say:

"The Wingleader Expansion Set was a special addition to games distributed by Chessex Manufacturing. The expansion was added at the time that Chessex acquired Nova's inventory. Dennis Greci estimated that there were approximately 500 of these sets.

A brief review of the Expansion Set shows that it is a "must have" for Wingleader players, if only for the twelve new aircraft cards (which add another 24 aircraft to the game.) Also included are campaign game rules, designed by Alfred Leonardi and Greci, which allow for large scale tournament games. There are also two suggested Speed Bonus rules for dealing with combat between aircraft of varying maximum airspeeds."

Expansion set aircraft include:

CA-12 Boomerang
Gloster Gladiator
Gloster Meteor
Beaufighter X
Spitfire IX
Typhoon
MS 406
MB 152 CL
D 520
ME-410
He-162A "Volksjager"
Fiat G-50
Re 2001
Ki-43 Hayabusa "Oscar"
Ki-44 Shoki "Tojo"
A6M5 Reisen "Zero"
P-39 Airacobra
P-47B Thunderbolt "Razorback"
P-51B Mustang
P-63 Kingcobra
F6F-5 Hellcat
I-16 Rata
Yak-9
LaGG-9

description:
trade·craft  (trād′krăft′) n. 
The methods used in clandestine operations such as espionage.

Knives in the dark. Secret plans. Information. Disinformation. What more could a spy ask for?

It's the height of the Cold War and you are a spy. You have a mission to accomplish and you would like to avoid being killed while doing it. And you want your side to win, of course. But who, exactly, is on your side?

Tradecraft is a hidden role card game where 3-6 players take on the roles of either Soviet or American spies during the Cold War era. Rather than using social deduction, players will have to carefully watch to see which cards are drafted and how they are played to decipher friend from foe. Players may never willingly show their role to another player, but may say whatever they want about it, even the truth if necessary. The game is played over the course of several rounds and can usually be finished in less than 30 minutes.

Each round has 3 phases:

1. Deal Cards - Roles and tradecraft cards will be shuffled and dealt randomly.  
2. Choose Cards - Players will pass a portion of their cards to the left 3 times to draft the cards they want.  
3. Play Cards - Cards are then executed in descending order, information flows, and bullets fly.

Scoring is based on the side winning the round (1 point) and completing a public or secret missions (usually 1 or 2 points).

description:
With the Heroes of Crystalia's constant incursions into his realm, Starfire has searched for ever more powerful relics for his hoard to increase his strength. With his discovery of the Horn of the Dragon King he has declared himself King of the Dragonback Peaks and Lord of all Dragons.

King Starfire is an all new resin sculpt of the classic Dungeon Boss. This version features an all new profile card with new deadly abilities; a new adventure card featuring the Cult of the Burning Star; and a powerful new magic item: The Dragon's Horn.

King's Favor is a semi-cooperative game for 4 - 12 players set in an aristocratic world.

The King is dying and has no living heir. You play a noble from one of the four great houses that jockey to succeed him. However, three powerful guilds each see a chance to build a new era. Many of the nobles hold secret positions within those guilds. These allegiances no doubt provide lucrative opportunities. The question to be answered is: will the nobles put family first, or strike out to make their own futures?

In this game, you play a noble with two conflicting allegiances: to your house and to your guild. Can you cooperate with either team long enough to secure victory?

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Crown of Destiny
description:
Gain access to a host of heroes and monsters in Crown of Destiny, a "Hero and Monster Collection" expansion for Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition). In addition to the heroes and monsters, Crown of Destiny offers two brand-new quests, inviting you to quest for a powerful artifact with the power to reshape your very future.

Crown of Destiny offers four noble heroes, ready to stand between the overlord and the people of Terrinoth: Corbin, Lindel, Jaes the Exile, and Brother Gherinn. These heroes are opposed by vile monsters, however – giants, chaos beasts, and lava beetles!

Crown of Destiny contains:


     Four heroes of Terrinoth, with detailed figures and character sheets
     Three complete monster groups with monster cards
     Two new quests: Burning Harvest and Crown of Destiny
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
Players spin the word roulette with 4 or 5 metal balls. Whatever letters the balls land become the letters the players must use to create their words.

All players are active at the same time seeking out the hidden words from the wheel. They must beat the clock. The object is to score the most points per word.

Points are awarded for using uncommon letters like "q" and "z" as well as for using all the letters available.

Each player spins for wild letters, bonus points, added score words and the chance to add a prefix or suffix for even more points.

Three levels with special features and options.

Patent-Mine is a roll and move educational trivia board game for kids/adults that teaches the patent process and little known facts about African American inventors.

Players move their pawns around the board, answering trivia questions about African-American inventors. When someone has a right answer, he may continue to advance on the board. The game continues until one reaches the end line (making a full turn of the board), winning at the same time.

Your quiet trip to Storm Crow Manor just got cut abruptly short. A deceased host, a crude instructional letter and the raging storm outside are just the start of your problems.

Welcome to Storm Crow Manor is a bluffing game where players take it upon themselves to kill or be killed in this classic murder plot. Your gracious host is dead and a letter is found instructing that a game is about to unfold.

Players awake one by one each night to secretly attack or heal one another in the night to attempt to kill the hiding murderers amongst them, beset to kill the guests one by one. If the guests don't harm anyone in the night with their item cards the murderers may have a free pass to harm whoever they like, if the guests do attack they risk making the wrong the choice.

A game of deduction and lying this is sure to please any horror or mystery fan who likes a longer game where everyone has a choice and the majority doesn't always win.

Viribus Unitis is a competition version of U.S. President game. It differs mainly in its map. Unlike U.S. President with its modified map of the USA, Viribus Unitis is played on modified language/ethnical map of Austria-Hungary around the year 1910.

(Viribus Unitis means "With united forces" in Latin and is known as personal motto of Austrian Emperor Franz Joseph I.)
 Like in the U.S. President game, Viribus Unitis is Easy-Fast-Fun game with pyramids about the last 12 weeks of fictional election campaign.

As far as the game mechanism is the same, tactics differ a bit, because in  Viribus Unitis all players put their pyramids simultaneously in all twelve rounds.

For competitions are available free pdf files with tables to write down all moves and follow the track easily.

The map for Viribus Unitis consists of ten main ethnic (language) groups of Austria-Hungary in 1910 and The City of Vienna as the eleventh district.
 There is no tricky district in Viribus Unitis (unlike Florida state in U.S. President game) and the game is meant for 3-6 players and 4 sets of pyramids.

Player need to secure at least 51 votes to win the game. In case of a tie, the one who secures votes in Vienna, wins the game.

Grand-Prix Elite  is a game about Formula 1 races (11 pages of rules).

The first car to pass through the finish line after 2 or 3 laps is the winner.

Grand-Prix Elite is played on tracks (size of gameboard = 40 cm by 60 cm, roughly). Tracks contains two types of spaces : straight section, or curve. The average length of a lap is between 40 spaces (fast tracks) and 60 spaces (long tracks). For the moment, there are 9 tracks : Monaco, Canada, Belgium, Japan, Italy, Catalunya, Singapore, China, Le mans.

Grand-prix Elite uses plastic racing cars (dimension = 27 mm long).
Six-sided dice are used for Wear tests, Skid tests, etc...
Optional rules include weather variation and Event cards.

HOW TO PLAY : each player has a certain number of Speed cards. In order to move his car he plays 1 Speed card. 
Then the Speed card goes to his dashboard : this is now the current speed of the car. 
Each turn, the player can increase or decrease this speed by 1 level. 
Also, extra movements are given by a Tyre-Setup card located in the dashboard. The Tyre-Setup card represents the strategy chosen for the car (soft, hard or rain tyres, aero grip, suspension, etc..)
Once moved, a car can engage another car in order to gain its position.

Each player faces 3 challenges in this game :
a) Damages. The number of Speed cards owned by the player is also the number of damages his car can absorb without consequences. Powerful Speed cards often leads to damages (due to engine wear or dangerous curve)...Some cars are fast but fragiles, so pit stops will be necessary in this case.
b) Curves. Each curve has an optimal speed. If a car's speed exceeds the optimal speed while taking the curve, the car can goes off track (and possibly taking damages). 
However, a driver has 3 choices when his car is about to take a curve : short, medium, or long trajectory. With a short trajectory, you go faster, but it's risky. With a long trajectory, you take the curve more easily, but you become an easy prey if engaged by opponents...
Here, tyre and set-up strategies are very important things for purpose of taking a curve.
c) Ghost cars. In addition to his normal car, each player controls one or several Ghost cars. Ghost cars follow their own rules, and symbolize the bulk of lapped cars driven by anonymous drivers. Their purpose is to annoy other players.

boardgame: PULL!: A Trap Shooting Card Game
description:
PULL!: A Trap Shooting Card Game is a partnership style card game that draws inspiration from classics such as spades, whist, and euchre.

Unlike in those traditional games, in PULL! there are two active tricks each round and the winner of each round does not get to lead the next round. At the start of each hand, the dealer deals 10 cards from the Shot Deck to each player and flips over the top two cards of the Target Deck.

Each Target is worth a number of points and players take turns playing a Shot card on each. The team that plays the highest valued Shot will win the Target and score the points, but you must play carefully, because shooting the wildlife might lose you points!

In addition, if a team wins both Targets, they might receive bonus points. A hand lasts five rounds; then points are tallied and recorded, and the deal passes to the left. Play continues in this fashion until one team scores a set number of points.

This is a set of rules intended to link existing board into one massive, yet playable war. Players can use 
   their own WW2 board games, using their own email to contact each other.

Players match up with those who own the same board games.

Any board game, including solitaire games can be used.

This is a FREE set of rules for download. Send me a PM for the links to the rules file booklets. (PDF)

Tactical, Operational, and Strategic games are fine.

Players can use Vassal or PBEM methods to play their games.
  
   Note this game is NOT played on BGG forums or using BGG email addresses.

Mike Raymond         Send me a PM for the links to the rules file booklets. (PDF)
   Author

description:
Family game for 2 to 8 players.

The aim of the game is to be the first who reaches the center of the board with one of your pawns.

Every player get 4 pawns in one color.
Put 3 pawns in the holes marked on the side of the board.

The starting player put 1 pawn in the first black or white hole.
Then he turns the black or the white disc one space to the left or the right.
If all the players puts there first pawn in play like this, the starting player can now move a pawn in play, one step ahead or back OR bring a new pawn in play in the first hole.
If you put a pawn in a black hole you have to move the black disc. And for the white hole you move the white disc.

If someone turns 1 of the 2 discs, it can happen that one or more pawns fall true the board. So you lose a pawn.

Can you get the team to guess who the celebrity is - without saying the name?

Hvem i all verden? (Who on Earth?) is based on celebrities.

Game mechanics are as simple as it is fun:
The game spans three rounds, and each round you will give your teammate tips about which celebrities are on the cards. In the first round there is no restriction on the number of hints. In the second round, the same celebs guessed again, but now players must navigate using only a single keyword. In the third and final round, the hints muest be wordless, like miming or humming.

Ching Gong for Four is a Halma variant for two, three or four players on a unique star-shaped board with eight points.  The board is comprised of 133 dots connected by lines, with 11 dots on each corner and 45 in the center of the board.  The eight star points are in four colors, each color matching the star point on the opposite side of the board.  The players' pieces begin on a star point of the same color, and the objective is to be the first to move all of a player's pieces to the opposite star point and win the game.

Rules for movement are the same as for Halma and Chinese Checkers, meaning that players take turns moving one piece at a time in any direction, and that players may jump their own or other players' pieces.  Pieces are not removed from the board.  The pieces must move only along the lines connecting the dots on the board however, and Ching Gong for Four's board is unusual in this regard.  Not only does it have eight points or corners instead of four or six, but not every dot is connected to the other.  Half the dots are connected to eight surround dots, the other half only four surrounding dots.

This game is not to be confused with another game from the same publisher, Ching Gong for Two, which is played on a standard Chinese Checkers board and has three colors of pieces.

The players buy merchanise for 1 million mark at the beginning of the game. The goal of the game is to sell their goods fast and for high Prices. The Player with the highest profit wins the game. Each game turn has two phases. In the stock exchange phase the prices für the goods are determined. In the sale phase the players travel the world by plane and try to sell their goods for the highest prices.

The game seems to be from the 1960s. or 70s.

Dungeon Arena is a frenetic dice-rolling arena battle for two to four players. Players construct a dungeon randomly from available tiles containing hidden bonuses, obstacles, and other features, and then select the class of their fighters. Each class receives a specific configuration of dice that contain some combination of movement, attacking, and turning that is unique to that class. Then, in real-time, players roll their dice as many times as they want to and "lock in" each of their desired dice and take the actions shown on the dice. After a time limit, whichever player has removed the most other players' pawns from the board while sacrificing the fewest of their own is the winner!

Seven Sleepers can be by played on an ordinary 8x8 square checkerboard, though in its original edition the board had starting positions for its pieces marked on the board.  There are 8 blue pieces for one player, 8 red pieces for the other player, and 7 yellow neutral pieces.

The game starts with each player's pieces on the row of 8 squares on opposite sides of the board.  The yellow neutral pieces are on the 7 junctions or intersections between squares in the middle of the board.  Players take turns moving one of their pieces at a time, diagonally to an adjacent space, or jumping an opponent's piece to an opposite vacant space.  Opponents' pieces are not captured or removed from the board.

Players may also move a piece vertically or horizontally, but only to jump an opponent's piece, landing on a vacant space on the other side.  Because of these rules of movement, pieces will be bound to the color of square on which they began play.

The neutral pieces can be captured (removed from the board) by either player making a single diagonal move over the junction on which the neutral piece rests.  Also, once a piece crosses the center line, it may not cross back to that half of the board.  The player who first moves all of his pieces to his opponent's starting positions and has captured the majority of neutral pieces wins the game. Any other outcome is a draw.

A Cold War hangs over Europe! A sustained state of political and military tension between the West and the Soviet Union has made the gathering of secret information a valuable resource on both sides of the Iron Curtain. As a spymaster for the British Secret Intelligence Service (SIS) you must outmaneuver and outthink your opponent in the Russian Komitet Gosudarstvennoy Bezopasnosti (KGB). Can you unmask your opponent’s spies before they can identify your agents in an exciting game of deception and espionage!

Spymaster is a game of bluffing and deduction for 2 players. Each player is a handler - a case officer from either the British SIS or the Russian KGB, responsible for managing a team of field agents (or spies, as they were more commonly known).

The object of the game is to be the first player to eliminate all of your opponents spies.

There are three difficulty levels that can be played - easy, standard or expert:

1. In easy mode each player has one spy.

2. In standard mode each player has two spies.

3. In expert mode each player has three spies.

Over the course of multiple games players earn ranks that indicate how good an intelligence operative they are. Only a player who has achieved the highest rank can call themselves a Spymaster!

Tri Bang is played on a 9x19 grid of squares, of which the outer rows and the long middle row are decorated.  Each player is given 25 colored discs, each his own color, and takes turns placing the discs on any square of the board.

The object is to form three in a row, either beginning or ending on one of the decorated rows, but not with a middle piece of the three on a decorated row.  Players call out "Tri Bang" when completing three in a row, and win the game.  Players can also choose to continue playing, to see who can complete the most Tri Bangs before running out of pieces.

A competitive game with 600 questions about Greece and the whole world focusing on History.

The game play is simple: Each player rolls the dice and according to the outcome of the dice he is being asked the corresponding question. If he answers right, he collects the card.

The player or the team with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

The board represents a spider's web. The spider's den is in the center. 19 pieces are used: one spider, 13 common(yellow) flies and five blue(green) flies. Blue flies count three and common flies are one for a total of 28 points. One team is the flies the other team is the spider. Object of game is to score 15 points. Flies attempt to get into spider's den and score according to their values. Spider seeks to consume flies and keep them out of his den.

Moves are somewhat like checkers. Spider jumps the flies and removes them from the board. Flies do not jump. Common flies may only move forward or around the circle. Blue flies may move backwards as well.

Flies start first. The spider may move in a straight line on any of the lines running toward the center as far as he please. He cannot pass over the double circle either going out or coming in without stopping there one move. On all the circles except the double one the spider cannot move any further than the flies can (one space). On the double circle the spider may move 1/4 of the way around at one move. The spider may jump in any direction as many times as his moves entitle him to. He is requited to stop at the next crossing beyond each fly jumped. If several flies are in position with but one crossing between each fly he must jump all such flies (no matter what direction it takes him) stopping at the next crossing beyond the last fly.

The spider may jump from his den and take any fly on the double circle unless there is another fly in front of or behind it. It also enables him when on the double circle to take a fly standing on the crossing 1/4 the way around if there is no fly on the next crossing beyond and none between. The spider may move clear across the double circle passing through the center on his way and jump a fly on the opposite side of the double circle if there are no intervening flies and none beyond the fly to back him up. The spider may move from the double circle to the outside circle in one move (nothing intervening) but cannot return to the center when outside the double circle without stopping one move on the way at the double circle.

First expansion pack with 30 additional bird-species.

The game is played by moving around the forest (game board) and collecting information tokens for birds. Certain places ask questions and answering them, lets you keep the card. First one to collect certain number is the winner.

TOKAIDO is a game for 2 to 5 players, with unique atmosphere and aesthetics, in which the players are pilgrims on a journey along the legendary East Sea Road of Japan. Along the way, you will try to collect the most beautiful souvenirs, discover glorious vistas, enjoy enriching encounters, soak in soothing hot springs, sample sumptuous cuisine, and much more! Because the Collector’s Edition will incorporate the already famous “Crossroads” expansion, it allows you to savor your journey even more while unveiling new secrets such as lucky charms, lovely calligraphy, or you can even try your luck in the Yakuza game rooms.

With unrivaled setting and aesthetics, TOKAIDO transports you, your family, and your friends to Japan. For all ages: From the youngest to the most experienced, everyone can gather for an ever-fresh gaming experience; it is extremely easy to get started, thanks to its fluid, simple, and tactical rules.

Although TOKAIDO has been acclaimed for its design and artistic qualities ever since its initial release, we at FUNFORGE never settle for just “good”: We are always thinking of how we can do “better”.

After more than a year of discussions with enthusiastic players, who are always asking for more, we decided to transform this work of art into a masterpiece. This long period of reflection has allowed us to design a very unique edition that keeps the advantages of the classic edition, but adds plenty of elements that will make it an exceptional game.

Kickstarter Yakuza/Pilgrim Level- This game contains 34 character tiles (Tokaido + Crossroads + Kickstarter characters) and 16 unpainted miniatures
Kickstarter Ronin Level- This game contains 35 character tiles (Tokaido + Crossroads + Eriku + Kickstarter characters) and 17 unpainted miniatures +4 Encounter Cards
Kickstarter Samurai Level- This game contains 35 character tiles (Tokaido + Crossroads + Eriku + Kickstarter characters) and 35 painted miniatures +4 Encounter Cards

description:
In this fast-paced training and combat game you can choose your character, choose your moves, then battle to become the King of the Dojo! Kumite: King of the Dojo provides excitement for up to 6 players.

You start off as a lowly white belt, knowing only basic punches and blocks. Each turn you unlock more and more advanced Martial Arts techniques. You can customize your knowledge, pick the techniques that work for your strategy, and combine different Martial Arts! When you think you're the strongest, declare yourself King of the Dojo! Can you hold on to the throne in this exciting king of the hill style card game?

This edition includes 6 different Martial Arts styles:
Karate Boxing Crane Taekwondo Judo Muay Thai

Box contains 288 cards, two 6-sided dice, over 100 tokens for tracking, and Instructions.

boardgame: Chicken Draw: The Chicken Maker Card Game
description:
Two Player card game in which each player attempts to reduce the other player's Power to zero before their own is.

Each player selects a card from their hand as their leader, and plays additional cards to support it. The two sides compare their Attack and the loser's Power is reduced by the difference. After that each player loses Power for each card they have played and play proceeds.

The game's art is from postings on the Chicken Maker blog.

In a city overrun by ninjas, mimes, and other villains, you and your fellow heroes have a bet to see who's the best:

Capture villains and put them in your pile for points.  The hero with the most points wins.  Sounds simple, right?  Yeah right, keep dreaming!

None of you are the most honest of heroes.  Steal and discard villains from your friends' piles, or even reverse the rules, in this fast and unpredictable game that keeps everyone scrambling to stay in the lead!

boardgame: Advanced Tobruk System Basic Game II: Action at Carentan
description:
ATS BASIC GAME II continues to Carentan with an all-new 2-piece board! The module adds a set of scenarios + a BG II rulebook and play aids! You can dive right in and begin experiencing the battle while mastering the system. The map is not printed on paper, and instead consists of TWO 12" × 18" BOARDS you are sure to enjoy! What's more, the presentation allows you to use your existing ATS counters from your collection for a low cost battle experience.

An Expansion for ATS.  First released edition does NOT include counters needed for play.  Counters needed from a previous game with American/German units, or available direct from Critical Hit.

boardgame: ATS Basic Game II: Busting the Bocage – Rangers
description:
ATS BASIC GAME II is back and brings you into the heart of Normandy's bocage country in this new edition that remasters the D-Day Rangers map terrain and adds a set of scenarios + a BG II rulebook and play aids! You can dive right in and begin mastering the system right away. The map is not printed on paper, and instead consists of TWO 12" × 18" BOARDS you are sure to enjoy! What's more, the presentation allows you to use your existing ATS counters from the collection for a low cost battle experience.

An Expansion for ATS.  First released edition does NOT include counters needed for play.  Counters needed from a previous game with American/German units, or available direct from Critical Hit.

boardgame: Advanced Tobruk System Basic Game II: Stalin's Fury
description:
The original ATS BG II is back in Stalingrad and uses a new 12" × 18" board (card, not paper) and standard ATS sized hexes. An updated BG II rulebook and play aids provide everything you need to play—just bring your own counters from the ATS collection—and begins you on the path to system experience at a low cost.

An Expansion for ATS.  First released edition does NOT include counters needed for play.  Counters needed from a previous Complete Game with Russian/German units, or available direct from Critical Hit.

Win with your words in a speed game based on the addictive online game! Start with the longest word you can make with your first 9 tiles, and then race to see who can get the most words on the grid. Don't worry about taking turns! Extend other players' words, play right through them, and get rid of all your tiles first to win! Or play in Score mode and score points for every word, double word and triple word you've got on the board. Word up with the WORDS WITH FRIENDS Race game! Includes 100 tiles and portable case.

It is the Battle of the Bulge and once again some of the finest soldiers ever birthed by a great nation ... are standing firm ... against the violent force of an entire Panzer Division. There is no turning back on the road to Bastogne, the linchpin to Hitler's last offensive ... and the scene of Fateful Stand ... replete with new counter art, a full collection of combat counters in Winter gear and camo schemes. Baby, it's cold outside! But we aim to warm the hearts of tactical-level gamers north and south of the equator.

COMPONENT-WISE: ATS Fateful Stand is an ATS Expansion.  Requires ownership of any ATS Complete game or the ATS Rules, Play Aids, status markers to play.

This is a ziplock ATS Historical Module that includes everything you need to play just bring your ATS Rulebook/Play Aids/10-sided dice. The counters are the original ATS --3/4", 5/8"-- widths, and you receive all the counters and markers you need to play. The map is 'winterized' for evocative gaming, historical leaders straight from 'Band of Brothers' ... winter whitewashed tanks ... it is ALL there for your to enjoy.

Tips! is a game of hot plates & cold revenge. Hustle for the biggest tips, sabotaging co-workers along the way.

~~~How does it play?~~~~

Serve your customers drinks, appetizers, main dishes and desserts in large quantities. The number of menu items you can serve of each type is constrained by the party size, also the menu items need to be served in a particular order (no dessert before the main meal in this place!). Minimize the number of times you get an order wrong, have customers run out the door without paying, and taking annoying restroom breaks (try to just hold it in!).

At the end of the game, each player counts up their tips - whoever has the most wins!

boardgame: Mens erger je niet! Card Game
description:
Translated from the box:
Who collects two complete series of cards first? Use your Joker and Choice cards well and avoid being hit by your opponents.

Gameplay:
Start with 3 cards. Take a card, play a card. Collect series of 6 cards. Your Start card determines in which order to build the series: up, down or random. Close a series with a Home card.
You can hit on the last cards in an opponents series using Tease cards as long as that series isn't completed with a Home card.
Jokers can substitute any number card and can be exchanged for the right number in your turn and must be played again in the same turn.
Choice cards make you turn extra cards from the deck (as many as there are players). You get to choose first. Don't forget to end your turn with just 3 cards in hand. The other players have to fullfil that condition at their respective turns.

Goal: Be the first racing to position '50'

Game Play:
Typical 'roll & move' game - Roll the die by turns and step forward - ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid.

Notes:
Likely published late 1920's/early 30's

Goal: Be the first to race to position '50' (back home);
learn more about fairy tales

Game Play:
Typical 'roll & move' game - Roll the die by turns and step forward - ending on a marked space follow instructions.
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
This game is based on the French comics "Lincoln".

From the publisher:

Lincoln is a speed and observation game where Lincoln and his friends need help to find their missing objects.

The game mechanism is such that when a combination of cards triggers the action of a player, he is not the only one to score points, but the other players could also get some, if they are fast enough.

Cerebronians are here! And they are invading Earth! Well... They are invading Braintown... but eventually...

Braintown is a game for 2 to 5 players who play as cerebronians: mind controlling alliens. The cerebronians want to start their invasion and they need to choose a good leader. The best cerebronians are selected to compete in a real invasion scenario: Braintown, a little town populated by normal and weird citizens.

Each player uses his or her dice to control cards by getting high values and by throwing them in the correct place over the field.

Each player will have an objective. Each objective consists in eliminating some inhabitants. The first player to achieve his or her objective, wins the game and becomes the leading cerebronian!

Objective: Get as many bonus points as possible
 - acting by turns -
Each player gets 3 x 3 cards; cards show animals (mostly herbivores and a few carnivores) and the hunter; each card has a special value, which adds up to a total sum of 220 points;

Active player plays a herbivore out of a given class eg. (4) birds, rated from 1-4 /the higher the number the stronger). Next player must play a card of this class; if he/she hasn't any there is a choice to play a matching carnivore or discard a random card. Trick is made by the carnivore player or highest rating within demanded class. Hunter card can be used as a joker, points can be doubled under certain circumstances (announcements).

Notes:
Different from 1966 published 'Halali' (Happy Families) card game (same publisher)

Based on the 1958-1960 ITV quiz show of the same name, which in turn was based on the short-lived 1958 CBS/NBC "Scandal-Rigged" American game show of the same name.

Players only get to join dots by answering questions on specific subjects. The harder the question attempted, the more dots the player is entitled to join together on that turn. After joining 25 dots the host sets the first clue to help identify the picture. A second clue was given at 35 dots, and a final clue at 40 dots. These clues should be written by the host and disclosed only to the relevant player.

A player calls "Dotto" if the picture is recognized at any stage of the game. The answer is given and written to the host, undisclosed to their opponent. More points are awarded for earlier recognition as each unconnected dot earned 5 points. A player must give the name of the picture to the host once all the dots are joined. The host announces whether the guess is correct or not. If correct, the second player has one attempt to guess and so draw the game. If drawn the game is repeated.

Alternative games can be played such as double Dotto where the value of the dots is doubled. If a player gives an incorrect answer the game is over. The opponent received no points but is victorious and remains to fight a new challenger. Any number of rounds may be played, at the end of which the player with the highest points scored is the winner.

Cave Escape is a push-your-luck dice game for 2 to 6 players where people are in a cavern that is about to collapse.

The players are working to be the first to escape from a 5,000ft deep cavern while making sure that no one gets out before them by sabotaging the other players. The first player to get their meeple top the 5,000ft mark is the winner and survives - the rest of the players are dead. Sorry!

The game adds a cutthroat twist to traditional push-your-luck mechanisms for you can sabotage another player's roll so that they lose it all or force them to quit rolling. It also allows players to get farther faster with special dice that they can purchase on their turn with adrenaline tokens.

Obliterate your enemies in this tactical war game of epic robot battle.

Kingdom Bots is a fast-paced competitive board game for 3 to 4 players. Each player controls a unique faction of robots with the objective to build a civilization while attacking and defending against other robot armies. It's a 3-4 hour, constantly evolving game of world domination that rewards aggressive gameplay and clever strategy.

Stunning comic book style artwork immerses you in this world of hack-and-slash robots. The art is showcased on the revolutionary new Command Card mechanic, which brings a level of strategy to the game that even new players can pick up only a few turns into their first game.

-STORY-
The world of Kingdom Bots is set far into the future. Mankind has vanished, leaving behind only relics and rubble. Machines that were once built for menial service have developed self-awareness and evolved to become the new form of dominant life upon the earth.

Like-minded robots found new kingdoms. Diggers who now worship rocks. Maintenance droids who now assemble patchwork monstrosities and builders who can only see one blueprint for the design of new bots. The different ideologies fall into conflict. But instead of learning from the mistakes of the fallen empire that created them, the robots find themselves driven by the old programming. To build. To grow. No matter what… or who is in the way.

What’s left of the world is yours for the taking.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the Publisher:
Expand KOI POND with new tactics, new characters, and new art! With these cards, you can even add an extra player to your group!

Includes:

20 new Koi cards: 4-Koi in four suits, 2 each; 5-Koi in four suits, 2 each
4 rainbow koi: A special hybrid koi that can be played as any suit
4 Novice cards: Scores for each 1-koi card wherever you place him!
4 Monk cards: Scores for each 2-koi card wherever you place him!
4 Abbot cards: Scores for each 3-koi card wherever you place him!
4 Shrines: One in each suit. A shrine doubles the number of koi wherever you place it!
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Llamanomics (a contraction of Llamas and Economics) is a supply and demand negotiation card game where you use Llamas to buy, transport, and sell goods.

Each turn a player takes a card from the deck. Throughout the deck there are 'Burgermeister' cards. When a 'Burgermeister' card is pulled players may choose to sell any of the commodities they have on their llamas for their current point value (determined by the 'Demand' cards). The values of the different commodities change throughout the game.

During the game, you can play action cards to upgrade your own llamas, or apply negative effects on other players.

The game ends when the deck runs out of cards. The player with the most money wins.


Llamanomics is a card game about supply, demand, and llamas (and the occasional alpaca).

The goal of the game is to get the most valuable commodities and sell them for the most points. 
 You play as a trader, taking his/her commodities to the market to sell.  And what will be carrying your commodities?  Why, your trusty llamas!  The more llamas you get, the more you can carry.  The more you can form corner the market on different commodities and squeeze out your competition.  Capitalism style!  Llama style!

So, the strategy comes in with 'ACTION CARDS', which allow you to upgrade your llamas, downgrade your competitors llamas, steal llamas, swap commodities, and all sorts of other shenanigans!

'DEMAND CARDS' add yet another element of gameplay as the value of the commodities changes throughout the game.

You have to be clever in placing llamas, commodities and action cards.  You have to be even more clever in choosing which other players you will trade with, or harass, make alliances with, and take the free-llama-market by storm!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You are well-mannered nobles settling grievances in the time-honored tradition: Dueling catapults attached to old-timey bicycles, launching wealth at each other!

Your goal is to hit and collect valuable items from the track. Unfortunately, your catapult is so rickety, it moves backwards the same distance that you launched!

Try to stay out of range of your opponent's catapults, because if you’re hit, you lose one of your valuables!
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: Agri-Cup: The John Deere Farming Game
description:
Farm development and trading board game.

The first player to: Repay the loan, build a farmhouse, build Livestock sheds, build Produce sheds, install electricity and water and buy a tractor is the winner.

- acting by turns -
Each player is given a piece of land, provided with a Government loan, and through wise buying, productivity and selling, has to develop his farm.

Roll the die and move forward; depending on the space you reach you may/have to buy, sell, get, pay...

Notes:
Similar approach to Monopoly, with a guidance for agricultural business.
Quite comparable to Agri-hazard.

A Print And Play dice expansion to the contest winning game "7 Dice Wonders".

The Expansion introduces Battles, as well as more Trade, and more unpredictability.

It is partly based on "7 Wonders: Leaders" by Antoine Bauza from Repos Production.

Variants:

Rulers - adds a new die to the game, and makes it possible to have rulers in your empire - which can be bribed (taken away) by others.

Players roll seven of the eight dice per round (age).

Teams - adds a corporation between two players (or with the bot) for a combined victory or defeat.

Territory - adds an map for the wonders - first come, first serve.

Components:
1 Die. 5 Rulercards. 4 new Scoresheets.

This is a card game.

Cards are covered with 8 yin-yangs which can be red or black.

The winner is the last player with cards in his hand.

Each player in turn tries to lay a card on the cards already laid out. The correspondence of yin-yangs between the card played and the card it covers defines if the move is possible and how many points are obtained.

Basically, each symbol of a colour must be laid out on a symbol of the other colour. A player gets 8 points if his card completely covers the previous one or 4 points if it only covers half.
 For each point marked, the player can take a new card from the remaining stack. A player without any card left is excluded.

The goal of the game is to get rid of all your card before anyone else does. However, it is easier said than done and players are limited in the ways they can play cards.

Players can only put down a card on a pile with the same design and color. Each card placed down needs to be higher then the previous one.

The game starts with three open piles. Each pile is played until no players can put down any more cards. At this point the first player who was unable to put down any cards opens up a new pile.

There are 8 special cards in the deck. 6 closing cards, and 2 switching cards. A closing card allows a player to close any pile of the same color, and open up a new one at any point. The switching cards allows a player to switch the color of a pile with the same background color, and the design of a pile to anything they would like. The next card played after a switch card must still be higher then the number under it.

In this 1941 game, players had to administer France's overseas colonies.  They drew cards representing colonies, then had to use the appropriate tools and resources, and manage local labor forces to gain exports from the colonies.  A colony would go out of play once its major exports were fully exploited.

Can be played by 2, 4, or 8 players.

boardgame: Heroes of Normandie: River Set Terrain Pack
description:
Terrain Expansion for Heroes of Normandie base game.
Introduces rivers to the base game.

Contains:
5 new double sided terrain tiles.
1 punchboard with ruins and explosives.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
There are two dogs and six cats. The cats try to steal the Wurst and escape. The dogs have to protect their Wurst. Cats and dogs may move in any direction as many spaces as they roll with one die. Cats can only jump over cats and dogs over dogs. If a dog gets on a space with a cat, he drives the cat from the game board. If the cat has the Wurst, the dog gets really mad and kills the cat. The cats even try to steal the wurst from each other. If a cat escapes with the Wurst, it wins the game. A dog has to kill three cats to win the game (nice doggy).

This game was published in the 1930s or 40s.

Steampunked is a game of strategy, subtlety, and sabotage. Players work against one another to be the first to assemble a time machine using turn of the century "Steam Punk" inspired components.

The first player to assemble their 9-part time machine and end their turn wins the game. However, this is easier said than done. This is the point in the game where the fur really starts to fly! Action cards are played in rapid succession as your opponents attempt to break and disassemble your machine in order to get parts for their own creation. The only way to assure your victory is to employ a multitude of strategies including, hording parts, conserving energy, forming alliances, and, of course, breaking said alliances at the most opportune moment!

Steampunked Time Machine is a card game with a core set made for 2 players. The game can however be expanded via additional expansion cards and 'booster packs' to play with up to 6 players.

It is the Caribbean in the 16th century. New lands have been discovered, and the stories about riches and influence on the new continent are abound in the old world. Resources aplenty cause the royal dynasties of Europe to secure huge chunks of
the new world. They send out brave captains and seamen to gather influence for their own country.

Set sail and head west, your monarch needs you.

This expansion focuses on Japanese food and the enjoyment of eating it. It contains Japanese food-related nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and a few particles for balance. Examples include Ramen, Sushi, Tempura, Eat, Cook, Fry, Delicious, Nightly, and Never.

Each expansion to Japanese: The Game adds new vocabulary words, and thus the rules that go along with how each noun, verb, or particle can be placed to make a sentence.

The expansion decks are shuffled in with the main deck to accommodate more players (the game plays 2 by itself and needs either an additional core deck or an expansion to accommodate more), or just to add less common words to the mix for 1-2 players.

This deck will help you become more fluent with words you’ll hear in Anime (and some manga) and in conversations about it, such as Fanatic, Space, Magic, Shut up, and Upperclassman.

Each expansion to Japanese: The Game adds new vocabulary words, and thus the rules that go along with how each noun, verb, or particle can be placed to make a sentence.

The expansion decks are shuffled in with the main deck to accommodate more players (the game plays 2 by itself and needs either an additional core deck or an expansion to accommodate more), or just to add less common words to the mix for 1-2 players.

This 36-card Travel Expansion is geared to teach you words you’ll need to get around in Japan. Words like Hotel, Where, and Bathroom as well as Pay, Taxi, Fare, Train, and Ticket will make your adventures in the Land of the Rising Sun more comfortable and less stressful.

Each expansion to Japanese: The Game adds new vocabulary words, and thus the rules that go along with how each noun, verb, or particle can be placed to make a sentence.

The expansion decks are shuffled in with the main deck to accommodate more players (the game plays 2 by itself and needs either an additional core deck or an expansion to accommodate more), or just to add less common words to the mix for 1-2 players.

This 36-card Business Expansion Deck brings you vocabulary appropriate to the office and business transactions. Words like “Profit,” “Office,” “Co-worker,” “Commute,” and “Boss” are included along with a mix of particles and other words that allow these cards to integrate into a Core Deck without any loss of play balance.

Each expansion to Japanese: The Game adds new vocabulary words, and thus the rules that go along with how each noun, verb, or particle can be placed to make a sentence.

The expansion decks are shuffled in with the main deck to accommodate more players (the game plays 2 by itself and needs either an additional core deck or an expansion to accommodate more), or just to add less common words to the mix for 1-2 players.

This 36-card Expansion Deck brings your Japanese grammar concepts up to an intermediate level.  You’ll be able to conjugate Verbs, Adjectives, and certain Copulas to many different tenses, including:

Past Tense
    Negative
    Command Form
    Polite Request Form
    Invitation/Volitional Form
    and many more!

Much of this isn’t discussed until the third semester of college. But you can integrate this after one or two days of getting used to Japanese: The Game’s Core Deck, maybe sooner.

Abstract Strategy Tile laying/Card game with Climate change theme reflecting the hunt for clean energy.

The game is for 2 to 4 players aged 7 to adult.

The game was developed in cooperation with Novozymes and was designed by Steen Riisgaard.

Contents are:

Mounted playing board.

Four sets of plastic playing bricks in red,yellow,green, and blue
comprising of 8 X 1 square bricks, 8 x 2 square bricks, and 8 x 3 square bricks.

48 one square black `consequence` bricks.

36 white `Clean energy bricks` comprising of
12 x 1 square bricks, 12 x 2 square bricks, 12 x 3 square bricks.

24 Rethink cards

32 Consequence cards

1 sheet of rules.

The object of the game is to collect all the stores of clean energy printed around the board, while making the most efficient overall use of the energy required to reach the clean energy points on the board. Symbols of clean energy come in the form of 1,2,or 3 white square bricks which are taken when one of his tiles covers (or partially covers) a white energy space on the board.

On a players turn he may place either a 1, 2 or 3 square brick on the board, providing the placed brick shorter side comes into contact with a `tail end` of a previously placed brick.

The board also has catastrophe and rethink symbols which when covered ( or partially covered by a players brick force a player to draw the corresponding card from the deck which he reads aloud and all players follow the event of the card.

The game ends when either the white energy symbols on the board have been covered and collected, or each player has 10 black consequence bricks in their possession.

At the conclusion of the game players add up the totals of their Plus energy bricks that have been placed on the board, minus the black consequence bricks they have, to give a final
energy point score.

The player with the higher Plus energy is declared the winner.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from  Thomas Cook travel company, located in the UK.

This special edition of Monopoly celebrates the growth of travel in the UK through the years.

Monopoly Overview
Players take the part of land owners, attempting to buy and then develop their land. Income is gained by other players visiting their properties and money is spent when they visit properties belonging to other players. When times get tough, players may have to mortgage their properties to raise cash for fines, taxes and other misfortunes.

Game play
On his turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he has the opportunity to buy it and add it to his portfolio or allow the bank to auction it to the highest bidder. If a player owns all the spaces within a color group, he may then build houses and hotels on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If he lands on a property owned by another player, he must pay that player rent according to the value of the land and any buildings on it. There are other places on the board which can not be bought, but instead require the player to draw a card and perform the action on the card, pay taxes, collect income, or even go to jail.

Goal
The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the U.S. Navy

Income is gained by other players visiting their properties and money is spent when they visit properties belonging to other players. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he has the opportunity to buy it and add it to his portfolio or allow the bank to auction it to the highest bidder. If a player owns all the spaces within a color group, he may then build houses and hotels on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If he lands on a property owned by another player, he must pay that player rent according to the value of the land and any buildings on it. There are other places on the board which can not be bought, but instead require the player to draw a card and perform the action on the card, pay taxes, collect income, or even go to jail.

The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Swindon, in the United Kingdom.

FROM THE BOX:

The game based on the popular TV Programme NOW YOU SEE IT. One of the players acts as the reader, reads out the clues and stars the timer. The first player to stop the timer and name the hidden word scores the points.

The board game itself was based on the 1985-1993 Seven Network (then later Nine Network from 1998-1999) kids game show which in turn was based on the short-lived 1974-75 and 1989 adult game show from America of the same name.

Cyclades: Titans, the second expansion for Cyclades, includes a new game board with large islands on which ground attacks can be carried out as well as the Titans of Greek legend, who can conduct attacks without the help of Ares.

In the Cyclades archipelago, tension has reached a boiling point.
The conflict is now focused mainly on two large neighboring islands. With the help of the Gods, there are now 6 great cities of ancient Greece fighting for supremacy.
Now, more than ever, the war is raging. And on top of all that, Kronos decided to join the party, and with him comes a horde of Titans, ready to annihilate everything in their path…

The Titans expansion allows for up to 6 players, and offers 2 game modes:
• From 3 to 5: competitive game. « Everyone for himself » just like in the base game.
The goal is still to have two Metropolises at the end of a cycle.
• With 4 or 6 players: team-play (with teams of two) – The goal of each team is to have three metropolises at the end of a cycle.
With 4 players, one can decide to play either competitive OR in teams.
With 6 players, the team-play is MANDATORY.

Part of Fleet: Arctic Bounty Kickstarter campaign. They will be available by other outlets

Quantity: 5

Bay Card: During setup, place all five Crab Meeples near the center of the play area.

A Crab Meeple is caught by the first full boat of each different Standard license in the game. If more than one player completes the criteria in the same phase and round, tie is broken by first in turn order. When a boat reaches capacity and is the first of its type to do so, that player immediately receives a Crab Meeple and places it on the license matching that boat. The Crab Meeple cannot be lost.

All Crab Meeples held at Game End are worth 4 VP

Part of Fleet: Arctic Bounty Kickstarter campaign. They will be available by other outlets

Quantity: 4 + 16 Salmon Boat Cards

During setup, four Salmon licenses are added to the pool of Standard licenses and 16 Salmon Boat Cards are added to the Boat Card supply.

The Salmon licenses are purchased and played from the License Deck in the same manner as other license cards.

Once owned, a Salmon license gives a player the ability to
launch Salmon boats. A Salmon license also gives a player the ability to draw one Boat Card at the beginning of the Fishing Phase. The player can choose to either keep the card in hand OR tuck it under any already-Launched and Captained boat he owns. The tucked card will catch a Crate along with the boat during Fishing and has a capacity of four Crates.

One (1) Salmon license owned: Draw one card during Fishing; may keep the card in hand OR tuck it under an already-Launched and Captained boat they own.

Two (2) Salmon licenses owned: Draw two cards during Fishing; may keep the cards in hand OR tuck them under separate already-Launched and Captained boats they own.

Three (3) Salmon licenses owned: Draw three cards during Fishing; may keep the cards in hand OR tuck them under separate already-Launched and Captained boats they own.

Four (4) Salmon licenses owned: Draw four cards during Fishing; may keep the cards in hand OR tuck

Part of Fleet: Arctic Bounty Kickstarter campaign. They will be available by other outlets

Quantity: 8

Dock Card: During setup, two First Mate cards per player
are placed face up on the table near the License Card deck

A First Mate on a boat that has reached its max capacity of Crates is worth 5VP. Otherwise, he is worth 2VP.

Part of Fleet: Arctic Bounty Kickstarter campaign. They will be available by other outlets

Quantity: 6

Bay Card: During setup, shuffle all Trophy Cards, randomly select three, and place them face up near the center of the play area.

Trophies are awarded to the first player to complete the criteria OR at Game End. If more than one player completes the criteria in the same phase and round, tie is broken by first in turn order.

Once a Trophy Card has been awarded, it cannot be lost. If there is a tie for a Game End Trophy, no one receives the Trophy

All Trophy Cards held at game end are worth 5 VP.

Trophy Card types:
First to Launch and Captain 4 Boats of the same Type
First to Launch and Captain 4 Boats of different Types
First to have 15 Crates on Launched Boats
First to have 3 of the same License Type
Most Launched Boats (Game End)
Most Standard Licenses (Game End)

Part of Fleet: Arctic Bounty Kickstarter campaign. They will be available by other outlets

Quantity: 8

Dock Card: During setup, two House Boat cards per player
are placed face up on the table near the Boat Card supply.

House Boat can be purchased during the Launch Phase for 3 coin and then discarded at anytime for 5 coin. It is a great cash spike! If you somehow manage not to use it, good luck, its worth 2VP at game end!

Part of Fleet: Arctic Bounty Kickstarter campaign. They will be available by other outlets

Using a combination of Fleet and Fleet: Arctic Bounty along with the Charter Boats, Fleet will be able to be played with 5 and 6 players! All 12 Charter Boat Cards are added to the Boat Card supply. Charter Boats can be launched under any license!

The Charter Boats can also be used as a mix in at any player count to spice Fleet up even more.

The object of the game is to get your own tiddlywinks into three cups worth either 25 or 50 points.  Players take turns flicking their winks onto the board, trying to land them in the cups.  There are some free turn spaces on the board, and if players land their pieces there, they can take another turn.

If players miss the cups, they can place an additional piece on the board, and on additional turns, players can either flick their winks or roll a die to move their piece on the board.  When they land their piece on a color that matches their pieces, they can make another attempt to flick a wink into a cup.  If they manage to get their piece to the end of the trail, they place one of their winks into the cup at the end of their trail and take their piece back.

The cups at the end of the trails are worth 25 points each, while the one that can only be reached by the tiddlywinks is worth 50 points.  The game ends when one player has all four of his winks in the cups, and players total up the points of all their winks.  Whoever has the highest points wins the game.

The Emperor is dead. Dragon Empire stands on the brink of destruction as four evil forces try to conquer the last place standing: the capitol — but then another threat looms, an alien invasion force that just wants to devour our world.

World Eaters gives players the chance to play as four main evils (Orcs, Demons, Undead, Cold ones) and to conquer as much terrain as they can by rolling dice, recruiting armies, then sending them to fight by collecting dice pools.

As much as evil will act brutally - that much Burn they collect and that much retaliation from Empire they will get. On Map 9x9 there will be cities to attack and either plunder them or conquer to gain resources bringing players closer to victory.

On their turn each player will throw dice depending on their logistic power to bring more troops, send them for quest, prepare the attack, build forts or sending spies to see the defense on city they wish to attack.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Wolverhampton, in the United Kingdom.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Bailiwick of Guernsey, an island in the United Kingdom.

FROM THE BOX:

The aim of the game is to collect four compatible Dexter cards for each section (Head, Body, Legs & Feet) to complete your Perfect Match, i.e. the right head on the right body and so fourth. On the way to meeting your Perfect Match you'll be revealing to the other players the real you. You'll be asking questions about your friends' personalities, as well as answering ones about yourself. You'll really get to know who your playing with as you collect, blind date and swap your way to your perfect match.

"Congratulation on purchasing your very own Perfect Match game. I had a lot of fun compering hundreds of episodes of Perfect Match on the television show. I hope you have a few laughs and many hours of enjoyment playing your game at home with friends."

"Best of luck and of course - I hope you find your Perfect Match"

Greg Evans

Based on the formerly popular and long-running 1980s Network Ten (and later Seven Network in 2002) Australian TV dating game show of the same name.

boardgame: Apples to Apples: Jelly Belly Special Edition
description: Choose the word from the Red Apple side of the card that you think is best described by the Green Apple side played by the judge. If the judge selects your card, you win 1 Jelly Belly jelly bean. First player to collect 3 Jelly Belly jelly beans wins.

The game follows the tradition of trick-taking card games.

Kylim allows up to 10 players to enjoy a trick taking game with a twist. Several suites of cards are mixed, in an untraditional arrangement; some of the suits are present several times, others only once, and not all suits have all the numbers in them, bringing variability to the game.

The aim of the game is to predict one's own ability to gain tricks. Furthermore, players can bid on other player's ability to take tricks. And finally, there is a booster card ready to throw everybody's strategy overboard...
