boardgame: Kanzume Goddess: Thor, God of Thunder
description:
Promo god for Kanzume Goddess.

Thor is a Norse god, with the War Drum and Smash abilities that enable you to pay energy to gain benefits during an attack or the discard phase.

Each player represents a royal family that is attempting to influence the King or the Queen of an Ancient Kingdom in order to gain the royal favor that will make you become the next most powerfull family in the land.

In order to achieve the goal the players will have 10 (Ten) Archetypes they will use to surround the Royal Consort of their color with as much influence as possible.

The player who uses with cunning the resources at his/her disposal will be the winner.


SETUP

Each player will place his/her 10 tiles with the ACTIVE (This is the side with the banner) side up in his/her Activation Zone (Area close to the player where all the archetypes will enter play or return from play)

Place the king and the queen in the middle ground between the players in the same row with 2 spaces between them.

The game will be played in an imaginary grid with each space the size of one tile.

GAMEPLAY

Flip a coin, roll a die, find your method of convenience to choose a first player.

The game will be played in turns one player at a time. On your turn you must do the following actions:

1) PLAY A TILE from your Activation Area.

The first tile played by each player WILL NOT ACTIVATE ITS ABILITY. So it is very important you choose well which of your archetypes wont be used in the beginning of the game.

A tile played must always be adjacent to another tile (even diagonally) So you cannot place a tile anywhere in the imaginary grid.

A tile played always enters the game on the side it was in the Activation Area. So if the tile played was ACTIVE it stays with the side of the banner up and if the tile was INACTIVE it stays with the side that has a token drawn.

Consider INACTIVE tiles as protected from abilities when they are in PLAY since abilities cannot target INACTIVE tiles. It´s a very good strategy to keep this in mind because an opponent won´t be able to affect one of your INACTIVE tiles.

2) ACTIVE its ability (if possible)

Tiles that have been affected by an ability are turned to the INACTIVE SIDE (the one with the token on it). Same happens with the King and Queen.

Abilities cannot affect tiles that are in play with the INACTIVE SIDE up. This means that once a tile has been turned to the INACTIVE SIDE it´s safe on the board. It can be moved as usual by its owner but will not altered by an opponent.


3) MOVE ONE OF YOUR TILES IN PLAY ONE SPACE (diagonally or perpendicularly)

Notice that the joker has a special ability that allows you to move ONE MORE TIME after you placed it. This is a very strategical ability that can make big difference when you are trying to reposition a high influence tile on your turn or when you want to remove an opponent tile as well. Under no circumstance a tile can end up or move over another tile in the table.

When you move a tile it must always end adjacent to another tile. So you cannot move it on empty space unless it ended there due to the movement of the other tiles.


END GAME & WINNING CONDITIONS

The game will end when one of this two conditions happen:

1) One player starts his/her turn with his target consort surrounded by his own tiles  (perpendicularly).

The victory is total. The more you play the less this condition is going to happen.

2) The players have played all of their tiles.

Once the players have played all of their tiles comes the scoring. For this step only the tiles that surround PERSPERNDICULARLY (up, down and sides) each royal consort will count. Add all the influence numbers in the tiles, PLUS all the tokens if there are INACTIVE tiles among them (each one is worth 1 point) MINUS the total influence on the tiles your opponent has surrounding your royal consort. Compare this numbers... the player with the most influence WINS the game. In case of a tie... you are both too smart to outwit each other :)


COMPONENTS

20 double sided tiles
1 king
1 queen

Your Minions serve you without question. You are their Gawd. Their souls feed you in your quest toward the power to realize XHAOS THEORY. As you carefully wager their souls in bloody battles against other Gawds you risk losing those precious souls. A Gawd without worship ceases to exist.
Command and guide your minions carefully or all hope is lost.

Xhaos Theory is for those that have always asked “What if?” Young and old, experienced or not. Play with a massive miniature collection, or just a handful of models or toys.

Xhaos Theory allows you to create virtually any miniature unit you want, from any time frame, and fantasy or Sci-Fi book you have ever read and take that force up against another player that has created their own force. That force could be a one miniature force of a giant city eating lizard, or a squad of super heroes, or perhaps a legion of undead! Scenarios, settings, number of models? That's all up to you!

Xhaos Theory is a scalable miniature wargame allowing players total control over size and setting. You can play a full out large scale battle, or a small skirmish with Xhaos Theory. Many battles have been fought with 7 miniatures on one side, and 20+ on the other.

Xhaos Theory is a turn based miniature war game simplified for younger gamers also for quick play. Complete customization of your own army and campaign system to advance your army.

Shifty Partners is a partnership trick-taking standard deck playing card game, with randomly-selected trumps. Partnerships change with each hand, and scoring is by individual. Trumps change with each hand, determined by a d6 die-roll.

The object of the game is to have the most points at the end of the game. The game ends after each player has dealt one hand. During the game, each player will be partnered with every other player. Partnership designations shift after each hand. A unique feature is that the dealer is always a partner with the player on his right.

A partial deck is used, with the number of cards determined by the number of players. Five cards are dealt to each player, with one being face-up. Only four tricks are played in each hand.

Each trick captured counts 1 point, and each Queen and Deuce counts 1 point. Each member of a partnership scores the total points caught by the entire partnership.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Objective:
be the first reaching space 90 - simple 'Roll&Move'-game.
Each player moves forward according die roll; ending on a marked/larger space follow instructions.

note:
cute drawings on a 23" x 15.3" map,
likely published 1950's

This is a Poker variant with a novel twist. Each player gets two cards and then tries to 'shoot' a ball into the spinner (rather like a Roulette wheel). You have four balls to shoot but you can stop if you think you have a card that improves your hand. Of course, there is a lot of scope for bluffing.

Game contains:
Board
Spinner
Playing Cards
Poker Chips
16 Plastic balls in four colours
Rules

Objective: Using the detailed street map of New York each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

American Football game that uses bits of film to show the outcome of chosen plays. Each player uses a playbook, one for defense and one for the offence. Plays are chosen and the apt piece of film shows the outcome.

Bump up the challenge of your Dungeon Attack! adventures with the addition of these two event dice.

With the Trap Die, each time a player Bust the Door there is a chance a trap is triggered.

With the Labyrinth Die, each time a player Bust the Door, there is chance the adventurer has wandered off and is lost. That is why it is a good idea to never separate the party.

This pack contains two event dice and their respective optional rules cards.

Action boxing game similar to Rock 'Em Sock 'Em Robots. Each player controls a boxer. The boxers face each other and start throwing punches. A clean punch to the nose will knock down that boxer. Play as long as you like, the one with the most knock-downs is the winner.

Included is a 45 rpm record of ring-side sound effects, which you can play in the background to build up the atmosphere.

Include these dice in your Dungeon Attack! games to battle against the taint that is chaos.

This set contains x4 Chaos Dice and rules card.

In game play a chaos hit taken taints the Adventurer preventing him or her from using any magic items. Ouch! Defeat these agents of evil quick before they weaken your group of heroes.

Indoor target game. Each player in turn will take the three plastic rings and try to throw them over the post. Most successes wins.

It is advertised as being two games in one because you can choose the post to be in a fixed base or you can choose the slightly harder game where the post is fitted into a base that rocks around as you hit it.

This is a car racing game. The first player to complete a prior agreed upon number of laps wins the game. Each turn, each player moves the spinner two times. The first spin determines the the number of spaces he can move his car. The second spin results in an event, that can influence his movement or allow him to draw a card. The players start with 6 cards. The cards can be played to gain advantages for ones own movements or hinder the opponents movements. The colored segments on the race track symbolize speed limits, which can be exceeded by playing double-, triple- and risk cards.

This game was most likely published in the 1960s.

boardgame: Fireball Forward: Britain's Lost Opportunity – Villers Bocage
description: Villers-Bocage, Britain's Lost Opportunity is a book of tactical scenarios for the Fireball Forward! game system. It is centered around the British attack on the town of Villers-Bocage and the larger Operation Perch against German positions west of Caen, in Normandy, on 12-14 June 1944. It features engagements between the British 7th Armoured Division-the famed 'Desert Rats' - and the 50th Infantry Division against the elite German Panzer Lehr Division, the 2nd Panzer Division, and the 101 st 55 Heavy Panzer Battalion. 15 Scenarios cover the desperate struggle for the town of Villers-Bocage, 50th Infantry Division's attempt to drive Panzer Lehr out of its positions west of Caen, and panzer ace Michael Wittmann's daring, single-handed counterattack against a column of British armor that changed the course of the battle. A Campaign Game, an Historical Summary, Orders of Battle, and Designer's Notes are included to help put the scenarios in the larger context of operations in Normandy and allow players to design their own scenarios.

boardgame: Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #8
description:
The Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #8 is composed of 3 expansions:

Mad City: The Capitol
The Capitol tile can be used to expand the scoring of tiles that adjoin it. Clever placing and timing is the main key to using it.

Station Master: Flags & Whistles
Includes two tiles.

Tile #1: The Station Master's Whistle
After the first train leaves the Station and has been scored give the Whistle tile to the player with the lowest score. If there is a tie for lowest score then wait until the next train has left the Station - basically as soon as there is a clear lowest score that player is given the tile. They may use it at any time in the game to send any train out of the station early (as in immediately the whistle blows). However, if the tile hasn't been used and another player becomes the lowest scorer then they take the tile. The tile may only be used once during the entire game (that is ONCE not once per player) - put it back in the box when it has been used.

Tile #2: The Guard's Flag
This tile may also only be used once per game. It begins on the table next to the train stack. On their turn any player may decide to use the Flag instead of taking their normal turn (ie they do not get to play a carriage or place a passenger chit). The Flag is placed above a train which shows that the train has a fault and cannot be moved. Remove all the passenger chits and return them to their owners, then discard the carriages and the train engine - no one scores this train. After use place the Flag back in the box

Zen Garden: Raked Sand
The two tiles allow a player to remove and replace a previously played tile - except a feature tile with a cube on it already.

This is available in Game Trade Magazine (Issue 173 - July 2014).

The goal of the game ist to get points by enclosing opponent playing pieces. The players can set their pieces (with their faces down) on any free space on the game board. When all pieces are set, the players beginn to move their pieces one space per turn. If a piece is enclosed, so that it can't move, its face is revealed. It's eliminated from the game and the encloser gets the indicated number of points, but only if a black inscripted piece is caught on a space with a black center or a white inscripted one on a space with a white center. If this is not the case, the enclosed piece is put on any other free space of it's players choice. When a 6-eyed piece is eliminated, it's player too is eliminated from the game.

This game was published in the 1930s or 1940s.

The players set their playing pieces face down on the spaces of their color on the game board. Only the spaces with the number 10 are left free. When all pieces are set, the faces are revealed. Now the players have to move their pieces to get them on the spaces of the same number. Spaces of other colors can't be moved on. Neutral spaces can be used to block the opponents. The first player to get all his pieces on the right spaces wins the game.

Objective: be the first reaching space 70 (catching Tom, an orang-outan)
 - smple 'Roll&Move' game - 
acting by turns
Tom (an orang-outan, kept as prisoner/domestic animal in a palace) has escaped. Rolling two dice (with special symbols) 5 pawns (players) have to catch the break-away; depending on dice combinations pawns/orang outan have to be moved; ending on marked spaces follow instructions.

notes:
likely published before WWI;
cute pewter pawns, funky story behind

Hounds of War Mk. II is a update to the original standardized skirmishing miniatures rules system which allows the players (with maximum flexibility during the CREATION phase) to be able to take ANY 25-30mm figures, assign them the game values the players WANT, and be able to bring them into play. With Hounds of War Mk. II you will be able to pit World War II Nazi Storm Troopers against your favorite Goblin Hoards, or even Star Wars© Storm Troopers!

This is a stand-alone set of rules, so no other rules are necessary for play. In addition,  you WILL need your own miniatures, a LOT of 10-sided dice (d10), marking chits, 3D terrain, rulers marked in inches, and your imagination to play this game.

Instead of being a "you-go-I-go" turn sequence, an initiative deck is used  and cards assigned to individual units to determine when units perform actions are performed in a turn. This creates a fast paced game in which players are constantly active.

Hounds of War Mk. II is the culmination of many post convention evaluations, fan suggestions, and clarifications. Inside you will find many clarified and expanded rules, as well as prebuilt armies, a campaign system suggestion, a victory point system, counter sheets, and much more. We thank all of you who have taken an active part in the growth of this system.

Objective: Collect as many bonus points as possible by crossing a 11 x 11 board (+ start/target positions) like Halma by
1. capturing other meeples 2. fast finishing 
 - acting by turns -

Players (2 or 4) move their meeples (vertical/horizontal/diagonal) out of starting position upon a 11 x 11 grid board. The board is divided into 4 areas, pictured with Skat-cards; starting from such a place you may move or jump trying to come closer to your finish positions; if you jump over opponent's meeple(s) you will capture them - but this isn't allowed if your starting position has a lower or equal value (according Skat). You may jump in all directions ('chain-jump') starting from a Jack-(=Jungs)space. Transition spaces between the 4 areas have additional restrictions/advantages. The earlier you finish the higher the bonus points. Game ends after last player reaches target positions.

Note:
Likely published during 1930's

Prototype is a new Mechanism Card for the Mars Needs Mechanics game.  Just add it into the Mechanism Card deck at the beginning of the game.  This card costs two Scrap and provides the following ability:

"At the end of a round after prices change, you may break this mechanism down and sell both scrap. If you do, sell one for the value of the highest valued component, and the the other for the value of the lowest valued component."

Note:  This card was included in the Unpub 4 Promo Card Pack.

Own it all as a high-flying trader in the fast-paced world of real estate. Tour the city for the hottest properties: sites, stations and utilities are all up for grabs. Invest in houses and hotels, then watch the rent come pouring in. Make deals with other players and look out for bargains at auction—there are many ways to get what you want. And for really speedy dealers, use the speed die for a quick and intense game of Marquette-opoly. So get on Go and trade your way to success.

Players move around the gameboard buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels, and collecting rent from their opponents, the ultimate goal being to drive them into bankruptcy.  The winner is the remaining player left after all the others have gone bankrupt.

boardgame: Corps Command: Dawn's Early Light – Red Hammer
description:
Now you command Soviet and NATO forces during the first battles of the Third World War.  Seven scenarios give you the opportunity to explore the desperate fighting in the Eisenbach Gap and Gerzenburg.  Dawn's Early Light: Red Hammer is the 2nd expanded edition of Dawn's Early Light and now includes the High Noon at Gerzenberg expansion from Line of Fire magazine #10. Dawn's Early Light Red Hammer is set in a hypothetical 1985 as World War 3 starts.  The game uses variable initiative, support element chit pulls, and a novel combat resolution mechanic to create a game modeling the lethality of the modern battlefield with a minimum of rules overhead.

Dawn's Early Light Red Hammer has been refined, corrected, and polished.  This edition has updated game manual, counters, player aid card and map.  The game map is now printed on a heavy paper stock in vibrant color and also includes a newly designed full-color box.  Players can control forces of America, Canada, West Germany and the Soviet Union.

Dawn's Early Light Red Hammer is easy to learn and play in an evening, so get ready to play.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Operation World Party Gaming introduces GETSOME, GETSOME: Bad Decisions, and GETSOME: Ladies night, which are a series of naughty games for normal people.

GETSOME is a 2-4 'player' non-collectible card game, expandable up to 5-6 players with purchase of the expansion, Bad Decisions, and the deck can be enlarged through purchase of the first booster, Ladies Night. The goal is simple: earn yourself 10 Bed Notches while avoiding the consequences of your actions such as lost turns, Clingy Exes, STDs, and babies. The dialogue is both horrible and hilarious. Play is quick as everyone has the ability to play cards on another person's turn. A player will have seven cards in their hand and can GETSOME, become PREGGOS, gain a Status Effect, heal a Status Effect, or block a player from a GETSOME or PREGGOS encounter. Don't forget to call in your Wingman when the time is right.

The heart of the game is the story telling, where you'll create the details of your deeds and how you overcame difficult challenges presented by your so-called friends in pursuit of your next conquest. Your evening will be filled with buddies, belly laughs, and blushing cheeks of the newly acquainted.

The heart of the replayability is in the multifunction design of many of the cards to perform different tasks depending on your needs. There are an unlimited number of ways for rounds to play out. There's an amazing breadth of crazy things you'll hear yourself say over the course of the night, and a card for just about every occasion. Once you GETSOME, you're definitely going to want to GETSOME again.

A print'n play, pit and peebles inspired African themed game. During the medieval times, kingdoms and empires rise and fall in the African continent.

The object of the game is simple, it uses a mancala/oware/awele style mechanic.

As a player, take control of one of the four kingdoms, rise, expand, and collect resources before falling.

You will do so using a "pit and pebbles" gaming mechanic, sowing your cubes and harvesting with the last cube you sow.

You will also use cubes from new comers (Europeans, Chinese, Persians, Arabs...) as Africa become a land of conquest. 
You can sow and harvest player cubes with these cubes, but you can't harvest new comers' cubes.

So new comers will slowly gain control of the land as players use them, and players' kingdoms will fall.

At the end of the game, when there are no more players cubes, the winner is the player who has harvested the most cubes.

The game is for 2-4 players, from 6 y.o. The board is language independent, rules are in French, soon in English.

Objective: score most goals (450 points or more) during a 2 x 6 matches lasting game
 - acting by turns - 
2 or 4 players (in this case you need two sets/games with 4 different colours) are dealt 5 cards each (from their own 32 cards deck); trying to score most points in a trick-taking procedure you earn bonus points regarding the values of the cards (within their won tricks); special card combinations (center-forward & goal & Joker) give additional points. After each trick you must refill your hand.

notes: likely published mid/end 1930's due to Joker card 'Sindelar': Team captain of the Austrian Wunderteam - died Jan. 1939 under mysterious circumstances

boardgame: Collectors Battlefield Wargaming System: D-Day – Battle of Carentan
description:
This is the first module of the Collector's Battlefield Wargaming System, a 'turn key' quick play set of rules with a full line of 28mm prepainted figures and vehicle.

CB: Carentan is a skirmish level miniatures wargame- that is, it represents combat at its most basic level of fighting: man to man.

CB:Carentan puts the player in the role of an infantry squad or platoon leader.  Firefights break out in 'mad minutes', followed by lulls where the player exercises leadership by rallying troops, bringing in reserves, and maneuvering his troops to set up for the next firefight.  Firefights center on the machine gun- placement of your MG42's and .30cals are crucial to glorious success or ignominious defeat!

Scale: 
1 figure = 1 soldier or vehicle
1 inch = 2 yards
1 turn = approximately 30 seconds
Combat Lull = 2 to 10 minutes.

Keeping track of units stats is made easy with quick reference cards included with every pack, detailing the capabilities of every squad, leader, heavy weapon and vehicle.  Squad level games are fast and furious, taking about 60 minutes to an hour and half.

Card game in which each player is a gardener who tries to get the best variety of flowers to grow in his garden.

There are flower cards and accessory cards, such as seeds, sunshine, water etc. Different kinds of flowers need different combinations of accessories and yield different point values.

The active player can draw cards, trade cards with open cards on the table, plant flowers and trade with the other players. The first player to plant ten points worth of flowers is the winner.

The forces of light and dark are running a tournament, with the winner ruling the earth for the next 100 years.

The game consists of cards showing magicians, spells, items and missions. Each player has a magician card with a special ability, and a mission card with another special activity. The active player can play cards with spells or items and use the abilities of his/her magician/mission cards. Other players can interfere with counterspells or special items. The goal is to gain three tokens, either individually (in a 2 or 3 player game) or as a team (in a 4 player game). The first player or team who gets the 3 tokens is the winner.

The players are monsters and have to escape from the monster hunters. This is achieved by elimination: Every monster which gathers 7 or more hunter points is out of the game. Last monster standing is the winner.
Each player has a monster card and draws two action cards into his/her hands, then three hunter cards which are placed openly in front of him/her. Now each player can play action cards on two of the hunters and the monster card can attack the third one. Some action cards can take hunters out of the game while others can redirect them to other players (there is no defense against this). When all actions are resolved, the player with most hunter points has to keep the highest hunter card. When after a few rounds the number of hunter points a player has gathered exceeds 7, that player is out. The others continue untili only one monster is left.

The game is played on a board with 16 numbered chips face down in three rows. The objective is to force your opponent to remove the last chip on the board. Players take turns removing up to 4 chips from any one row until only one chip is left. The player who removes the last chip scores the value on that chip. The first player to accumulate 30 or more point loses the game.

From the game box: Fast-paced family game is easy to play, tricky to win! Payers must count, strategize & think ahead!

The game is played on a board 10 squares by 9. Each player has 9 pieces which being placed side by side in two lines along one diagonal. The objective is to be the first player to move their 9 pieces into a 3 by 3 square in their corner of the game board.

From the game box: Slide carefully along the ground to the sacred Aztec temple ... Keep and eye on your enemy, overcome obstacles, and make sure all the members of your tribe reach the safety of the temple.

1. Assemble the Aztec warriors in diagonal formation along the center of the board according to tribal colors.
2. Slide a warrior along a row or column until he encounters a boundary or another warrior.
3. Plan the best route, slide the warriors forward and help them enter the sacred temple.
4. Be the first player to slide his/her entire tribe of nine Aztec warriors into the temple ... and WIN!

Similar to Outwit, which introduces power chips that can move diagonally, and can stop whenever they want (but must still stop for the required reasons). Outwit also has a rule about not leaving your corner once you enter it.

YOU are an apprentice witch. You can procure magic from a variety of forest resources. You can create magic called "mug", and use it to carry out the extraction of "Mana" - the energy of nature. Who can hold the most excellent performance?! Please be careful so as not to get in the way of resource procurement earnestly. It is a card game that anyone can play easily!

- From the Magic Forest and Black Cat Rola

This card game uses trick taking mechanics. The suit color of the card winning each trick becomes the trump suit of the next trick. Cards earned become magical resources and can be combined to score bonus points.

Translated from description on publisher's website.

This is Liar Kingdom. Funny people lie for the good of the kingdom, but the poor citizens despise them for their lies. YOU are the authority of darkness, dominate in the shadows of this crazy kingdom. Even the king is afraid of you. Nobles and slaves alike will die not even know your existence. You are invincible and your power is absolute, but one question has been asked that might change the kingdom's fate: 
"Who among the kingdom's people has the most power?" 
 Using only three cards and three "declaration chips," play a game to earn victory points faster than your opponents.

Each player plays one of their three cards face down and one of their three chips face up on top of the card. The face up chip can be the true identity of their played card or a lie to try to trick the other players into believing what they are playing. There is an optional round where players can change the chip on their card to a different one. Then the cards are revealed and the way they interact decides who won the round and receives VP. Also, if you were lying or telling the truth with your chip determines if you receive or lose additional VP.

Designer's summary:
暗殺者、王様、奴隷のカードを１枚ずつ持ってゲームをします。プレイヤーは順番に手番を行います。自分の出すカードがどれかをチップを出して宣言する。任意のカードを裏向きで出す。全員がそれを行ったら勝敗の判定を行います。自分の出したカードが次のとおりだった場合、勝利します。暗殺者を出して王様がいた場合、それを１人敗北させて勝利します。王様を出して自分を殺す暗殺者がいなかった場合、奴隷を全員敗北させて勝利します。奴隷を出して王様がいなかった場合に勝利します。勝利したプレイヤーは得点を獲得します。勝利しなかったプレイヤーは全員敗北。宣言と異なるカードを出していたプレイヤーはマイナス点を獲得します。誰かが８点以上を獲得するとゲーム終了。得点の高いプレイヤーの勝利です。

Each player has 3 cards and 3 tokens, each of which represents Royalty, Assassin, and Slave. Starting player declares a card he will be playing by placing corresponding token face-up, then plays one card face-down. He may lie about the card he's playing. Then next player does the same, and so on. When all players are done, reveal all cards they played and resolve the outcome. If one or more Royalties are in play, each Assassin in play kills one of them. Successful Assassins win the round. After the assassination, if one or more Royalties are still in play, survived Royalties win the round, otherwise all Slaves win the round. Winners get points, and players who have lied and still didn't win the round lose points. A game ends when a player gains total of 8 points. A player with the most points wins the game.

Squarit is a played on a board containing 16 squares each surrounded by four spaces for tiles, with each pair of adjacent squares sharing one tile space. Each tile space has a specific tile that can be placed on it. The squares are numbered from 4-64 by fours. The edges of the board are also numbered, from 1-4.

The game can be played in two ways. In the aptly-named "Game of Chance" two to five players take turns drawing a tile and placing it on its designated space. The player placing the fourth tile around a square scores the value of the square multiplied by the value of the edge of the board corresponding to the played tile.

The "Game of Skill" is for two players. The scoring rule is the same, but here each player starts with half the blocks and can choose which one to play, generally speaking. If three sides of a block have been played this forces the fourth side to be played as soon as possible, and if that is the third tile for another square it forces that fourth tile immediately, etc.

The designer patented the game some years before publishing it.

Note that the board shown in the patent is not exactly the same as the published version, as the rules state the squares in each quarter of the board sum to 136.

Game of Defense: Pacific is a light wargame. It is for two sides (U.S. and "Asiatic"), each of whom has five ships each of three types (battleships, cruisers, and submarines). The ships are deployed on a map of the Pacific with a grid divided into red and white squares.

On a turn a player moves one piece (or possibly more than one, as the rules are a bit unclear). Battleships and cruisers can move several squares on a turn but are restricted to squares of a particular color. Submarines only move one square a turn but can use either color. Submarines capture enemy ships by landing on them, but the larger ships capture by jumping.

Each side starts the game by mining ten squares on their shores; when an enemy ship crosses such a square a card is drawn which may have a bad effect.

Each side has four objective cards which they randomly select one of at the start of the game, keeping it hidden from the opponent(s). The game is won by the side achieving its objective first.

The game can be played by more than two players by dividing one or both sides between players.

Minimal Space Combat is a game of tactical space ship battle. The players design ships and then can battle to the death. It may be used with any scifi background as it has a very minimal theme. Players plot movement and then move simultaneously. Combat can occur at any time during movement when opposing ships are in range and in the weapons firing arc. The goal is simply to destroy the other player's ships.

Timothy Swenson describes the reasoning for designing this game as follows: 
"The premise behind this game is this: what is the simplest playable game possible to game tactical space combat. The game must make some reasonable attempt to come close to simulating space combat and not just the simplest possible game. The emphasis in this game is fast and easy. Playability is given more consideration than realism. There is no consideration for expansion of these rules, because then it would not be minimal. Use the game for those times when you want a quick and dirty space combat game or for introducing someone new to gaming."

In addition to the basic game Timothy also has minimal rules for a campaign game, a method for playing solo and a tutorial for introducing new players to space combat games.

SeaDogs is a fast paced family game for 2-6 players, ages 10 and up. Players take on the rolls of Pirate captains taking part in a competition to gather the most Gold and Treasure.  The Captain with the most Gold after all is said and done is the victor.

Sail into a port, and get gold by selling any cargo you have earned from your acts of piracy.  Pillage ports to gain cargo, but once a pirate pillages a port they must move to another Port to take any more actions.

There is no honor among Pirates, and it's every Captain for themselves on the high seas.  Players may attack each other and steal Cargo, Gold, or even Treasure that has been found on mysterious islands in the area.  Pirates who take plunder from other players get a Bounty on their heads that goes to the next Pirate to take them down a peg or two.

Sea Dogs has a whimsical style.  The world it takes place in is populated by animals.  The base game focuses on dogs, but who knows what other animals are out there.

SeaDogs is a quick playing game that is fun for kids and adults.  And it is designed to be kid friendly so everyone has a chance of winning, while still having enough depth that adults can play together and enjoy it as well.

boardgame: Katsu Curry?! This is not the time for that!
description: It is currently an era of worldwide economic deflation. The politicians of your country seek to understand the common people. Players become the leaders of an opposition party who have taken it upon themselves to reestablish economic responsibility. Each player will bid to enforce a "fair and folksy" price of a variety of goods and services. Each time a player sets a price, they will earn honor points. The winner, and new political leader, is the player who earns the most points.
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Monopoly set in Landshut, a German city in the Bavarian region.

Silent Operative adds MERCS from the FCC House 9 faction to MERCS: Recon. Included are five House 9 MERCS and their corresponding player boards and special rules. In addition, the mission pack contains new missions and a new tile.

Commercial game, related to Monopoly, for the purpose of promoting the city of "Baden-Baden", in the Southwest of Germany.

The players move around a board, which consists of 40 squares. On most of the squares are parts of companies, which can be bought. The companies have values between 1000 and 8000. Three companies are combined by a color. Whenever a player lands on a win&loss-square, he must draw a card and the wins and losses of the business year are settled. According to the outcome the players gain money or have to pay money for their companies.
As soon as one player goes bankrupt, the game ends, all company cards are sold for their original price. Player with most money is the winner.

Commercial game, related to Monopoly, for the purpose of promoting the city of "Münster", in the West of Germany.

The players move around a board, which consists of 40 squares. On most of the squares are parts of companies, which can be bought. The companies have values between 1000 and 8000. Three companies are combined by a color. Whenever a player lands on a win&loss-square, he must draw a card and the wins and losses of the business year are settled. According to the outcome the players gain money or have to pay money for their companies.
As soon as one player goes bankrupt, the game ends, all company cards are sold for their original price. Player with most money is the winner.

As soon as one player goes bankrupt, the game ends, all company cards are sold for their original price. Player with most money is the winner.

As the economy of Madeira flourishes, the guilds are expanding and sending ambassadors to distant countries to strengthen their influence. Madeira: The Ambassadors enriches the game with four new guild favors that are all related to the colonies, bringing new strategies to interacting with them.

Commercial game, related to Monopoly, for the purpose of promoting the city of "Würzburg", in the South of Germany.

The players move around a board, which consists of 40 squares. On most of the squares are parts of companies, which can be bought. The companies have values between 1000 and 8000. Three companies are combined by a color. Whenever a player lands on a win&loss-square, he must draw a card and the wins and losses of the business year are settled. According to the outcome the players gain money or have to pay money for their companies.
As soon as one player goes bankrupt, the game ends, all company cards are sold for their original price. Player with most money is the winner.

boardgame: Mage Wars: Forged in Fire – Spell Tome Expansion
description: Mage Wars: Forged in Fire introduces two alternate Mages with unique abilities — the Adramelech Warlock and the Warlord of the Anvil Throne — to Mage Wars, and it includes multiple copies of the 41 new spells featured in this set, such as the legendary "Sersiryx" and the "Harshforge Monolith".
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The goal of the game is to shoot the heart of the opponent with Amor's arrow. The game pieces are the heart, Amor, the devil, 3 male guardian hearts, 7 female guardian hearts and 2 impetuous hearts. They are used to protect the player's heart and impede the opponent's Amor. To do this, the pieces can move in specific ways (like in Chess) and can block or eliminate one another. The devil is the only piece, that can eliminate Amor. When Amor gets in a straight line with another's heart, he can shoot it and wins the game. The game can be played by 2 or 4 players. In the game for 4 players, the players at the opposite sides of the game board play against one another.

The Game was most likely published in the 1920s.

A thrilling race between a skilled motorist, a jealous pedestrian and a beautiful girl: Each player has 3 meeples: a car, a girl and a man. The goal is to get all 3 meeples around the game board and back into their own garage. The opposite players move in opposite directions. The moves of the meeples are determined by a spinner. Whenever the different kinds of meeples meet, different things happen. The girl enters every car it meets and may change cars if the oportunity arrives. The man gets every girl to leave the car. If a car with a girl of another color enters its garage, the girl has to begin anew. The spinner can send the car or the girl back to its starting point too, if it shows "Weine nicht" (= don't cry).

This game was published in the 1920s or 1930s.

Fast-Paced Strategy Game that Allows You to Play as a Terrorist Setting Bombs Off in Europe or Operatives Trying to Stop Him...

A terrorist is wreaking havoc in Europe. Every time the terrorist leaves a city there is the possibility he has left a bomb, trap, or recruiting cell. If Interpol does not find the bomb before it detonates, then the terrorist makes progress toward his goal of instilling fear across Europe.

The job of Interpol is to coordinate the efforts of Secret Agents, Bomb Squad, Special Forces, and an Informant Network to stop the terrorist from accomplishing his goal. It will take the combined special abilities of each Operative and the Informant Network for Interpol to win.

During the game, one player plays as the terrorist and tries to elude the operatives chasing him. With hidden movement cards, traps, bombs and recruiting cells to use, he tries to outmaneuver his opponents and stay one step ahead of them. The operatives goal is to catch the terrorist and defeat him in combat. Along the way, they want to avoid traps, defuse bombs, and defeat any recruiting cells the terrorist puts in their way. But the operatives have to be careful. If they get too spread out, the terrorist might attack and defeat them while they are isolated.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Phileas Fogg & Co up to four players bet on Fogg's attempt to travel around the world in 80 days. All players determine the speed of the journey by plaing cards. You win, if Fogg and his companions move according to your role. The own role is kept secret and can be changed. There are four different roles: Phileas Fogg wants to arrive exactly in time, Passepartout wants to arrive as soon as possible, detective Fix wants to arrive as late as possible, Jules Vernes is interested in an exciting race.

The game is part of a scientific publication about Jules Verne's Around the World in 80 Days. The box contains the game and a book with 80 essays on Verne's novel. The name of the whole box is: Weltnetzwerke – Weltspiele. Ein Buch und ein Spiel zu Jules Vernes 'In 80 Tagen um die Welt, edited by Passepartout, Konstanz 2013.

Players use dice to navigate around the board to the finish line. Along the way, Fact Cards or questions regarding the Salmon Life Cycle educate the players.

Targeted for children in Grades K-5, this game takes players through the salmon life cycle, from egg-laying to spawning. A fun way to learn salmon biology, including the dangers they face from natural predators, food supply, weather, and human influence.

boardgame: History Maker Baseball: Century League Card Set
description:
Expansion set of fictional players, umpires, and ballparks, based on 1940s era baseball, for History Maker Baseball.

The Century League Card Set contains 252 cards, enough for eight to ten teams. You can arrange the players any way you like, or you can use the numerical team assignments we've included on each card.

The set also gives you 32 different umpires and a set of eighteen different ball park cards, each with its own unique set of qualities.

boardgame: Race to Adventure: Strange Travels Rules
description: Race to Adventure: Strange Travels Rules is an official download from Evil Hat Games that provides rules and components for solitaire and 6 player play, 2 new variants, plus alternative board set-ups.

The title is more recognisable to English speakers as
"Eeny Meeny Miney Moe
Catch a ****** by his toe
If he wriggles, let him go
Eeny Meeny Miney Moe"

Card game. 32 cards in four sets of eight. Each set is in the same colour and is numbered from one to eight.

Four cards are dealt to each player and 4 are turned up in the middle of the table. The rest are placed in a stack to the side. Each turn a player may exchange one card from his hand with one from the middle. Or, he can take all four cards with his hand. After everyone has had a turn and nobody has exchanged a card, the four in the middle are replaced by four new cards.

The object is to collect together 4 cards of the same colour or four cards of any colours that have four numbers in consecutive order.

If you achieve either of the two objectives, you lay down your hand. All the other players now reveal their hands. For each card not in any order, they score one to three points. In effect, this means that they will score one to three points. After nine rounds of play, the player with the highest score, loses.

boardgame: The Game of Rat and Dragon
description:
The Game of Rat and Dragon is a 2 player abstract strategy game played on a 6-by-6 square grid.

Each player has four Characters made of wooden cubes; a Rat (a single cube), a Dragon (3 cubes in an L shape) and two Soldiers (two cubes each).

At the start of the game each player builds a cube out of their Characters in the 2-by-2 squares marked on opposite corners of the board. Players then take turns moving their Characters on the board.

The aim of the game is to build a cube of Characters in the central 2-by-2 "Sun Square" or to capture one of the opponent's Characters.

Opposing characters can be captured, or trapped, by covering enough of their square faces with faces of your own characters.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – The Prize of the North
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Every faction finds new tools to hatch intrigues and win battles in The Prize of the North, the fifth Chapter Pack in the "Conquest and Defiance" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. New Bannerman characters open possibilities for multi-House decks, and fourteen out of twenty cards (three copies of each) in this expansion possess the new Prized keyword. Whether players man the Warships of House Greyjoy, march to the Wall with House Baratheon, or join another Great House, they'll find plenty of new paths to power.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Plants is a board game that attempts to model the life of plants from their point of view in a specific, easily playable format. Plants is also a modular, organic game system that can grow and adapt beyond a single game board or a set of rules. The game is not meant to be an educational simulation, but more like an open creative project that deliberately intertwines certain biological concepts with suitable board game mechanisms.

During the game, the players continuously move through several different types of phases and perform actions at their own pace. They must try to adapt to the whims of the competing players and manipulate the upcoming situations to help them pollinate their flowers and gather enough nutrients and energy to produce seeds. At same time the players may sprout from the seeds, use the nutrients to grow in the habitat area and be forced to use up their energy to get rid of pesky larvae. Having enough water available is always a concern, and sometimes the players must accept losing their precious biomass. And if the players are not careful, they might even be eliminated from the game completely.

Plants features no randomness after a simple setup and there are no auctions, negotiations or trading. It has an open spatial element with shared resources and some three-dimensional play. The actions are short and the turns go by quickly and the possible butterfly effects keep situations tense. Clever planning and timing is required for success, and in the end only the number of seeds matter.

Fabrica de Software is a game designed for IT Managers to simulate software development concepts. Also possible to see some Project management, Scrum, SOA, and other software development concepts.

In this game, each team will produce software. They need to have resources, programmers and time to make the desired software. Each software requires a certain amount of time, some resources, and programmers to be completed. Teams receive money according to software complexity. The team with the most money wins.

From the package:

Spill the critters
take your pick
find the hippo
Do it QUICK!

The game pieces are 24 plastic animals packaged in a plastic tube.

There are 2 ways to play the game.

- Dump all the animals onto a table. The first player to find the hippo wins.

- Put all the animals in a bag. The first player to find the hippo wins.

Another animal may be picked to vary the game.

Contactics is a business card with a set of rules which allow you to play an area control boardgame in which the components are other business cards.

Each player's stack of business cards is shuffled into a deck, and a hand of 6 is drawn. The list on the back of Contactics uses a sequence of four questions to define which effects your card has. Each turn a player plays a card, and its effect takes place. 
The game ends when the board (3x3 grid) is full, and the player who conquered the most grid spaces wins.

It's meant to aid in networking.

Abstract game.
There are 49 double-sided pieces on the board, each consisting of 2 pieces glued together. One color is up, the other one is down. The active player replaces one of the pieces with the 50th piece, trying to create a line of exactly three adjacent pieces of the same color. The other player contiunues with the replaced piece. The player who cannot create a correct line, loses the game.

From the instruction sheet:

The object of Konfusion! is to "make it through the day." We've all had one of those days...sometimes they last a week! By moving logically and with planning, one can get through a simple day with ease...Right?  Well...at least once in a while. But all days are not that simple -- as one trip around this board will show you. It will be your logic and luck that will get you through one of those KONFUSING days. The first player to reach the end of the day (the finish line space) may earn that coveted three (or five or 52) week vacation AND does win the game of Konfusion.

The Battle of San Fermo took place the 27 May 1859 at a mountain pass near lake Como in northern Italy.  It was an engagement during the Second Italian War of Independence in which the Italian volunteers of Giuseppe Garibaldi's Hunters of the Alps defeated an Austrian force, forcing the Austrians to abandon the town of Como.

San Fermo is the first wargame of the N-SB (Small Battles) series and is also the first one dedicated to the Italian Risorgimento. Players can play both the phases of the battle or the entire battle with optional rules for better Austrian training and troop control.

Based on activation chits and operative orders, the game system is centered on the totally different battleground of Italian theatre that forced both armies to fight battles "hand to mouth" sending in company after company. Counters represent single companies, cavalry squadrons and half batteries.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Age of Space is an economic space colonization "spaceship placement" game.

Age of Space continues the story of "Ryce: Empire of Sand". After exploiting the new energy resource "Ryce" on Mars, the manship is ready for the journey to the stars. You take the role of a space flight nation and your order is to colonize new planets.

The players build spaceships and use them to explore new planets and build colonies on them. Colonies produce raw materials or make further processing of goods (example: water and chemicals is processed to medicine). Goods can have different conditions (piece good, bulk goods, fluid goods). The player have to transport these goods from one colony to another or sell them to the market. There are different ships for the different kinds of goods.

Players can get influence points for money, for delivery of goods, exploring new planets, the most colonies on one planet, the majority of colonies of one kind of good, and for the most cargo area of one kind of good. The player with the most influence points at the end of the game wins.

Hunted is a tabletop Tactical miniatures game in which your faction fights to survive! Whether it is small skirmishes or flat out war, Hunted can be played with 2 players or as many players as you like.

In Hunted, you control units from one of four Factions, which are portrayed with 28mm scale models:

- United Terran Defence Force, humans who are fighting to survive on an unknown planet.

- SESHIMAI (SEed SHIp Modulator AI, often shortened to MAI), AI control robotics whose corrupted data cores have listed all organic life as needing to be destroyed rather than protected.

- Predators, defending their territory from new and strange beings.

- Herbivores, fending off new predators and old in order to continue to live.

Players build their own armies with models in each faction and build a deck to complement it, then take turns in order to wipe out their opponents models. 
Unlike traditional miniature games, there is limited use of dice within the game, with no dice rolls to hit or save your miniatures; instead you rely on your cards.

Each player has 8 ships with different speed and weapons range. The ships move on the grids on the game board. The goal is to steal the treasure from one of the home harbors of the opponent with one of the two pirate ships. It can be arranged before the game, if the winner only has to reach one of the opponents harbors, or if he has to return to his own Harbor afterwards.

boardgame: Smash Up: The Big Geeky Box
description:
Smash Up: The Big Geeky Box is a storage case for Smash Up cards that also contains a single faction deck for the game: "The Geeks", based on the characters and shows on the online Geek and Sundry channel, including TableTop.

"The Geeks" faction (as well as Smash Up: All Stars) are also included in Smash Up: The Bigger Geekier Box, released August 20, 2018.

family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Pirates of the Caribbean Dead Man's Chest: 3 in 1 Pirate Games Collector's Edition
description:
Yo ho, yo ho, it's a pirate's life for YOU! Stow away with friends anjd mateys for hours of high seas merriment with a treasure trove of pirate fun--Chess, Checkers and Pirates Dice!


     32 custom chess pieces.



     Die-cast metal checker pieces.



     4 pirate cups and 20 dice as seen in the movie!

Shogun Showdown is a trick taking game that puts a whole new spin on the genre with a dexterity element. The game begins with cards being dealt to each player evenly and then hands are played in rounds with each player playing 1 card. There are 3 suits: Red, Black, and White. In between rounds of card play (similar to Rook or Euchre), players take turns flicking cards at the winner's temple. Points are awarded on both card play and card flicking.

Simple and fun, Shogun Showdown is a nice break from conventional games that have you sitting for long periods of time.

Storyteller Cards are a playing card deck, designed to inspire creativity in gamers, role-players, writers, artists, teachers, parents and children. They were designed by Jason Tagmire and illustrated by Campbell Whyte.

Each card features an illustration with 4 unique elements. There is a character, holding an item, completing an action, in a location. Each of these elements can be used to create something new, get you out of a mid-project slump, or just to have some creative fun with family and friends.

The cards also feature additional icons in the corners to help you dig a little deeper into storytelling, creating, and gaming. The icons represent a rank, suit, mood, season, letter, and color.


The Storyteller Manual includes rules for 16 different ways to play. From strategic coloring to push-your-luck storytelling, there are game variations for everyone. Many variants are story based gaining points for each card played, best poker hands and matching card elements, but others include strategic coloring (where players must complete illustration quadrants to earn points), and endless possibilities (where the players create and change the rules in a race to 30 points).

3-Kaart Trio-Spel contains actually two different games: one like Quartett and one like Memory. In both games sets of 3 cards should be collected. Each set forms a complete figure being one of the Dutch football players representing The Netherlands at the 1978 Football World Cup in Argentina. The game contains cards for completing 18 players.

Antediluvian Wars - Extermination, "AWE" for short, is a tactical card game for 2 - 4 players.

AWE is non-collectible but very much expandable. The base set will consist four pre-built decks, 30 cards each, so it is playable right out of the box and thus non-customizable. However new, additional sets will add deck customization as an option as well.

In AWE, each player assumes the role of a mighty god ruling over one out of four fascinating factions, the goal of the game being to breach the opponent defenses and destroy all the opponent guardians in order to "dethrone" their god. An undefended attack will lead to the destruction of one guardian and if there are none left, a direct attack dethrones the opponent god, spelling defeat for that opponent.

AWE is set in a historically not at all accurate prehistory, where four advanced, lost civilizations - Atlantis, Hyperborea, Lemuria and Mu - vie for ultimate domination. The game will feature awesome and fantastic untis you can send into battle such as Raptor and Pterodactyl Riders, Zombie T-Rex's reanimated by black magic, advanced Vimana aircraft, War Mammoths and much more, as well as a pleasant mix of unique and exciting mechanics.

This game is a collaboration between talented Indonesian artist Widodo Pangarso and Austrian Game Designer Andreas Propst.

Brullende Sequenties is a children’s game in which they learn the meaning of the word “sequences” and become familiar with the terms “before”, “after” en “finally”. Players connect  jigsaw puzzle-shaped pieces with scenes from the movie Cars on it in order to complete a logical sequence. Adults can help the children by telling the accompanying stories which come with the game. The game provides 6 different sequences of 9 scenes each, which are combined if played with 2-6 players. On their turn players choose a piece from the table and check whether it can be connected with the previous/starting piece of their sequence or not. If so, they can keep the piece, otherwise they has to put it back on the table. The first player to finish his sequence wins the game.

Elemental Clash HEX gives you the "classic" Elemental Clash CCG experience - gone HEX!

Elemental Clash HEX is a hex-tile-based, fully customizable fantasy game for 2 - 4 players.

The goal of the game is to run your opponent out of tiles in their magical bag of hex tiles (this serves as the equivalent of a deck in a regular, card based CCG) before they can do so to you. For each damage a player takes, that player discards a random hex from their magical bag. If a player is required to draw a hex from the bag and cannot do so, they lose immediately.

While emulating many key mechanics of the original Elemental Clash Customizable Card Game, such as deck depletion as the win condition and the beloved-by-many "Boon" mechanic, Elemental Clash HEX is a whole new experience and twist on the original game, as many mechanics are new, fresh and unique, taking advantage of what the medium hex-tiles can offer.

Furthermore, the game features stunning artwork by a talented cast of international artists, elevating the game on state-of-the-art level when it comes to visuals as well.

Passport to Adventure is an imaginative play and storytelling board game that develops creativity, critical thinking, active listening, and expressiveness.

Players choose heroes with unique powers and move through a fantasy realm where they must collect treasure cards (or avoid pitfall), used to defeat various monsters through imaginative storytelling. A player (or team) wins when she navigates to the final battle and defeats the boss monster.

In every village you enter, you find inhabited ruins. You hide in them when you need shelter. You search them for food, for clothing, for equipment. You make business there and you rest. Ruins are your whole life.

Ruins contains forty new cards — including new Leaders, Locations and Contact cards — for use with either 51st State or The New Era (or both). The Ruins expansion introduces new abilities for cards, including the possibility of recovering discarded cards. New locations not only provide storage for resources, but they provide other ones, too.

Cartoon is a 3 players simulation boardgame of a cartoonish situation. The players are a dog, a cat and a mouse - each one a pwn moving on the squared-grid map of a house with garden. During the 61-75 turns of the game, the dog will try to put the cat KO and keep order, the mouse witt try to put the cat KO and stay in the kitchen as long as possible, and the cat will try to put both opponents KOas much as possible. Objects can be taken, thrown and broken in the process.

Each turn, each player secretly choose an action: attack, move, take an object, drop or throw an object and so on. Actions are executed in a strict order according to the turn structure.

First Published on Kaos magazine 1.5 (Nexus Editrice, 1994). Available since 2014 as print'n'play by 2 Cents Boardgames.

From the official 2014 presentation:
Dear old Granny says goodbye to her pets, Trusty the dog, and the cat Scamp, and peacefully goes to the grocery store.

She doesn't think of staying away much: 75 turns at most.

Meanwhile, Nibble, the mouse, has decided to pay a visit to the kitchen, thereby causing a ruckus: cat and dog have some old scores to settle ...

A Print & Play boardgame for three players inspired by classic cartoon animations, fast and easy but rigorous as a simulation.

boardgame: Drill It: The Print & Play Game
description:
DRILL IT is a 2 player game were players collect gold by digging deeper and deeper into the ground. The first player that collects the most gold is the MASTER driller.

Every turn u drill deeper into the earth, u have 2 options where u can choose from to keep the big gold away from the other player !!!

If your hit to many rocks, your game over !!!

DRILL IT is an easy to fabricate (30 minutes), fast to learn little game with tactical decisions to make.

A set of five games played with musical instruments.  In "Deep in the Ding Dong Forest," players compete to turn over matching tiles and be the first to place three different baby animals in their mouse homes.

Publisher's description:

Play along with the My Very First Games: Ding Dong Forest from HABA! Every animal of the Ding Dong Forest knows how to play an instrument. Only little mouse Mia cannot decide which instrument she wants to play. So one by one, she visits her friends and tries their instruments. In five very different games, children become acquainted with simple rhythms and different sounds while, at the same time, their musicality, creativity, and imagination is enhanced! A musical game collection for children ages 2 and up!

Arsenal: Arena Combat is a 2-6 player customizable card game that uses a hex-map game board, tokens or miniature mechs, counters and 6-sided dice to facilitate play.

In Arsenal, you take on the role of a pilot in a combat mech arena. This arena is represented by a small hex map (the gameboard). You move your Mech around on this map to gain an advantage against your opponent, and then play cards from your hand to attack or defend. You will manage your power levels (the resource used to play cards), and use all manner of tactics to win out against your opponent.

To play Arsenal, each player first chooses a Mech, then they each build a deck from the cards available to that Mech (not all Mechs are created equal). Next you and your opponent(s) shuffle your decks, grab 8 energy cards, a handful of 6-sided dice and damage counters, and put a token on the board that matches your Mech. Then you figure out who's going first and start playing!

- Players take turns moving their Mech around the arena, and playing cards from their hand to represent things like lock-ons, deployable objects, or any number of actions and reactions.
- On your turn, you play attack cards from your hand that represent both your weapons and your ammunition, provided your target is within the attack's effective range.
- When an attack is played, all players have a chance to react to it, by playing their own cards that represent things like taking evasive maneuvers or bracing for impact.
- After everyone has had a chance to react, the attack is resolved: the attacker and defender both roll a number of dice determined by the attack card, range, and other variables. Both players then add up the number of 1's and 6's they rolled.
- The defender must meet or beat the attacker's roll in order to dodge the attack. If they don't, it hits, and their Mech takes damage! Continue until only one Mech (or team) is left standing.

All cards have an energy cost. You have 8 energy to spend on every player's turn, but you also have the option to "burn" any or all of your 8 energy. Doing so could pay off big, but "burned" energy comes back at a much slower rate, leaving you open to counter attack.

description:
In the vast wilderness exotic travelers, mystic relics, legandary lands, and fabled heros have been found. Now, with Treasures and Traps: Random Encounters you can wield these forces and bring glory to your realm — but be quick or your opponent may seize these findings of good fortune first!

The Random Encounters expansion contains thirteen powerful cards introducing a new class, two custom dice for use with the cards, a set of rules explaining how to use them, and a large box to hold the T&T base game and all existing expansions.

As with Expanded Realms 1, Treasures and Traps: Expanded Realms 2 consists of 26 cards to be added to the T&T game deck. Each card has a unique effect, examples of which are time control spells to affect the next few rounds, poison traps, the deadly monk dojo, the surprise mimic trap, locations to power your hand and your defenses, the duel event to challenge another player, and the dimensional box to hold your goodies.

Beastie Bash! isn’t just a card game for kids, it’s outside-the-box thinking -- in a box.

Beastie Bash! is a silly character creation game for 1-4 players. It’s 20 minutes of family fun created by children’s book author/illustrator,

Players combine the highest scoring HEAD card with a BODY card to win the cards for that round. The game winner is the player who collects the most Beasties.

Conquest of the Stars is a strategy card game for 2-4 players where the goal is simple: you are out to capture more planets than your opponents. 
Of course your opponents are trying to do the same thing, so it's not going to be easy ...

Each turn you draw cards to add to your fleet, then explore to find a planet. You will decide how much of your fleet you want to commit to take this new planet, but the other players may decide they think it is worth battling over. They will then commit some part of their fleet, and the battle is on. Players then continue to commit ships and upgrades to better their rivals until one player emerges victorious.

Sounds simple enough. And it is simple; but simple doesn't always mean easy. Victory can be snatched away by a well timed counter attack.

Watch your strategy on when and how much of your fleet to commit to a battle. You may end up over committed too soon in the game and be unable to effectively fight the final battle when it comes.

description:
Düdle - a game of illustrating ridiculous randomly generated phrases.

The game has four decks of cards separated by parts of speech. Each player draws 5 cards: 1 adjective, 2 nouns, 1 verb, and 1 adverb. These 5 words make the phrase the player will illustrate. All the players keep their words secret while they draw their own phrases. When everyone finishes, the group works together trying to guess the words that each artist had based on their drawings. Each correct word guessed earns the artist two points, while each close, but not exact word earns the artist 1 point. The player with the most points wins!

Since it’s a game about creativity, adapting the game and playing by house rules are encouraged. For instance, younger players may want to only draw 4 cards (skipping the adverb) to adjust the game to fit their level.

While groups of 2-8 players work best, the game can be played alone as a drawing exercise or even in a group of up to 54 players each with their own 5-card phrase!

Game description from the publisher:

"Welcome to Heinlein."

First Contact is the third Data Pack in the "Lunar Cycle" for Android: Netrunner. After heading Upstalk and passing through The Spaces Between, it's time to land on the moon. First Contact shakes up the contests between Corp and Runner by introducing three new region upgrades, mythic ice, new traps, resources, and a new Anarch identity. The Data Pack's sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) also depict a new meat space where gravity is low, people all wear spacesuits, there's only one fully established city, and everything is enclosed. Confined. Watched.

From the remotest edges of Port Anson Grid to the seedy underbelly of Duggar's, First Contact immerses players in a strange and alien setting in which there are very few places the game's Corps don't control completely, everything you thought you knew is just a shade different, and it's all that much more important to find someone to guide you through your ongoing cyberstruggles...

Whether you're on the hunt for new gamblers or thugs, you can be sure that Power & Influence will make your Nothing Personal experience that little bit more backstabby...

Have At Thee is a card game of multi opponent sword fighting for 2 to 6 players aged 9+. It is a fast paced game which lasts between 20 and 40 minutes.

Players attack their opponents who then defend against the attacks, trying to avoid taking the most damage.

The game was conceived of and written by Richard Williams and Dave Skidmore, with further help, feedback and play testing from Ed Boreham and Calne Gamers Group.

From the Publisher:
