Wing It - The overland, underhand birdwatching challenge.

Race around the British countryside to be the first to spot unusual birds and at the same time hamper other players to prevent them from getting there first.

A Chough has just landed at the RSPB reserve on Ramsey Island and as you jump in your boat to be the first to get there you discover one of your opponents has hidden your paddles. However, don’t worry, they can’t get there either, because you’ve just punctured the tyres on their bike.

Wing It is a great fun family board game, requiring a little cunning, some guile and the employment of underhand tactics in order to win. Utilising official RSPB illustrations, calling on the experience of RSPB wardens and using actual RSPB reserves, Wing It  combines an authentic birdwatching game with family entertainment.

You might ruffle a few feathers but remember the early bird wins.

The players have to travel from Genua to Elba or Triest (first edition). The movement of the meeples is determined by a die. In some cities, the players gain or lose game coins. The first player who arrives on Elba and the player with the most game coins at the end of the game share the first prize.

There are 4 starting points in this game: London, Lublin, Serajewo and Tarragona. From there the players have to travel to Rome. The city cards are dealt to the players and determine, which cities the player has to visit on his way to Rome. The players get a number of game coins, which they can lose on some spaces on the game board.

The first player to reach Rome gets the first prize. The second prize gets to the player with the most game coins.

The game was most likely published in the 1950s.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Nîn-in-Eilph
description:
At first they made fair progress, but as they went on, their passage became slower and more dangerous. The marshes were bewildering and treacherous, and there was no permanent trail even for Rangers to find through their shifting quagmires.
    –J.R.R. Tolkien, The Fellowship of the Ring

The Nîn-in-Eilph is the fourth Adventure Pack in "The Ring-maker" cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game. Its sixty cards introduce a new scenario in which Middle-earth's heroes must survive the challenges of a deadly bog as they race to a site rumored to shelter ancient secrets. You'll also find a new hero, and three copies of each new player card, several of which permit new interactions with the first player token, representing the leadership and assistance that other companions can offer.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Melanżeria (Melange) is a card game by Let's Play, intended for parties and social entertainment (usually involving 3 to 8 players), aimed at selecting the real Party Star from among you. Each player receives points at the beginning of the game (their number depends on the number of players), which he has to keep by completing orders. The orders are given by... the other players, who each time choose a person supposed to complete a task. The player who does not succeed, loses a point.

The tasks (presented on 64 cards) include pretending to be someone else, making faces, speaking in funny voices, telling stories, feeding other players with snacks, proposing toasts, drinking, etc.

The PromoPack for Praetor acts as a mini expansion for the base game and contains 6 promo City Tiles, 2 promo Wall Tiles, a double sided player board and a few extra Gold tokens.

The promo City Tiles blend in perfectly in the universe of Praetor, offer an enhancement to the game play and lots of fun. 

     the Senate allows a player to take over the first player position for the next turn thus being in the pole position to build or activate the most attractive City Tile.
     the Temple of Mars allows players to score Favor Points for each retired worker. Combine its ability with one of the expert sides of the player boards and you have a potentially lethal combo.
     the Fort provides a mix of Favor Points, Gold and the infamous Morale - probably the most expensive and hard to obtain "resource" in the game.


Last but not least, the promo player board offers two brand new expert sides, giving players opportunities to discover new strategies in the Praetor universe.

Any promo component can be added to the base game separately or together with the rest.

The Praetor Promo Pack will be available in the Geek Store.

Carcassonne: Die Klöster, a mini-expansion first published for Easter 2014, lets you replace the monastery tiles in the Carcassonne base game with six new tiles, each with an illustration based on a particular German monastery: Kloster Andechs, Eberbach, Lorch, Kloster Maria Laach, Kloster Marienthal, and Maulbronn.

When you place a tile from Die Klöster, you can place a monk on the tile as usual (with the figure being scored and removed only when the monastery tile is surrounded by eight other tiles) or you can lay the figure on its side to indicate its status as abbot; abbots remain in place until the end of the game, then they score one point for the monastery itself and for each tile connected to the monastery in rows and columns leading away from it.

There are multiple versions of this expansion, including German, Dutch, and Japanese monasteries. The rules do not differ, just the artwork.

Carcassonne: Die Klöster is part of the Carcassonne series.

Objective: place most of your markers upon the board; improve the multiplication up to 100.
 - acting by turns –

Each player gets his/her coloured markers/chips and rolls the 4 dice (with numbers up to 10):

Form a square with the 4 dice and multiply either the horizontal, vertical or diagonal numbers:

Both results may be marked with your chips on the board. Achieving a 9, 25, 49 or 81 you have a second turn, a ‘100’ gives you a third turn. If you cannot use numbers you loose that turn, game ends after all numbers are marked.

Note:
Likely published during 1930's

This is an educational health quiz game for children and adults, developed by Danish doctors, with 400 questions about health (200 questions for kids and 200 questions for adults).

The game is made to challange your knowledge about you health, change your opinions and ways and thereby improve your health.

The game board is divided into 100 spaces in five different colours: Yellow, Red, Black, Blye and Green.
Each colour have two corresponding decks of cards; one for Children and one for Adults. Each card contains a question if different categories - and one of them (Black) is about you and your habbits!

If you answer the question correct you will gain an extra roll. Some questions might leave you skip a turn if your answer is not healthy enough!

The winner is the one who first arraives to the finish space (Snakes head)

Observation game which stimulates the concentration. Find the common element on the displayed cards.﻿ The same hat, same cheeks or same jumper : three ways to combine cats and win the game by getting rid of all your cards.

Go Fetch! is a children's game from Asmodee. Players hold a cup (puppy) and the aim is to capture (fetch) the balls as they pop out of the doggie bowl. The players are in a race to capture the balls before their competition do and 3 challenge cards mix up the requirements for each round.

The goal of the game is to collect as many bits of kibble (colored balls) as you can before the other players snatch them up. To add another level of difficulty, players can also choose to only eat certain colors of kibble or only 1 piece of kibble in each color for those puppies who are on a diet.

Not only is the game fun for children and their parents but the game can also be educational as it promotes colour recognition, counting skills and hand-eye co-ordination.

The game is well constructed also with holes in the bottom of the puppy cups that allow the balls to slip into the cups when they are banged down on top of the balls.

Players first tile together wall cards to create a fortification.  Then players toss rock cards at the opposing walls, scoring any wall segments hit.  A tossed rock card must be from at least 2 feet way, it most be from at least 1 foot in the air, it must flip over at least once in the air, and it must no land with the 'miss' side up.  Players each toss six rocks, and then then player who destroyed the most wall segments wins.

From the Box:
Only one Baron can rule the realm.  Will it be you?  To master the land you must build a mighty fortress and once safe behind your walls, rain rocky death from the skies upon your rivals.  Do you have what it takes to reign supreme?

Clash of Catapults pits 2-6 players against each other in a battle to build mighty fortifications then destroy them with catapult rocks in crazy card flipping action.

Black Slither Games is at it again, and this time we've gone micro. Kick, Punch, Burp, Fart, Slap is a 2-5 player micro card game based on the classic hand gesture game Rock, Paper, Scissors. KPBFS takes those hand gestures and puts them on cards, with that silly Black Slither twist.

It's incredibly fasted paced and anybody can play. Kick, Punch, Burp, Fart, Slap can be played for points, or as a drinking game. Without giving all the details away, here is how it's played. Deal out cards, turn over cards, drink if you lose, don't drink if you win, wash, rinse, repeat. Oh, and keep track of points if you care. Simple and to the point.

This 8-card mini expansion for Bootleggers was originally released in 2013 in connection with the Mayday Games re-release of the game. It is a set of Thugs to be added to the game, and consists of the following cards:

Honorary Mayor - Collect all the graft normally paid to the bank this turn.
Potent Mash - Roll an extra die of production at a single still.  May affect Copper's placement of enforcement.
Payroll Tax - Force a player to pay $1G per influence marker he controls.
County Line - Play on a loaded truck. Crates fall off the truck during a chase. Discard half the loaded crates (rounded up) from the truck.
Army Buddy - Play when an opponent plays an influence card.  Receive the same influence.
You Got the Wrong Guy, See - Redirect the target of a Men of Action card to another player (but not the player of the card).
Friends in High Places - Reroll a single die of your choice, discard after use.  The die need not be your own.
I Gots an Idear - Play on a truck. A trailer is permanently added to the truck which expands its capacity by 2 crates.

Kingdoms in Peril is a multi-strategy card game set in 700BC that can be played with 2 to 6 players. Players create a Kingdom through selecting City Cards that vary in strength and risk.

Players choose a Kingdom and then select City Cards that vary in strength. These cities are placed onto the game table and make up each player's Kingdom. Players collect and purchase various cards to build a playing card hand, which will be used during game play to battle opponents over cities. There are also some special feature cards in the mix, as well as prized Defensive Cards that allow a player to surrender to an opponent and pay tribute rather than lose a valuable city to defeat..

Throughout the game players must balance their army (playing hand), resources (gold), and siege weapons (keys). While strategizing how to use various Battle Strength Cards to fend off attackers and defeat opponents in Battle.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

description:
Promotional game -

Collect as many chocolate bonus points as possible
 - acting by turns -

Collection of different games with activities like Activity and Memory; for each successfully solved challenge you are going to achieve chocolate bonus points.

Players slip into the role of penguins who try to collect as many ice floes as they can while trying not to fall off the iceberg, which is becoming smaller and smaller with every round.

Each movement must be well thought out, because on the slippery ice each movement will also have an effect for the next round. The movement you perform on Round 1 will become your momentum for the next round, and so on.

But that’s not all. You must also imagine the intention of your opponents, because if a penguin collides with another penguin, it will transfer its momentum to the other penguin.

The game ends as the last penguin falls into the water. The player with the highest pile of ice floes wins the game.

The game was nominated for the "Landespreis für Spielkultur 2010" (State Award for Board Game Culture) awarded by Styria (Austria) under its German name "Eisberg".

A word game in which you gain points for finding synonyms to complicated English words. The opponent draws and reads a word for you and you need to find simple alternatives that match the ones on the other side of that card. The person who gains the most points is the winner.

From the publisher: 
"This is a great game for those with extensive vocabularies... Use your knowledge of language semantics to simplify the meaning of ‘big’ words into ‘small’ ones. For example, if the ‘big’ word on the card was countenance, the ‘small’ word could be visage or expression.
So let the battle of the minds commence and it will be clear who is the victor and has the greater understanding of the English Language.
A great little game for airplanes, train or car journeys, or perhaps you could make it some sort of drinking game!"

Now you've done it...you pushed Olivia a little too far! Move Olivia around a self destructing grid of 5x5 cards and have her catch your opponents before they have her catch you! Survive three rounds of play to be the winner!

Players draw cards not from a deck, but from a board that Olivia starts the game in the middle of. Players can then move Olivia around the board by swapping her card with other cards. The board gets progressively smaller as the game goes on, a mechanic you need to use to your advantage to knock out other players or end rounds while you're ahead on points. Drawn cards allow you to move Olivia, discard cards from the board, secretly swap the card in your hand with one on the board, and, eventually, have Olivia catch other players who are not protected by any cards between them and her!

Every round the cards that are available change, so your strategy can switch between heavy offense, strategic card removals, and unofficial alliances with other players to knock out those who are further ahead. If two or more players survive the round, the values of their discarded cards determine the winner.

A family game for 2-4 players about becoming the head chef in the world famous "Burrito! Burrito!" restaurant.

The world famous Tex-Mex Restaurant “Burrito! Burrito!” is looking for a new head chef. As an up-and-coming chef, this is a huge opportunity for you. However, you are not alone in your quest to become world renowned. 
The restaurant owners are running an open competition to find the right chef for the job, and you will have to compete with other chefs, for the position. The task is simple; be the most productive and resourceful chef to complete the last fajita and the job will be yours.

Chefs will compete to be the first to complete 5 finished fajitas. The first chef to fulfill their fifth fajita order is the winner.

boardgame: Piwne Imperium: Polskie browary i puby
description:
Extension to Piwne Imperium board game, named „Polskie browary i puby” (Beer Empire: Polish breweries and pubs). It is a deck of cards that contains 15 recipes from small and medium-sized regional and contract breweries. These breweries are located in Poland, and they create Polish „beer culture”.

In this deck you will also find 10 pub cards, that show real, special places on the Polish beer map where you can enjoy a good beer and spend time with your friends.

This extension also contains 30 new challenges and events cards, 12 of which were created in collaboration with those who supported the Beer Empire project at Wspieram.TO, a Polish crowdfunding platform.

With these cards added to Beer Empire, you can move in the realities of the Polish beer market, were you can meet interesting breweries or take the challenge for pubs, which are really places worth knowing.

Many of the event or challenges cards often refer to the situation we have seen in our home market - sometimes funny, sometimes serious, and sometimes unusual and unprecedented.

Add this expansion to Beer Empire and have fun, discovering trivia and elements of the "Polish Beer Market."
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
T.V. tie-in game for the BBC series Wallace & Gromit: World of Invention. "Start a space race and build a rocket with this cracking family game!"

A simple roll and move with 2.5D rocket models that slot together. The winner is the first player to collect all the pieces of their rocket. No tactical or strategic decisions with more random noise added by "Chance" cards that hinder, help, allow "take that" or defend from it.

Mutology is a brand new strategy card game that transforms players into Mutologists, bioengineers obsessed with creating extraordinary creatures in their laboratories. Players will fire Radiation cards at their Specimens, and depending on what combinations of Radiation they're exposed to, Specimens will turn into one of two unique Mutants. Some Mutants are hyper aggressive, others have defense mechanisms, and some manipulate the way you or your opponent can play the game! You collect Research Points at the start of each turn for every Mutant you have in play, and the first player to 5 points wins.

Players mutate their Specimens by firing one of three kinds of Radiation, (Alpha, Beta, and Gamma Rays) at their Specimen cards. Each Specimen card comes with a unique ingredient list called "mutation requirements." Alone, Radiation cards give perks to the Specimen such as boosting their Power or Stability. Whenever a Specimen has met one of its mutation requirements, they would mutate into the corresponding Mutant card, which would wait outside of play until this moment.

Apart from Specimens and Mutants, player can build Devices like the Electric Coil and the Transmogrifier, or use Procedures like Brainstorm and Devolution. Lab Assistants like the Medic, Technician, and Researcher can lend their talents to you throughout the game as well. There are two zones of play in which cards can be placed: the Field Zone and the Lab Zone. These cards behave differently in each zone, so Mutology games always have a bit of tactical placement strategy involved.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Dictionary is filled with words you may or may not have heard of. They are the words you used to look up on the internet or call each other when you were young. They are vulgar, sexual, positional, provocative, and just crazy. You will ask yourself, "do people actually do these things?" at least once every game.

But watch out, not every card in the Dictionary is Lewd and Obscene. Your creativity may get ahead of yourself. In that sense, you'll need to "get your mind out of the gutter."


Lewd and Obscene is an adult party game.

The game begins by picking a card from the Dictionary. The word is read aloud but the definition remains a mystery to everyone except the picker.

Players try their best to define the word. They write their made-up definition to the word on their "Definition Slip".

When all players are finished defining words, the slips are turned in, mixed up, and read aloud. After all definitions have been read, voting takes place.

When voting, tally points on the definition slip. If you convince a player to vote for your definition, you receive one (1) point. If you vote for the correct definition, you receive two (1) points. Once all the scores are collected, the next player picks a new card and starts all over.

Upon starting, you will all agree to a score to end on and once that has been reached, you will have your winner.

boardgame: Scripts and Scribes: The Dice Game – Mini Expansion
description:
This mini expansion adds two main elements to the base game:

It adds a new "Power Die" that's rolled along with the standard dice. The "Power Die" can be chosen instead of one the other dice and it grants its own unique benefits.

It also adds an auction card and some cubes that are used to track an altered form of the "Auctions for Gold" from the base game.

boardgame: PIIGS: European Economy in the New Era
description:
Are you tired of seeing on TV the latest data of the Risk premium? Bored of hearing experts macroeconomic data retrospectively? Overwhelmed by high unemployment and social decline? In PIIGS players represent that pipe dream called "markets", whose purpose is to enrich themselves at the cost of eliminating the welfare state of the five countries that make up what some economists call PIIGS: Portugal, Italy, Ireland, Greece and Spain.

The game is played in several rounds, split shifts players who make reservations debt countries can buy and sell it later for a profit. On each turn a new debt issue comes into the world economic circuit, which can be purchased in successive turns, and the price will vary as dictated by the rating agencies and the European Central Bank.

The objective of the game is to buy the largest possible number of companies for which we have to get the maximum amount of money on successive shifts, buying and selling bonds of debt to debt reserves of the countries they last or the bankrupt companies. The player at the end of the game has more companies will be the winner.

Christina Regina - the Board Game is a game about politics and intrigues in 17th century Sweden where each player represents a faction with a hidden agenda. The end of their intrigues is the newly appointed queen Christina of Sweden and their means include persuasion, agitation, manipulation and even assassination. This is achieved by carefully steering the queen towards influence tiles bearing their faction colors and away from other influence tiles. At the same time, they must closely monitor the other players' actions to guess their agendas. But beware, if your own agenda is too obvious, you may yourself become the victim of your opponents' intrigues!

Game mechanisms include:

1. Tile laying: Tiles are laid at the start of the game and make every session unique. 
2. Asymmetric scoring: There are no "good" or "bad" tiles but they all score differently for different players. 
3. Hidden agendas: The players do not know how much the other players score until the very end. 
4. Guessing game: Based on how players play, the other players may gain points by guessing their agendas (or lose points by guessing wrong).

Christina Regina is the third game in the Nova Suecia series.

Vasa Regalis: The Maiden Voyage is a game of the building and launching of the Vasa ship. The players take on the roles of shipyard leaders in 17th century Stockholm who have been given a royal order - the building and launching of the greatest battle ship ever seen. But to manage the delivery, they must procure goods, craft them into building material and use them to build the ship. At the same time, they must make a profit by secretly deciding how much to use for the ship and how much to keep for themselves. The players' total contribution determines the fate of the ship. If she is successful, the player who put most aside wins but if she is a disaster, only the player who put most into the ship will be spared the King's wrath. Each player must thus closely monitor the other players' strategies to determine whether to spend or to save. The victory conditions will not be clear until the game is over - but then it is too late to change the strategy.

Game mechanisms include:

1. Role-based: The players choose roles to perform actions, actions that give some players more benefits than others. 
2. Economic balance: The players need to balance between the good types (wood, cloth, iron and sculptures) so that they always have something to work with. 
3. Ambiguous victory conditions: The victory conditions depend on the sum of the players' actions and will not be clear until the game ends. 
4. Guessing game: A player strategy is not visible to the other players and must be deduced from their actions.

Vasa Regalis is the fourth game in the Nova Suecia series.

Critical Extraction adds MERCS from the USCR faction to MERCS: Recon. Included are five USCR MERCS and their corresponding player boards and special rules. In addition, the mission pack contains new missions and a new tile.

Mysterious Creatures was created to engage player's curiosity in the mysteries of nature that exist across the world. The game takes each player on an adventure/race across the globe in search of their creature's evidence cards.  The first player to collect all 3 evidence cards and make it back to their creature's country of origin wins! Along the way players will learn geography, country flags and multiple creatures such as Big Foot.

A roll and move elimination game for children starring The Gunge Gang - 4 monsters called Sticky Icky (Yellow), Scumbag (red), Crud (blue) and Yuck (green).

Each player takes charge of a team of 3 identical monsters. Players turns rolling the dice and moving in the swamp, a circular path on the board. The object is to land on the same space of an opponent's monster, which removes them from the game.

Double Cards allowed players to double their roll.

The last player with monsters left on the board wins.

The game 'Become the prime minister' gets you in the battle of politics! You have to propose innovative and convincing ideas for society problems, so that you gain good evaluation numbers!

Moreover you have to keep good relationships with your rivals... Tactics and politics are closely related.

Every candidate moves using a dice and takes the  corresponding card. He reads the problem, and starts setting up arguments and concluding to a solution for the problem. As soon as he finishes, everybody evaluate his speech with their available card numbers (positive, neutral or even negative). You don't want negative evaluations in your hands at the end of the game...

The player with the greatest sum of evaluations after 3 rounds wins the game.

It's a pleasant and joyful party - trivia game in which one player or team of players asks the other for 10 answers. For example 10 movies of Ppielberg, 10 fruits with one kernel, or 10 tints of blue.

Each answer (of the 10) depending on its difficulty gives the player, or team points (from 1 to 5). The player or team with the most points at the end of the game is the winning one.

Lists is a very pleasant trivia game!

Every player has to place in the right order some answers in the card that is being played.

An example could be chronological order of some events, or American presidents, right order from the longest river to the shortest one, right order from the movie with the most Oscars to the one with the least. The answers are given. You'll have just to place them in the right order.

Depending on your right answers in the row, you move along the board till the finish line!

Thirst for Power from Nexus Games is a strategy and resource management game in which players are governors of regions and compete to be the first to provide for their citizens' energy needs.

The objective is to be the first player to meet the region's energy goal without exceeding its environmental impact limit. In the game, you will find that energy production, water consumption, and climate change are tightly intertwined. We call this the Nexus of Energy, Water, and Climate. This nexus makes it hard to get what you want and requires making trade-offs to win.

As governor, you will accumulate energy sources to provide for the transportation and electricity needs of your citizens. You will have a wide range of energy sources to choose from, both hydrocarbon-based and renewable. But, watch out! Every kind of energy source requires water and has an environmental impact on climate, land, water or living things. You will have limited water resources and a cap on your environmental impact, making it challenging to reach your energy goal. Energy sources like coal and oil will produce a lot of energy, but also use up a lot of water and have a big environmental impact. Renewable sources use less water and have lower environmental impact, but generate much less energy.

ACTION cards (TECHNOLOGY, POLICY, AND CLIMATE IMPACT) bring in real-world developments that change the course of the game and force players to quickly adapt their strategy. Buying technology gives you an advantage, while implementing policies can set your opponent back, and climate impacts wreak havoc on everyone. Strategy is critical along with negotiating trades throughout the game. Getting the right mix of energy sources and technology boosts for your region can be easier with negotiations and trades than taking your chances drawing from the deck.

Zombicide’s Compendium #1 is a 92-page book entirely devoted to your favorite game! Inside, you’ll find updated versions of all the official Campaigns and Missions published for Season 1, as well as brand new Missions, a precious Designer’s Note, and an exclusive Campaign!

Military Geography is a game of strategy similar to the war exercises played out by Admirals and Generals in the defense Department and the United States War College. Player learn about navigations, military strategy, tactical, maneuvering, positioning, networking, and logistics.

A fast paced, "in your face card game" or slow down and teach the kids strategy, math and decision making skills. Skip and hit any other player on the table, lay down long runs like in rummy. Go out with big points. Get caught with the Deck Master and you're the dealer. If you've ever liked UNO, Then you'll love DD Mau!

The deck consists of 80 playing cards, 52 of which are numbered 1 - 13 in four colors.  The remaining 28 cards are “Power Cards”.

Players get rid of their cards by following the color, matching the number of the card on the discard pile or by playing a “POWER CARD”.

The hand is over when any player gets rid of all the cards in his or her hand. That player SUBTRACTS the total points from the card or cards that player went out with from their base score. All other players ADD the total of the card or cards in their hand and add it to their base score. The game is over when any player's score EXCEEDS 505 points. In the event that a player's score is 500, 501, 502, 503, 504 or 505 then that player's score is reduced to 400 and play resumes.

The winner is the player with the lowest score at the end of the game.

Space Racing Game is a tactical racing game in which players take control of powerful space ships, facing them against each other. Players race simulated space ships with the hope of crossing the finish line first.

Each ship has its own unique maneuvers and powers.

At start of the game the players will buy ships and components.

Space Racing Game is a tactical game of miniatures, cards and dice which takes place in two phases: 
1. Planning
2. Moving

Planning phase:     at the same time each Player secretly chooses one maneuver and speed using dial.

After Planning phase, each Player move their ships and use its powers.

In sequential order all Players perform their “Moving phase” (Player initiative).

Moving phase: the player has to use a move template and move their space ship, which was secretly chosen.

Players take penalties if they miss their maneuvers, bump into another ship, are blocked by other ships.
The player with the most game points wins the game.

Space Racing Game has 3 different scenarios (racing types) and each of them has its own set of special rules and victory conditions. 
 However, the fun does not end with a single race! The game rules include the ability to customize your ships and change ships components types to modify your speed, maneuvers, powers and special abilities.
 You should plan your strategy, and complete your mission!

The Blood Contagion Mission Pack adds the Infected to MERCS: Recon

Infected in Recon are experiments that have gone bad. They are considered to be Employees. If they are killed, civilian penalties apply. However, Infected do not try to leave the building like other Employees. They seek out and attack other OpFor and MERCS.

The Infected have a chance to turn other OpFor into Infected. Infected OpFor are replaced with special Infected OpFor cards and are marked with a token. Infected OpFor have altered priorities and do different things. The mini-campaign in Blood Contagion revolves around the MERCS going deep into a lab, finding the cure, not letting the Infected get out-of-hand, and saving as many Infected as possible.

Additionally, the Infected bring with them a completely new way for the MERCS to lose: if ever there are 15 Infected in the structure the MERCS have been defeated.

This Mission Pack is a Myth Crossover. The Infected can also be used as Minions in Myth.

The Infected Minions are tough to kill. They have 3 Vitality. On a successful attack, the Infected roll a 1FD. If the Infected rolls the Darkness symbol, the Hero cannot produce treasure. Additionally, if that Hero while infected becomes adjacent to other Heroes, then the infected Hero rolls 1FD for each Hero adjacent. If the roll matches the defining attribute, then the adjacent Hero becomes infected and can no longer generate treasure either.

Ta Ziemia (This Land) is a family, cooperative board game. It allows players to move back to the year 1979 and participate in the preparations for the first pilgrimage of Polish Pope John Paul II to his homeland plunged in the complicated reality of communism.

The task set for the players in Ta Ziemia is not easy: they must confront the forces of the communist regime, which hinder the organization of the pilgrimage and try to diminish its importance. Players work together to help in preparations for the visit of John Paul II. Acting under pressure of time and the communist regime, players must show cleverness, imagination and the ability to predict opponent's next moves.

Box contains:
- Board
- 44 Renovation cards
- 44 Regime cards
- 10 Central regime cards
- 8 Special city rules cards
- 4 Faction cards
- 16 Citizen pawns
- 1 Time marker
- 15 Influence tiles
- 15 Faith tiles
- 15 Resistance tiles
- 30 Faithful tiles
- 8 Agent tiles
- Instruction

From instruction sheet:

OBJECT OF GAME:
Object of the Divorce Court game is to move as many WEBB-STICKERS as each player can in the WEBB-SITE.


This is a basic roll, move, draw cards game. As you go around the board you land on spaces related to court appearances and collect poker chips (chits).

Each card has a set of three Attributes: Value, Power and Clan. Value Attribute is the general value of the card. Power Attribute is a specific break-down of a character's/object's strength. It is expressed on a 3×3 grid and shows six different categories: Body, Mind, Spirit, Land, Water, and Air. The Clan Attribute shows the cards suit. There are six different Clans and each Clan has its own unique symbol.

Swarm! Anti-Body Assault is a competitive-cooperative science fiction game for two to four players. A breach between dimensions has opened at the edge of the galaxy, and the Anti-Bodies, a race of horrible interdimensional space monsters, are spilling out and threatening to consume everything in their path if left unchecked. That's where you come in! You and a couple friends can take control of one of fifteen giant robots from all across the Milky Way, equip an arsenal of futuristic weaponry, and mow down the horde. And although everyone is working towards the same goal, killing some enemies will bring you more recognition from others when it's time for the Senator to hand out the medals...

Gameplay consists of rounds of play, called waves, in which a random number of enemy cards are dealt out of a deck to each player. Then, players take turns making attack rolls using up to three different colored dice, which correspond to enemy types (for example, red dice can only deal damage to red enemies, etc.). After all attacks have been made by players, any surviving enemies counterattack, then remain in play during the next wave.

The primary goal is to work with your teammates to deplete the 100 card enemy deck entirely, but in addition to that each enemy a player kills is worth anywhere from one to six points. So, while players are competing against each other for points, they are also required to work together to prevent a total party loss.

Players can move anywhere they choose on the board but the player in last place always moves next, so it is important not to run too far ahead and give other players an advantage as they catch up to you.

Paranormal Investigation pits your powers of deduction against those of your opponents.  Using your initial glances at the face down mystery cards, and your opponents actions, try to deduce which cards meet your objective as an investigator.

At the beginning of the game, each player gets an Investigator card as well as an Affiliation card, giving a unique combination of abilities, making each game different.

The Believer is looking for evidence to support that paranormal phenomenon exists and cannot be scientifically explained. Reveal two "Phenomenon" cards to win the game!

The Skeptic is looking for logical explanations to explain the event and is eager to prove the world is a scientifically explicable place.  Reveal two "Logical Explanation" cards to win the game!

The Fraud does not care one way or the other, he is simply along for publicity, money, or thrill.  Since he doesn't care, he truly just enjoys stirring the pot to see others fight to defend their position.  Run out of cards in your hand to win the game!

The Medium believes that the mystery is actually a message from beyond, and that neither the phenomenon or science matters as much as what the messenger is saying. Reveal a single "Message" card to win the game!

Tixel is a combinatorial game for two players. The name comes from the playing pieces, which are basically pixels (squares) with a curved indentation on one of their edges. Tixels are "directional", meaning that their orientation is relevant.

On a turn, a player can either place a new tixel or move one of her own tixels already on the board. A tixel can be "active" or "inactive"; only active tixels, which are at a 45º angle, may be moved. Making tixels inactive is done by moving next to or passing an active tixel.

Chaining moves is important as this temporarily unlocks three additional move types (aside from placing or moving a piece). If a tixel that is moved is made inactive at its end position — i.e., it's sacrificed — a player can take an extra move if she so chooses; aside from placing or moving a tixel, she can now remove a tixel from the board, activate an inactive tixel, or pivot an active tixel to reorient its hollow side.

Tixel: Tix Expansion Pieces consists of four extra pieces for each player. These expansion pieces are different from regular pieces in that they are completely square instead of being hollow on one side. For each expansion piece used, remove one tixel from the game. The expansion pieces can be used to add variety to games, but they can also be used to handicap a stronger player. Simply give the stronger player more expansion pieces compared to the weaker player. With two expansion sets, the original Tix game can also be played on the Tixel board.

boardgame: Dirty Minds: The Ultimate Board Game for Adults
description:
The Ultimate Edition of Dirty Minds contains four new categories of relatively innocent material that only YOUR mind can make naughty.

Dirty Clues is a category featuring clean answers to naughty clues. 
Dirty Deeds are naughty stories about innocent acts. 
Dirty Lies means the truth is just too naughty. 
Dirty Secrets is a category in which you decide to tell, or not tell.

The seemingly filthy clues point toward totally clean answers.

Added to them is Dirty Deeds, which sound like naughty little stories, but they're about completely innocent things. In other words, if you have a dirty mind, you'll lose.

Dirty Lies contains simple facts of interest to adults... along with two ways to lie about them; Dirty Secrets are tasty little tidbits from your own very personal experiences. You alone must choose to either tell the truth or lie about them. With both Secrets & Lies, other players score by correctly guessing your intentions.

Each game comes with 63 Dirty Clue/Dirty Deeds cards, 63 Dirty Secrets/Dirty Lies cards, 24 dirty bets cards, 1 dirty board game, 8 dirty pawns, 2 dice and instructions.

description:
40 cards, each containing one or two letters, are placed face down in the center of the table in eight stacks (four stacks in top row and four stacks in bottom row). Player ONE begins play by turning over top card of each stack, and from the 8 face up cards makes ONE word of 2-or-more letters and places it in his play area. Words are played fanned together like a Rummy hand to save space.

Player TWO (and every player thereafter) flips over any face down card atop stacks the prior player used to make their word, thereby replenishing the formation to 8 face up cards. Player TWO likewise makes ONE 2-or-more letter word and places in in his play area. However, in addition, he may steal words from other players' areas by adding one or more of the remaining letters in formation to those words (inserting at any point of word or optionally rearranging the letters in those words). Multiple play-ons are allowed each turn, and players can "play on" their own words also. Play continues with the next player. When any stack is depleted of cards, face up cards from other stacks with multiple cards are slid over to occupy open position and underneath card is flipped over to be face up.

The winner is the player with the most letters in his play area when all cards in the 8 stack formation are depleted.

80 cards total in deck. Deck is re-shuffled before each game for a fresh set of 40 cards in the formation for each round.

Play best 2-out-of-3 or 3-out-of-5 rounds (as decided by players beforehand) to determine winner of match. Each round takes about 10 minutes.

With two players, each player plays with seven pieces on a 4X4 grid. (Eight pieces and a larger grid are used with more players.) On his turn a player either places one of his pieces onto an open square of the grid or captures an opposing piece by jumping it. A player is forced to jump a piece if his opponent placed the piece next to the player's piece. Play ends either when one player achieves four pieces in a row or when no player has enough pieces left to form four in a row. Players score four points for completing four in a row and one point for each captured piece. Play multiple rounds until one player reaches twenty one points

boardgame: Doctor Wars: The Hospital Strategy Game
description:
The board consists of 12 hospital rooms bordered by hallways. Into each room is placed a randomly chosen patient card, which in turn is covered with four randomly chosen cards that are turned face-up. Each of those four cards represents an ailment. Each patient's ailments must be cured in the order that the cards are stacked, top to bottom.

Each player controls a nurse, resident, intern, and attending physician. These pieces move from room to room curing the ailments and scoring points for the cures. The more difficult cures are worth more points. Interns can move the greatest distance in a turn, doctors the shortest, but doctors and residents outrank interns. Credit for a cure goes to the highest ranking doctor in a room or, in case of a tie, to the player with the most doctors in the room. Nurses can block access to rooms. The games ends when all patients are cured of all ailments. The player with the most points wins.

72 tiles, each containing three colored squares: blue, red, and yellow. The squares are arranged differently on each tile - no two tiles are the same. Each player has a hand of three tiles. You take turns placing a tile (and then drawing another), matching the colored squares. You score points when you create a three- or four-corner match, and when you match two or three different colors on the same play. The game ends when all tiles are played. The highest score wins.

boardgame: Backstab: A Political and Strategic Live-action Card Game
description:
Backstab, A hybrid between a card game and a live action role-playing game, set in a medieval royal court torn apart by conspiracies. Backstab is a unique type of game meant to be played not seated around a table, but in a room in your house, or even a public place like a bar for instance.

Backstab is a game of politics without mercy, made of secret plots and double dealings. You are assuming within it the role of a Character who will have to interact and negotiate with other influential actors of this universe with the purpose of furthering your interests and ultimately, winning enough Victory Points to declare yourself victorious

Each player will choose a character to play, read his victory objectives and unique abilities. He will then start speaking in a corner of the room with other players, in order to trade cards or to  propose helping with one of their objectives in return for a card, an information or the use of their abilities for their own benefits.

Each character has unique abilities, and you can use resources cards that allows you to create situations in your favor. You can, for example, force another player to answer a single question honestly, spy on the cards and objectives of a rival, or even send an opponent to prison under false motives.

The game is played with 5 to 12 players and each game lasts from 20 to 60 minutes.

Each game will be quite unique, even if each character keeps the same victory objectives, the opportunity cards will give you other ways to achieve victory. This can lead to quite surprising alliances and plots.

description:
Man vs. Apocalypse has 2 decks, the Scrounging Deck, and the Apocalypse Deck. The scrounging deck contains all the people and items you will need to survive.

The Apocalypse deck contains the disasters and the Apocalypses themselves, at the end of the every round one of these cards will be reveled and have to be dealt with.

At the beginning of the Man vs. Apocalypse you and your friends will draw up to a full hand(5) of cards from the Scrounging Deck. You can play as many cards as you want from your hand on your turn, but be warned, if you run out of cards in your hand you are killed and have lost the game.

As you play these cards, each survivor have special abilities that can help you, some will provide you some defense vs. a specific disaster, other may bring a useful skill, some are only useful for a time, before they must be sacrificed for the greater good(You).

On their turn a player will draw 2 cards,(no more then 5 in hand) and be place in play as many as he wants.  Once all players have gone you draw from the Apocalypse Deck, these cards will force you to discard your survivors in play, discard cards from your hand or create other chaos.

Once all players have dealt with the Disaster that was drawn, the play starts with the first player again.

The goal is to survive to the end of the Apocalypse deck, only then can you begin to rebuild.

Players on three (or more) teams compete to rant about their "peeves".  Those ranting must avoid using certain words, as indicated on each peeve card, while the other teams try to be the first to determine the nature of the peeve.  Points are earned for successful ranting, as well as for being the first team to figure out what the rant is about.
NOTE:  This game was tested as a prototype and was never manufactured or released.

The players begin with sets of identical blocks and place their respective blocks upright on opposite sides of a battlefield that consists either of hexes or of squares. Players take turns downing an opponent's piece (placing a piece in an unobstructed line of sight to the opponent's piece), taking out an opponent's piece (moving onto a space occupied by an opponent's downed piece and then removing that piece from the board), or restoring a downed piece (placing that piece upright in one's home area). A player wins when all of his opponent's pieces on the board are down.

Sites are shuffled with the Safe-Houses to a “sites deck”, before setting up the game. Each player (beginning with the first player) draws one site from the sites deck and chooses which direction to place it in towards the player to their left. That site marks the shared Faction between the player who placed it and the player to their left.

Note that a site provides a special ability to the player towards whom they are facing (i.e. their text is facing). These abilities apply as long as the text is facing that player. If the site is turned (e.g. by the Engineer), then the ability is then granted to the opposite player of that Faction.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Val Jean Expansion Pack
description:
The Val Jean was a small raider-type craft operated by the Maquis during the early 2370s. The impulse engines of Val Jean were originally built in 2332.

In 2371, while under the command of Chakotay, the Val Jean was pursued by the Cardassian warship Vetar, Gul Evek's flagship. Chakotay ordered the ship into the Badlands to try to escape the Cardassians.

After eluding pursuit, the Val Jean was scanned by a coherent tetryon beam and transported into the Delta Quadrant by the Caretaker. Once there, the crew was abducted and subjected to medical tests to determine their biological compatibility with the Caretaker. One of the crew, chief engineer B'Elanna Torres, was removed from the others and sent to the surface of the Ocampa homeworld.

In order to recover his missing crew member, Chakotay reluctantly joined forces with Starfleet captain Kathryn Janeway of the USS Voyager, who had been sent to capture him but had instead been pulled into the Delta Quadrant as well. The cooperation proved successful, and both Torres and Voyager crew member Harry Kim were rescued.

This expansion adds a new ship and a new set of cards to Star Trek: Attack Wing.

description:
Two players face off in this unconventional turn-based, patent pending board game developed by late Hungarian inventor József Vadász. Exercising one’s spatial reasoning skills, this innovative game rewards a fine balance between carefully planned strategy and tactics.

The game is played on a hexagonal board with several rows of holes. Each player has two pieces. One piece contains five holes and is long enough to cover five holes in the board; the other piece contains three holes and covers three holes in the board.  Players take turns inserting a peg through a hole in one piece/board and then pivoting the piece into a new position. A player loses when one of his pieces is unable to make a move at the beginning of his turn.

Exciting mix of strategy and luck, a 2-4 person, fast paced card game that has unique characters with powerful abilities.

In Metacell: Genesis you take the role of one of humanities last defenders who are training to battle a scientific abomination that was created by our hunger for answers on the nature of reality. Battle your friends and learn the skills you'll need to survive!

Quick match making game play and activated player powers, with sudden game endings and complex scoring system creates a dynamic game that can either be a drawn out head to head or quick sudden duel. The rules include a 2p vs. 2p teams mode, as well as modes for 2, 3, or 4 players to play independently. Awesome variability in play makes it a game with plenty of replay value.

Get ready to exercise your fingers and your mind. Tile Pile is a Memory / Dexterity game in which players must locate lettered tiles in order to complete words and score points. Simple rules and quick gameplay make this game a fun learning tool for children and excellent party game for adults.

There are 3 Matches in the game. Each Match has 3 Rounds. Players are given cards of varying difficulty. In each Round, players search for letters to complete words of the same difficulty. First to finish hits the bell and the Round ends. Points are scored and tallied and the game continues. After the end of the third match, the game ends and final scores are totaled.

This official Mayfair promo expansion is included on a tile sheet titled: Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #8

The two tiles allow a player to remove and replace a previously
played tile - except a feature tile with a cube on it already.

This official Mayfair promo expansion was not released separately, but is part of the Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #8.

Includes two tiles

Tile #1: The Station Master's Whistle:
After the first train leaves the Station and has been scored give the Whistle tile to the player with the lowest score. If there is a tie for lowest score then wait until the next train has left the Station - basically as soon as there is a clear lowest score that player is given the tile. They may use it at any time in the game to send any train out of the station early (as in immediately the whistle blows). However, if the tile hasn't been used and another player becomes the lowest scorer then they take the tile.
The tile may only be used once during the entire game (that is ONCE not once per player) - put it back in the box when it has
been used.

Tile #2: The Guard's Flag:
This tile may also only be used once per game. It begins on the table next to the train stack. On their turn any player may decide to use the Flag instead of taking their normal turn (ie they do not get to play a carriage or place a passenger chit). The Flag is placed above a train which shows that the train has a fault and cannot be moved. Remove all the passenger chits and return them to their owners, then discard the carriages and the train engine - no one scores this train. After use place the Flag back in the box

This official Mayfair promo expansion is included on a tile sheet titled: Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #8

The Capitol tile can be used to expand the scoring of tiles that adjoin it. Clever placing and timing is the main key to using it.

Publisher's description:

In Animalacious everyone is an animal. The object of the game is to guess which animal you are. If you woke up one day as a different animal and there were no mirrors how would you figure out which animal you are? Well, you would use cunning and craftiness and you would observe those around you, and of course you’d use your animal instincts.

More specifically: Imagine Celebrity Head where you are an animal rather than a celebrity. Add a deck of cards with pre-specified questions (descriptions), in response to which all players vote for which animal best fits that description. Points are scored for voting for the most popular choice! Players are entitled to guess the identity of their animal after scoring 10 points.

The components include cards (identities and questions), headbands, name badges, dry-erase markers and voting wheels.

The game is played with a 5-suited deck that includes a joker, two special cards, and cards numbered 1 through 10 in each suit. It is a trick-taking game in the vein of Wizard or Oh Hell. Players bid simultaneously on the number of tricks they will take each hand. They receive a ten point bonus for being correct, zero for overbidding, and points equal to the number of tricks taken for underbidding. A player receives an additional bonus if he is dealt a special card, bids the amount shown on the card, and then makes the bid. The game ends after ten rounds. High score wins.

boardgame: Impact: Velká Morava – 1500 let křesťanství ve středu Evropy
description:
Expansion set of archaeological card game Impact (2014). Dedicated to the archaeological exposition called "Great Moravia - 1500 Years of Christianity in Central Europe".

The expansion adds a new set of playing cards (18 new cards) including a new card type called written sources.

Thirty two tiles, each depicting a fish with five attributes ---- fin, eye, mouth, pattern, and color ---- are spread in a grid on the table. Each attribute comes in one of two states; eg, the mouth is either open or closed. A card is drawn that depicts two fish. Players try to be the first to draw and display the tiles that transform one fish on the card into the other by changing the state of one attribute at a time.

boardgame: Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Grim Bastards promo card
description:
The 'Grim Bastards' Clubhouse Card is a promotional bonus included in most first print copies of Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem.

It only includes the Grim Bastard Clubhouse Card (nothing else) and can be used as a substitute for one of the other Clubhouse Cards during play.

This Clubhouse Card is part of a full expansion in the Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Grim Bastards Club Expansion.

description:
This is a Print&Play solo game: you are trapped in a maze filled with monsters, with only one laser-gun and two random objects, the goal in the first level is to find the three parts of the sword, and exit.

You can download all you need to play in the file section.

boardgame: Mr. Card Game: Mr. Store Cards
description: These character cards were available via the Kickstarter campaign to backers who backed in the first ten-dozen hours. Each player is randomly dealt one of these items before the game begins and each provides a special power for the player. There are twelve unique Mr. Store cards in the package.

These character cards were available via the Kickstarter campaign, and currently when direct-ordering, and add some variable player powers to the game. There are seven unique identity cards in the package.

Players represent a couple in bed fighting for control of the covers. Players each have a deck of 8 cards and a hand of 3. Players will reveal 1 card simultaneously, high card pulls the covers to their side of the bed. The winner is the player with the most covers in the morning.

From the Rules:

"Battle for Fallujah: New Dawn is a simple, single player game simulating the second battle for Fallujah, Iraq. The game begins on day D+1, November 8, 2004. While fighting in Fallujah continued until the end of December, this game portrays the first seven days of battle in which Coalition forces took the most territory and faced the fiercest resistance. This simulation is intended to represent a modern, urban counterinsurgency operation.

As a player, your goal is to clear Fallujah of insurgents while searching the city, block-by-block. Time is of the essence. Meanwhile, you must maintain the political will and support of both the American and Iraqi governments. You will be combating insurgents whose goal is to inflict as many casualties as possible and force American 'infidels' from their city, and eventually, country. The conflict is contained within the city of Fallujah."

Additional German army counters.

Basic game is needed to play with this expansion.

boardgame: Stop: Das Verkehrsspiel für Jung und Alt
description: The players start at the lower right corner of the game map and try to be the first at the train station at the upper right corner. They beginn as pedestrians, but can take the tram or a car along the way, if they pay a certain amount of game coins. Whenever a player gets on a numbered space, he has to consult the game rules and follow the instructions. In the center of the game board, there is a crossroad with a traffic cop. The cop is rotated every turn to block one road or the other.

description:
Fardh al-Qanoon is a simulation of the 2007 counterinsurgency operation in Baghdad, also referred to as the ‘Baghdad Security Plan.’ The simulation takes place from February 2007 to November 2007 in month-long turns as the US-led Coalition and Mahdi Army vie for control of the Iraqi capital.

Players & Objectives 
One player represents the Coalition, and another the Shia Mahdi Army. The Sunnis of Baghdad are represented in a non-player faction that affects the game.

The Coalition’s goal is to secure control of Baghdad’s districts and Sunni neighborhoods by November 2007 while minimizing casualties and violence. The Mahdi Army’s goal is to maintain a strong presence in Baghdad and prevent the Coalition from securing the city.

description:
ALL ABOARD!

Iron Horses throws you back into the 19th century when Railroads dominated commerce. Climb aboard any one of Lydon Rail Company’s four railways and race to be the first train to successfully deliver $1,000,000 in resources! Be wary of what lies ahead, as setbacks are bound to happen! Tariffs will slow our delivery, maintenance will stop you in your tracks and hijackers are eager to steal your payload! Fix what you can, deliver the goods and attain victory!

Gameplay Overview:

Each player chooses one of four unique railways each with a unique advantage. During a turn, a player has up to 4 available actions, Draw, Transport, Remedy, and Sabotage, each of which will either bring you closer to victory or slow down your opponents. Your main goal is to accumulate $1,000,000 in resources before another player beats you to it. Denominations range from $25,000 to $250,000, so keep a look out for highly valued resources. But it isn't as simple as drawing and transporting resources...Setbacks can bring you to a halt. Remedy them quick to keep moving! Game play is quick, with each player allowed up to 3 actions per turn! You may draw, transport and sabotage in a turn, Draw twice and then remedy, or if you are feeling notorious, Sabotage three times! A 2-player game usually lasts about 10-15 minutes. Add a third and expect a bit longer.

The players start on the dots of their color inside the pink ring and have to get to the numbered spaces of their color alongside the pink ring. The direction of movement is indicated by arrows. If a meeple is on the pink ring and gets on an arrow, which points to a yellow, blue, green or red colored ring, the meeples have to take a detour on this ring, before they can return to the pink ring. This way, they may overshoot their destination and have to travel an additional round on the pink ring.

Sentinel Tactics: Uprising includes six new characters, environment elements for both environments from Sentinel Tactics: The Flame of Freedom, and a scenario book with scenarios that revolve around the new characters, bringing them into the ongoing storyline of Sentinel Comics! The scenarios involve comic-book style stories, map set-ups, and scenario specific tokens, powers, and even side characters!
This is a poster map and scenario for Sentinel Tactics based on the Mobile Defense Platform environment from Sentinels of the Multiverse.
This is a poster map and scenario for Sentinel Tactics based on the Freedom Tower environment from Sentinels of the Multiverse.
This is a poster map and scenario for Sentinel Tactics based on the Wagner Mars Base environment from Sentinels of the Multiverse.

boardgame: Sentinel Tactics: Citadel of the Sun
description: This is a poster map and scenario for Sentinel Tactics based on the Citadel of the Sun environment from Sentinels of the Multiverse.

Every player draws 15 flip-flop shaped pieces, each with a letter on one side and a different letter on the other. Players race to create chains of words in which the final letter(s) of one word is/are the first letter(s) of the next. Play continues until one player completes a word chain using all 15 pieces. He gets 5 points. The player with the longest word gets 10 points, and all players receive one point per word created, including nested words.

boardgame: Sentinel Tactics: Promo Power Card Pack
description:
This pack includes one additional power card for each playable character from Sentinel Tactics: The Flame of Freedom and Sentinel Tactics: Uprising.

Each character has a deck of 6 Power Cards that they select from on their turns. These extra Power Cards give each hero and villain additional capabilities.

Le Val is a game of memory and strategy. It's form is one book of 130 pages entitled "Le Testament de Sinloss" ("Sinloss' Will") which describes the universe and explains the rules.

It is played with a classic 54 card deck (The standard 52-card deck + 1 black Joker and 1 red joker).

Each player is a Star and will use his card to invoke entities and attack the Majesty of his rival (One of the inspirations of the game is "Magic The Gathering").

Eighteen cards bearing parts of advertisements from the 1950s. The eighteen pictures should be displayed about the room and the players write down the full name of the advertiser or product that each picture represents. The player who identifies the most with the greatest precision wins.

This ancient game was played in the 18th century by noble families in the city of Bern in Switzerland. Although it all but disappeared in the 20th century, it has survived thanks to an enthusiast who was one of the last remaining connoisseurs.

Patricius is some kind of "bingo meets freecell".

In this abstract game, players are trying to form four stacks of numbers starting with 1 and going up to 16. However, the numbered tiles are drawn randomly and players will have to store the numbers in five columns until they are able to place them on a stack.

All players play with the same draw, and when all the tiles have been drawn, whoever has made the highest stacks is the winner.

Memory-style game to build North-German swearwords out of pairs. Just for the fun of learning or revisiting North-German dialect words.

The game plays like memory (unlike memory you don't keep matching pairs - put them back on the table, again). One word is the first half of the full word, one the second (1:1 match). To avoid unclarities, the matching parts have the same color - wrong combinations result in funny new creations, adding to the fun.The cards contain also an explanation what the resulting words actually mean (for those that did not know the word before).

It's mainly a fun party game leading to discussions about local dialects, but the one finding most (new) matching pairs is the winner.

It's mainly a fun party game leading to discussions about local dialects, but the one finding most (new) matching pairs is the winner.

In Pixelmon, also known as "Prehistoric Pixelmon - The Deckbuilding Game", 2 to 5 players tame, train and battle with pixellated prehistoric monsters in order to win Prizes.

The goal of the game is to win the most prizes after 8 game turns, whereby the first 5 turns are for taming and training your Pixelmon only, whereas in turns 6 to 8 and possibly beyond, players battle each other with their fighting teams of three Pixelmon each. The "last man standing" in a battle will be awarded a prize card and the player with the most prize cards after three battles were fought is declared victor (in case of ties, additional turns will be held until one player emerges victorious).

This game is a deckbuilding game on the lines of Dominion, Thunderstone, Nightfall and others as each player will start out with one Starting Pixelmon and a Starting Deck associated with it. Over the course of the game players will build up and optimize their decks by "learning" (buying from a supply) new attacks which they can use in battle phase to overcome the other player's Pixelmon teams.

The game will be illustrated in glorious retro-style pixel artwork and graphics by the game's designer himself.

Times are tough for ninjas. Smoke bombs are waaaaay more than you'd think, and jobs are scarce - so both you and this other ninja have to take the almost impossible task of breaking into Tomiko, the Dragon Queen's cave and stealing back the Kyorinrin scroll.

Into the Dragon's Cave is a custom dice game for 2 players, who'll attempt to make sets and earn 60 silver before their opponent. They'll work their way into the cave as far as they think is best and judiciously use smoke bombs to get out of tight spots.

Players will roll custom dice (or a set of standard d6s with conversion table) trying to create sets - and choosing to push their luck if they think they can earn more.
